# Thread: 15 year old who could use some advice!

### Matty_M — 2015-02-05T00:50:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/post-154756>

I was told in the meet and greet section I might be better starting a thread here:

Thanks for responding to my tweet Dr. Kruse, I've been on the forum hundreds of times but never realized I could post without a paying membership

So I'm 15 years old (sophomore in high school), 155 lbs., 5' 7" and have been interested in (more like consumed by) paleo/primal for a little over a year, ever since my health was absolutely falling apart- I had chronic migraines, never ending gas, horrible sleep, no energy, sever light & sound sensitivity, horrible allergies, ALWAYS stuffy nose, etc. etc. (I was a processed starch and sugar-based vegetarian.....thinking vegetarian was good for me and not considering the rest of my diet's role in my health at all.....). Ever since finding the Whole30 concept December 2013 (which was waaaaaaay overdue given how much I suffered and how much I would've done for any solution), I was mostly paleo/primal but with short time periods of over-restricted dieting (because my symtpoms would not go away as they should've, although I got 75% improvement with just primal)- that I was not physically or mentally prepared for, which resulted in binging on crap at certain times, which I often felt I could not control ..... Anyways, when this school year started I really got more and more serious and decided to see a functional medicine practitioner from primaldocs. I got a stool test and we found a parasite- blastocystis hominis, and i went on a 10-day antibiotic and 10-day anti-something else, which just ended today.
ALSO, I got basic bloodwork and it showed a low white blood cell count of 4.1 thousand/uL (out of the reference range). Doc says the next step will be a saliva test.

A few more details- I have eaten basically an epi-paleo diet base for the last few months, however I have occasionally inserted starches (against my instinct thinking they would make me feel better, and they did), eaten quite a bit of dairy products (mostly raw, grass-fed even though I don't think I tolerate them currently), over eaten some sugar or other crap _very_ occasionally (like once a month I'll have ice cream at a friends even though I know I don't want it while working towards optimal- it usually coincides with further restricting my diet to try to feel better), and made other small mods. per thinking they might help.

\-I knew that I couldn't tolerate fodmaps (lots of gas if eaten), and the parasite discovery explained it (I tolerate them now after the antibiotic and other pills. >95% of the time I avoid grains, legumes, sugar, vegetable oils, etc., etc., and I have more recently (2-3 weeks) cut all dairy (besides ghee), and nuts/seeds (except coconut and macadamias), and any alcohol (I use it in cooking- I never drank at parties because I know I'll get a stuffy nose, gas, and feel like shit), all of those things because I have a persisting stuffy nose and need it out of my life ASAP, and I'll use this band-aid approach for the meantime. I eat ketogenic epi-paleo w/ those modifications, fish at least one of two meals daily (mostly salmon) lots of vegetables (non-starch), maybe a serving of berries, some macadamias and tigernuts, lots of fat (coconut oil, olive oil, ghee, etc.). I take enzymes, HCl w/pepsin, and bile with meals, vitamin D, I do use cannabis occasionally which, although likely far from optimal, is a vice I use opposed to the alcohol chugged by most kids every weekend. If necessary to eliminate completely for a period of time, I will indeed. (My goal is optimal in the modern world, so I'm hoping there is room for very occasional vices such as that, but it's far from the forefront of my research.)
\-For the last 2-3 weeks I have drunk only fluoride-free water, and I am increasing my water intake currently.
\-I have to wake up daily at 6AM for school (about hour before sunrise. If I need to sleep until sunrise for optimal, I WILL get a doctor's note and do so daily (my mom is a _great_ fighter when she is right). Sunrise does wake me up consistently on weekends, possibly because I did an attempted leptin reset for 4-6 weeks but was not persistent (on weekends) and the sour taste in my mouth and probably bad breath were horrible, I don't think I could digest the protein, not to mention 50g is probably too much for me.
\-I spend MUCH of my time in an EMF hell-hole. Think a school with 1300 kids, ALL with school-issued macbooks (one of best and wealthiest public schools in country), almost everyone has an IPHONE ON AT ALL TIMES and a wi-fi router in EVERY SINGLE ROOM. I am hoping to get accepted for an abroad program to Turkey next year. How great would that be. Otherwise, I would seriously consider trying to move with my mom's family in another country until college when I have more control over my env't, or just getting home-schooled or something.
\- I walk whenever possible and use a steam sauna sometimes, but no weight lifting or sprinting (I would normally follow generally the primal blueprint fitness plan) because I get headaches and a very dry mouth and just don't perform how I know I could.

The persisting symptoms are poor sleep, inability to eat more varied diet w/o symptoms, fatigue, occasional headaches, a little excess chub on my face (probably not a biggy), rings under my eyes (not as bad as a year ago), DRY MOUTH, SOUR TASTE IN MOUTH, PRESSURE FEELING IN ESOPHAGUS after not eating, HORRIBLE MOOD (sometimes), FEELING JITTERY AND ON EDGE, MIGRAINES WITH EXERCISE (caps locked are the worst and most annoying because they having very significantly impacted my life making me socially withdraw for a few months, literally, because I thought I had horrible breath. I do see a therapist currently for family-issues (my parents are divorced and I have four siblings and we all live at my mom's and it's not peaceful by any means, so I started staying at my dad's alone (with just him) 3-4 nights/week.

So I have ~1.5 million questions, but I will focus on a main few and I would really appreciate any advice from anyone. Also please keep in mind that I have likely read a lot of general paleo/primal (and even a decent bit of Jack's) recommendations on these things. My situation is shifting after the antibiotic and other anti-thing so I will keep updating if anyone has time to listen. Also, with the low white blood cell count I haven't gotten sick at all in the last year (maybe the parasite being strong and watching out for me....). Lastly, my old symptoms had been around since age 7 since i was a vegetarian, so I think the parasite has been with me for a long time.

1) my sleep- especially EMFs and grounding. I know to avoid EMFs and I know the largest sources, but I don't think turning the wi-fi off will be easy to push through my hard-headed family members, unless I prove it is very important, which I'm very willing to argue for. Turning of the breaker is also unlikely, although I will fight very hard for it if is really necessary (please let me know about this). I tried using earthing/grounding mat/sheet but I haven't noticed any benefit, and I have stopped as I said previously. Does anyone have any near-definitive points to make for those, as it really does seem that it's hard to replicate earth-to-body grounding through an electrical system or even a direct ground rod? For now I am just stopping it, but if I were to use it I would probably use the strap and attach that to the rod directly into the ground. it would be great if someone could make clear why artificial grounding is different from real grounding.

2) any thoughts on why cocoa, coffee, dairy, and the small amount of non-paleo foods cause problems? I know I have a leaky gut, and I'm working on it currently, but any connections to be made?

3) in the context of a leptin-sensitive person doing CT in the winter, does a sauna or hot shower totally mess up the pathway? and if I must remain in my EMF hellhole school would CT be prudent while in session (not summer break)

4) I bike to school when I can but have stopped until I reach optimal because I feel it is an uneccessary stress. It is 4.5 miles from mom's and 6.5 from dad's (very hilly). Of course, I'm the ONLY!!! one in school who does this.... everyone thinks I'm crazy, but I'll keep it up when optimal. Could this be bad in any way (besides risk of accident, I have VERY bright lights and am very careful) so early in the morning, right after breakfast, possibly before sunrise (if I am OK to wake up that early and reach optimal? other thoughts?

5) I usually eat only breakfast around 6:30 am and dinner around 3:30-7:00. I skip lunch because it is sooooo much easier for my diet (although I could make it work if necessary). Should I eat lunch? I DO make up for the deficit in calories with a big breakfast and big early dinner

6) I want to avoid EMFs as much as possible. Based on my situation, what are the best moves? I know sleep is most important, so I am very willing and probably will start sleeping outside once I reach optimal, or once it's warmer. I will push to have wi-fi and breaker off at night. I think earthing mats/sheets will probably not help me but I might be wrong. I will continue to work on this one and try to learn more. Any merit to any repulsion/absorption such as crystals, lead stuff, other devices? I'm going to pick my chem and physics teachers' brains a lot to learn more

7) I use amber goggle consistently after dark and constantly get to sleep before 10pm, usually 9 (most kids don't sleep until 11 or 12........  . What are your thoughts on having to wake up before sunrise?

8) Also, what do you guys think I should do for diet? I definitely want to stay high in seafood, high in low-carb vegetables, high in fat, and other stuff.

Please let me know.

Thanks,

\- Matt

### Matty_M — 2015-02-05T01:34:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/post-154761>

Coming from a state of really poor health, I dont think I could do CT just because my instinct is that my body couldn't handle it and it would hurt more than help.

Also, I don't think I should try a leptin Rx until I am sure this parasite is gone because I don't think I will be digesting a lot of the protein (my stool sample showed poor digestion).

And I don't know whether I will even be able to do the Leptin Rx as I "have to" rise before sunrise every weekday............would one of those alarms that work with blue light work or totally throw off my circadian biology by unnaturally altering the length of day my body perceives?

I'm from Narberth, PA, part of a string of pretty wealthy suburbs about 12 miles NW of Philadelphia. I looked at a map in my teacher's classroom of lights at night on the globe and I'm in basically the brightest area (NY, DE, Philly) besides the Netherlands and Tokyo, and I'm sure the lights accompany the EMFs. My school is part of the district that had a scandal a few years ago about pictures being taken of kids remotely with their school-issued laptop. It's so high tech. Every room has a projector, again, ~25 cell phones and 25 computers per room with 1300 kids- I'm seriously considering trying to go to school with some of my mom's family in another country, also awaiting acceptance to an abroad program I applied for, most likely to Turkey, but I could go anywhere (I really hope it works out).

I really wonder whether letting time play out could be my best move with diet and all.

Hi Matty, thanks for joining!

Turning of the breaker is also unlikely, although I will fight very hard for it if is really necessary (please let me know about this). I tried using earthing/grounding mat/sheet but I haven't noticed any benefit, and I have stopped as I said previously. Does anyone have any near-definitive points to make for those, as it really does seem that it's hard to replicate earth-to-body grounding through an electrical system or even a direct ground rod? For now I am just stopping it, but if I were to use it I would probably use the strap and attach that to the rod directly into the ground. it would be great if someone could make clear why artificial grounding is different from real grounding.

Here is an attempt at a simple answer from my understanding:
\-The earth generates a native magnetic field that is within the ELF (extremely low frequency) range, termed the Schumann Resonance: [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Schumann\_resonances](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Schumann_resonances)
\-Their range is 3 Hz - 60 Hz, with the major peak at 7.83 Hz and then additional, less major peaks of decreasing intensity at roughly aligned multiples of 7.83 Hz (i.e. 14.3, 20.8, 27.3...)
\-The field is a DC/direct current
\-Their intensity is 0.25 - 0.65 gauss
\-The fields are stronger when the sun is not visible in your area, i.e. at night

All of the above criteria are what humans have been exposed to during sleep for the entirety of our history, up until roughly 100 years ago, which factors all assist in 'our' bodies to properly detect night and heal/recover sufficiently

So, WiFi:
\-Generate 2.4GHz -5 GHz fields (note these are GHz vs Hz, 1 GHz = 1,000,000,000 Hz; 1 Hz = 1 'cycle'/'wave' per second, so GHz is extremely higher cycles)
\-Typically generate pulsed signals (vs. a direct current/constant signal such as the Earth's)

The power grid in your home:
\-In the USA, the standard power grid is 60 Hz.
\-Our power grid is AC/alternating current (this means it switches back and forth constantly)
\-Power is running through the lines even when nothing is plugged in

Considering the distinct discrepancies between the Earth's field, and these couple of examples, I hope it is easy to see why humans are incompatible with our own inventions. Shutting off the WiFi will reduce the pulsed, GHz fields. Another 'hack' you could try, per se the family wasn't convinced, would be giving your router a 'foil hat' at night (in a very literal sense).
By shutting off the breaker to your bedroom, you are going to be more likely to 'sense' the Earth's natural fields. If you are living on the second floor, consider that the room below you is still providing the incorrect 60 Hz AC field unless it is also shut down.

### sjoshua — 2015-02-05T02:39:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/post-154764>

> [Matty\_M said:](/goto/post?id=154756)
>
> 2) any thoughts on why cocoa, coffee, dairy, and the small amount of non-paleo foods cause problems? I know I have a leaky gut, and I'm working on it currently, but any connections to be made?

Cocoa & coffee are stimulants; if your cells can't work at their normal speed, giving them 'hyperdrive' will only increase the chaos. Dairy, it seems you are already aware is an issue.

> [Matty\_M said:](/goto/post?id=154756)
>
> 3) in the context of a leptin-sensitive person doing CT in the winter, does a sauna or hot shower totally mess up the pathway? and if I must remain in my EMF hellhole school would CT be prudent while in session (not summer break)

CT as much and as often as possible would be suitable in our modern world. If you want to utilize the hot WITH the cold, then alternating back and forth is additionally beneficial, always ending on cold.

> [Matty\_M said:](/goto/post?id=154756)
>
> 4) I bike to school when I can but have stopped until I reach optimal because I feel it is an uneccessary stress. It is 4.5 miles from mom's and 6.5 from dad's (very hilly). Of course, I'm the ONLY!!! one in school who does this.... everyone thinks I'm crazy, but I'll keep it up when optimal. Could this be bad in any way (besides risk of accident, I have VERY bright lights and am very careful) so early in the morning, right after breakfast, possibly before sunrise (if I am OK to wake up that early and reach optimal? other thoughts?

I believe you are wise here, exercise should only be done to each's tolerance. Too much of a good thing doesn't mean its a better thing. IF you wake up feeling ready to ride once in a while, it may be wise to follow that instinct. Listen to your body.

> [Matty\_M said:](/goto/post?id=154756)
>
> 5) I usually eat only breakfast around 6:30 am and dinner around 3:30-7:00. I skip lunch because it is sooooo much easier for my diet (although I could make it work if necessary). Should I eat lunch? I DO make up for the deficit in calories with a big breakfast and big early dinner

I do not imagine there is a reason to force a meal, and it would seem entirely more beneficial in my view for you to maintain a fasting state during your day 'swimming' in EMF.

> [Matty\_M said:](/goto/post?id=154756)
>
> 6) I want to avoid EMFs as much as possible. Based on my situation, what are the best moves? I know sleep is most important, so I am very willing and probably will start sleeping outside once I reach optimal, or once it's warmer. I will push to have wi-fi and breaker off at night. I think earthing mats/sheets will probably not help me but I might be wrong. I will continue to work on this one and try to learn more. Any merit to any repulsion/absorption such as crystals, lead stuff, other devices? I'm going to pick my chem and physics teachers' brains a lot to learn more

Sleeping outside may prove beneficial before achieving optimal, and before it gets warmer... think - free CT  Also, this would avoid convincing your family of turning things off - OR alternatively, you could do it as an experiment for a couple of weeks to help DEMONSTRATE to them that you sleep/function/etc better when NOT exposed to the fields.

I think you are right about the earthing mats/sheets - these are contextual and rely on your circuitry being spot on.

I believe there is merit to crystals, faraday cages, other devices etc.

> [Matty\_M said:](/goto/post?id=154756)
>
> 7) I use amber goggle consistently after dark and constantly get to sleep before 10pm, usually 9 (most kids don't sleep until 11 or 12........  . What are your thoughts on having to wake up before sunrise?

Following the sun's schedule is our easiest bet to track properly with sleep, but in lieu of being able to switch your school time, I think you are properly mitigating the effect with an earlier bed time. Earlier than that even (again, with the sun's schedule and time of year in mind), may be beneficial too.

> [Matty\_M said:](/goto/post?id=154756)
>
> 8) Also, what do you guys think I should do for diet? I definitely want to stay high in seafood, high in low-carb vegetables, high in fat, and other stuff.

Your list is solid. Local, seasonal, fresh whenever possible and available.

Stool showed poor protein digestion? interesting. My point above regarding antibiotics destroying the natural homeostasis of gut population may have to do with this too... Maybe others can speak better regarding if/when the Leptin Rx could be re-approached.

> [Matty\_M said:](/goto/post?id=154761)

The blue light alarms/lights do mimic morning, but then again also require power being turned on where you are using it. Consider that you may still be able to go through the Leptin Rx even with this schedule, but it may just hinder/lengthen your adaptation phase.

> [Matty\_M said:](/goto/post?id=154761)

Every room has a projector, again, ~25 cell phones and 25 computers per room with 1300 kids- I'm seriously considering trying to go to school with some of my mom's family in another country, also awaiting acceptance to an abroad program I applied for, most likely to Turkey, but I could go anywhere (I really hope it works out).

Check out your address on [www.antennasearch.com](http://www.antennasearch.com) - it is a repository of all cell/antenna/towers within a 4 mi radius anywhere in the US. It may be an interesting comparison to check out your mom vs dads for starters... The other country thing would likely be 1000 fold better still, unless it was somewhere quite tech advanced.

Time is all we truly have here, friend. Embracing change when and where necessary to maximize your personal enjoyment and span of that time may be a worthwhile factor to ponder.

### cantweight — 2015-02-05T03:39:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/post-154767>

I lived in that area as a kid...Northeast Philly, Royersford, Chester Springs. You're right its crappy, you need to mitigate not wait it out.

Dude, ground....feet to earth, get sunshine, drink good water, eat as much seafood and as little sugar as you can. Limit screen time. Work on getting good sleep. End you hot showers with cold water....start with a few seconds and work up. Face dunks are an option too.

I like food grade diatomaceous earth for parasites....

### sjoshua — 2015-02-06T18:13:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/post-154905>

> [Matty\_M said:](/goto/post?id=154836)
>
> any thoughts on what Dave Stetzer has to say on this podcast- [http://oneradionetwork.com/health/d...nergy-they-are-not-the-same-december-17-2013/](http://oneradionetwork.com/health/dave-stetzer-the-difference-between-grounding-the-body-and-discharging-energy-they-are-not-the-same-december-17-2013/) ? it is basically about how all ground is electrified because the US uses grounded electricity systems, and he says it is best to "discharge" on aluminium foil.

I haven't listened to this podcast, and don't specifically have a desire to... so I am merely basing this on your quick synapse:

My first question is, where is the aluminum foil discharging to? Last time I checked, aluminum foil alone doesn't have the ability to act as any significant capacitor to store charge, so this seems a little silly...

My second question is, if 'all ground is electrified' is the case then why do I pay for power? it seems I could just plug things into the Earth... (I haven't tried this since I was a child, but it didn't work then... feel free to give it a whirl now to test )

My third question is, if 'all ground is electrified', then how does it still manage to ground our circuitry? This would seem impossible

EDIT: Thinking about this for another couple of minutes, I think this would be a fairly good point of reference for analyzing recommendations such as this:

Does the recommended action/choice take place in nature? (i.e., could you find an insect, plant, animal, element that mimics the same or a similar course of action/choices naturally?)
\-If yes, it is probably safe to assume it will work for humans
\-If no, it is much more likely nonsense, and/or a 'modern' approach to something that has a natural alternative. More often than not, I'd bet the natural alternative will be the more optimal choice.

I think I will definitely keep out the nuts/seeds (besides macadamias and coconut) and the dairy (besides ghee). The way that I have noticed cheese cause me symptoms so quickly and consistently tells me I definitely have a leaky gut problem and should avoid it all (besides ghee), I think even the amount of milk solids in butter will hinder me in trying to get rid of my persistent stuffy nose (that has gone away a lot in my dealings with paleo but has been sticking around recently far too long). Any thoughts on what might cause this leaky gut (or so it seems)? I am eating a very low-toxin, low-inflammatory diet (almost strictly epi-paleo).

I just get so impatient with all of this stuff because I really never know which way to turn. Is it mostly my diet, the EMFs, leptin, adrenal problems, or something else? I am working with a functional medicine practitioner who I'm seeing again soon, but even in the meantime I never know what to do. I'm set in stone on staying ketogenic, seafood-based- but I always get into a gray area with vegetables, low-sugar fruit, FODMAPs (which, even after antibiotics, sometimes cause symptoms, sometimes don't), etc., etc.. I get caught up in all the details that I stress over them, lose sight of the big picture, which explains precisely the binging issues that I face a few months ago.

I'm doing my best, and it's greater than anything to even be able to have some people to talk to on here. I even see a therapist for family-related stuff, but I am certain when I have hit optimal, I will have no need because 95% of my perceived worries (the ones about health) will be gone. ### Penny — 2015-02-07T08:07:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/post-154951>

Greetings Matty
Dairy products contain glutamates - as does wheat - and anything processed - think "citric acid", "natural flavors" etc - these are the things you are reacting to and the reason you have migraines - it's called "excitotoxicity":
[http://jackkruse.com/msg-your-gut-and-your-brain-post-trauma/](http://jackkruse.com/msg-your-gut-and-your-brain-post-trauma/)
[http://jackkruse.com/cpc-8-quantum-brain-cancer/](http://jackkruse.com/cpc-8-quantum-brain-cancer/)
[www.unblindmymind.org](http://www.unblindmymind.org)

You might also want to replace any dairy with coconut oil and avocados as they are healing for the gut - keep the ghee if you have to have it but coconut oil is so much better for you - add bone broths and probiotics as well - or even grass fed gelatin - I've also heard that cravings for chocolate mean you are deficient in magnesium - so many of us are - Maybe you could take some magnesium at night - away from the zinc - great for sleep also - if you have restless leg syndrome - this is a mag deficiency -

the above link has a glutamate list of foods/drinks/ingredients you should avoid - glutamates are put into all processed food to "tickle" your neurons and make you think the crap that is mimicking real food actually tastes good - then it kills the neuron - I never understood whether calcium is going out or coming in but I do know that EMF causes calcium to efflux - this is bad: google "calcium efflux emf" and you get a bunch of links -
[http://www.salzburg.gv.at/Proceedings\_(12)\_Blackman.pdf](http://www.salzburg.gv.at/Proceedings_%2812%29_Blackman.pdf)

google "glutamate list" for the lovely names MSG goes by - Russel Blaylock also wrote a book: "MSG the taste that kills" -

remove the glutamates and it's smooth sailing on the migraine front... my sister in law can stop a migraine in about 2 minutes with magnesium and B12...

I think K2 with some vitamin D would be an excellent move as well - take it with zinc and iodine in the morning - with a B vitamin - like maybe Thorne #12 -

I would recommend Epsom Salts - either bath or foot soak - chased with morning sun over most of your body between 9:00-noon for the infrared light.

I also would recommend zinc for the low stomach acid that let the parasites in to begin with... you need zinc, iodine and B1 to make stomach acid - zinc is found in oysters - 200% of the daily requirement - it is also used to make active B6 (P5P) which is used to make dopamine/serotonin - which is needed for melatonin - which is the sleep hormone... zinc is used in like 300 enzymatic reactions - also, when stomach acid is low, you might pick up something like h pylori riding in on the food - so my standard advice is to get a stool test from American Gut ([www.americangut.org](http://www.americangut.org)) - for $99.00 they will tell you the DNA of your gut flora - like down to the .04% - even getting the stomach bugs... a very accurate test - they list 3 pages of taxa - better than anything I've seen Genova or those other pricey labs - the turnaround time might not be as fast, but you will know what is killing you at the end of it...

Unfortunately, you need stomach acid to break down zinc, so I usually recommend zinc with l-carnosine - the carnosine helps with the zinc absorption - just get this stuff: Now: Ulcetrol - cheap and great for the gut - zinc lozengers also work -

And check out this awesome post by Dr. K regarding calcium efflux - it's amazing:
[http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/jack-for-calcium-help-its-elaine.11935/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/jack-for-calcium-help-its-elaine.11935/)

You might also want to get the Wahls protocol book - it's for MS but it's great for anyone trying to implement an autoimmune protocol - the dry mouth concerns me as you might actually have Sjogrens Syndrome - or not - it's all EMF - it makes the bugs grow - the more protons, the more bugs -

Stetzer - I have listened to him talk several times - is referring to the neutral wire going back to the electric company - the power companies are now using that instead of ground... or like it's ground... the only way to know is to measure over time - to see if there are any spikes - you can used a voltmeter to measure the ground - check out all the info by Michael Neuert - an EMF guy who mitigates it - google:
"michael neuert emf"

He shows you how to make your own voltmeter - then you can see what effect dirty electricity is having on you -

And lastly, it seems to me that I am less hungry and less willing to "cheat" when I eat seafood - and it works best when the seafood has bacon wrapped around it... I am not hungry for hours... it might work for you - also, going to bed with ice packs while reading with a red incandescant lightbulb seems to really help me fall asleep - not that I stay there, but at least I go there in 7 minutes or less... get the bulbs at any big box store for $3.00 - so worth it really -

Not to overwhelm you but you can also read Peter at Hyperlipid for adventures in going in and out of ketosis using the ketogenic diet - it's entertaining at the very least and illuminating too:-0

Dr. K wrote a book called "The Epi Paleo RX" - well worth it IMHO - frankly, I can't wait for the next book as this blog is becoming over the top to find stuff as there is so much stuff here to find...

Happy thoughts Do NOT give up - there is an answer for you - you just have to find it

> I also would recommend zinc for the low stomach acid that let the parasites in to begin with... you need zinc, iodine and B1 to make stomach acid - zinc is found in oysters - 200% of the daily requirement - it is also used to make active B6 (P5P) which is used to make dopamine/serotonin - which is needed for melatonin - which is the sleep hormone... zinc is used in like 300 enzymatic reactions - also, when stomach acid is low, you might pick up something like h pylori riding in on the food - so my standard advice is to get a stool test from American Gut ([www.americangut.org](http://www.americangut.org)) - for $99.00 they will tell you the DNA of your gut flora - like down to the .04% - even getting the stomach bugs... a very accurate test - they list 3 pages of taxa - better than anything I've seen Genova or those other pricey labs - the turnaround time might not be as fast, but you will know what is killing you at the end of it...
>
> Unfortunately, you need stomach acid to break down zinc, so I usually recommend zinc with l-carnosine - the carnosine helps with the zinc absorption - just get this stuff: Now: Ulcetrol - cheap and great for the gut - zinc lozengers also work -
>
> And check out this awesome post by Dr. K regarding calcium efflux - it's amazing:
> [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/jack-for-calcium-help-its-elaine.11935/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/jack-for-calcium-help-its-elaine.11935/)
>
> You might also want to get the Wahls protocol book - it's for MS but it's great for anyone trying to implement an autoimmune protocol - the dry mouth concerns me as you might actually have Sjogrens Syndrome - or not - it's all EMF - it makes the bugs grow - the more protons, the more bugs -
>
> Stetzer - I have listened to him talk several times - is referring to the neutral wire going back to the electric company - the power companies are now using that instead of ground... or like it's ground... the only way to know is to measure over time - to see if there are any spikes - you can used a voltmeter to measure the ground - check out all the info by Michael Neuert - an EMF guy who mitigates it - google:
> "michael neuert emf"
>
> He shows you how to make your own voltmeter - then you can see what effect dirty electricity is having on you -
>
> And lastly, it seems to me that I am less hungry and less willing to "cheat" when I eat seafood - and it works best when the seafood has bacon wrapped around it... I am not hungry for hours... it might work for you - also, going to bed with ice packs while reading with a red incandescant lightbulb seems to really help me fall asleep - not that I stay there, but at least I go there in 7 minutes or less... get the bulbs at any big box store for $3.00 - so worth it really -
>
> Not to overwhelm you but you can also read Peter at Hyperlipid for adventures in going in and out of ketosis using the ketogenic diet - it's entertaining at the very least and illuminating too:-0
>
> Dr. K wrote a book called "The Epi Paleo RX" - well worth it IMHO - frankly, I can't wait for the next book as this blog is becoming over the top to find stuff as there is so much stuff here to find...
>
> Happy thoughts Do NOT give up - there is an answer for you - you just have to find it

Thanks for all the advice! I am not unlike the average person in that my two biggest hindrances are TIME and MONEY.... I am not very well-off currently, I have about $100 to my name. What I mean is that in the last year a very good amount of money has been spent on all types of supplements and other things to help me "heal". My parents are very ignorant only in the sense that they do not bother to try to educate themselves on what I am doing, therefore it can, understandably, come off as pseudoscience or something, sadly. I am extremely hesitant to spend any more money on supplements, and will probably only be able to do so anyway with my functional medicine practitioner giving to OK to my dad. Otherwise he is very skeptical. I think what JanSz recommended about a fecal transplant could be my most cost effective solution. I continue with my digestive supplements (HCl (w/pepsin) , Enzymes, and Bile, as well as apple cider vinegar. I NEED bones for bone broth, but bones are just so danged hard to get in winter.

I will search glutamates but as I only eat fish, meat, eggs & some fowl, vegetables, some berries, some coconut/macadamias/tigernuts, and a lot of fat, I can't imagine I'm consuming much at all. I am going to try to build into my schedule more sauna time, definitely some epsom salt baths, and I am going to slowly begin CT (I am trying to sleep in my backyard at night to avoid EMFs, but I'm near Philly and it's averaging 10 degrees outside at night, so I think I will work with it to the point where I can burn fat as free heat. If the native americans here did it, I can do it.). I cook with coconut oil, but, like all other fats, I don't eat it out of the jar, especially as I'm a little afraid of the herxheimer that I have felt from it, although after the antibiotic it might be non-existent (regardless, is there any reason/scenario I should eat the oil alone? I will continue to prefer it for cooking, AND I have begun making breakfast shakes with frozen frozen kale, spinach, a can of full fat, BPA-free lined coconut milk (like 65g of what is coconut oil), a half cup of frozen blueberries, 2 tbsp collagen powder, and cinnamon. Talk about a lot of coconut fat. Sadly, that stool test probably won't be feasible, but I have a feeling it won't be necessary.

here is my list of things to buy that I will like get parental assistance with(any approval/constructive criticism is well-warranted)

1- a 64 oz hydroflask water bottle- undoubtedly the best at keeping water just as cold as it starts for a LONG time (especially when I'm in EMF hell (cough, school, cough) and don't want to refill with tap or buy water bottles)
2- a "light" alarm clock that will wake me up each morning because I have to wake up before sunrise for school (during late fall/winter)- could there be any adverse affects to this?
3- an adjustable standing desk (i will probably make my own) so I can work outside grounded because I spend a large portion of time studying/researching
4- a reverse osmosis filter (so my parents dont have to keep buying the big 2.5 gallons jug things)
5- jack's webinar library (this will probably end up being the biggest expense....)
6- a tri-meter for radiation detection (this is the one I want most but it will probably scare me when I get it, I've already envisioned myself trying it and school, knowing that it WILL go off the chart, and just walking out of school .
7- emf protective clothing (I think a light jacket to wear over my first layer, under my second; a hat lining that I can put into any hat, and boxers to protect what might be my kids- although with overpopulation it might be better to become part of the solution...)
9- headphone extenders and low-radiation headphones so I can remain somewhat part of society
8- a "disposable" shielding tent (they only cost $13 on less emf) and a schumann resonator device (this is a much more hypothetical idea- it would be like a bubble from nn-EMF)

Thanks for the help.

\- Matt

Your Mom sounds as best candidate.
I think it would be a good idea to confirm that the parasite is gone, but if too much trouble, leave that for latter. First step is to get you on your feet first.

You will have to research more on the system, I do not have details, but it seems rather simple procedure.
You may want to contact the links in my post above, have a consult, decide if you want to use white coat help or DIY. Anyhow you would learn some details from consults.

The small capsules sound good, just not too small.

In case of the other end I was using this setup for my coffee/Epsom salt enemas, beats anything else I tried, no spilling, total control.
Catheter + 60cc syringe, got from Amazon.

Off hand I suggest supply from both ends unless you figure other convincing suggestions.

Ideally we should get all from food and not use any supplement.

In practice, for myself I use Spectracell Micronutrient test that tells me about 35 most commonly discussed micronutrients. You can have that test for cash or your functional medicine practitioner can order it for you. (I found it simpler to to pay cash but your mileage may vary).
(Ie; having that test available doctor may feel by-passed).
Practically, because of the way that test works it make sense to do it no more than once every 6 months. That is what I did first 2 years, but now I am rather stable and test once a year.

That test measures (functional) levels, the results are applicable individually to you, no guessing.
Other tests measure and results are compared to average levels within population.

My last test I got directly from company (they held my hand by e-mail till end, until I got pdf file with results).

[http://spectracell.mybigcommerce.com](http://spectracell.mybigcommerce.com)
Micronutrient Test
$390.00
\------
Same test is also available from re-seller (no personal experience):
[https://healthremedies.com/intracellular\_vitamin\_analysis\_spectracell.html](https://healthremedies.com/intracellular_vitamin_analysis_spectracell.html)
Spectralcell Comprehensive Nutritional Panel
$350.00
\------

Previous years;
first test, on my request, my doc ordered for me.
From my pov all was peachy.
Doc told me that it was too much paperwork for his staff.
Following years I did the paperwork, doc signed, all was well.

Now test is available without doc's script, so I do not bother my doc.

### Graeme — 2015-02-08T14:50:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/post-155027>

Hello Matt,

It is so nice that you are here. How I wish I had the lively brain of a 15 yr old again! You are our hope for the future. Your presence on these blogs is vital. Please stick around even if us oldies are a little tiresome with our wisdom sometimes. I have two nieces of your age and I am delighted to be able to use your input as an inspiración to them. Thank you.

Now, here's the best bit: you are only 15, but you are willing to seek advice and you are very keen to learn. You have already shown a great understanding of the concepts. Here, people who are very broken and often rather old, manage to fix themselves over time. You have to face directly the challenges of the modern world especially the EMF nightmare aka school, but you are young and now becoming truly informed.

Just keep following the protocols, drink even more water and forget the supplements. You are young enough that your turnaround will be more rapid than you imagine and way quicker than 99% of people here.

Try to be a little patient. You may need 3-6 months, but you will make it.

Whatever happens, please stay active on here. It is a pleasure to have you around.

Finally, two quick thoughts.
Maybe only two meals per day is a bit tough for a growing lad. Don't force yourself, three is fine until you're fixed.

Regarding the fecal transplant idea. I think you are young enough to fix your gut using the protocols alone. Nevertheless, it remains an interesting option. My own thoughts are that gut flora is highly sensitive to nnEMF, so it is likely that your family's flora is also altered and not ideal. Although maybe they have developed a balance and have a flora that works well in your environment. If it were my choice, I think I would try to choose flora which is currently not exposed to high nnEMF and then try my hardest to keep it in tip top shape by using the protocols rather than start with an already modified flora from someone who shares your own nnEMF situation.
It is an interesting question though: is it better to take perfect flora then expose it to nnEMF or go with altered flora (which appears to work ok for your family) as it may already have adapted to somehow cope with the altered environment?

Please keep us informed.
All the best!

Graeme

### JanSz — 2015-02-08T18:05:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/post-155037>

**Donor Requirements**

[http://thepowerofpoop.com/epatients/donors/donor-eligibility/](http://thepowerofpoop.com/epatients/donors/donor-eligibility/)

To be an eligible donor you must be at least 18 years old, in excellent health and on a healthy diet. Your stool (poop) should look like a 3 or 4 on the [Bristol stool chart](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bristol_stool_scale) and you should satisfy the requirements of this [Donor Screening Questionnaire](http://thepowerofpoop.com/wp/wp-content/uploads/2013/12/FMT-Donor-Screening-Questionnaire.pdf). Please don’t proceed if you don’t satisfy these requirements as your time will be wasted.

You cannot be on medication, ever have used antibiotics regularly or in the last 6 months, have travelled overseas to a high-risk country in the last 6 months or practise [unsafe sex](http://www.oxforddictionaries.com/definition/english/unsafe-sex). Personal medical questions, blood and fecal [testing](http://thepowerofpoop.com/wp/wp-content/uploads/2013/01/Fecal-Transplant-Donor-Tests.pdf) will be required. For specific screening criteria, see the [Donor Screening Questionnaire](http://thepowerofpoop.com/wp/wp-content/uploads/2013/12/FMT-Donor-Screening-Questionnaire.pdf).

\- See more at: [http://thepowerofpoop.com/epatients/donors/donor-eligibility/#sthash.V1gPWeAY.dpuf](http://thepowerofpoop.com/epatients/donors/donor-eligibility/#sthash.V1gPWeAY.dpuf)

> here is my list of things to buy that I will like get parental assistance with(any approval/constructive criticism is well-warranted)
>
> 1- a 64 oz hydroflask water bottle- undoubtedly the best at keeping water just as cold as it starts for a LONG time (especially when I'm in EMF hell (cough, school, cough) and don't want to refill with tap or buy water bottles)
> 2- a "light" alarm clock that will wake me up each morning because I have to wake up before sunrise for school (during late fall/winter)- could there be any adverse affects to this?
> 3- an adjustable standing desk (i will probably make my own) so I can work outside grounded because I spend a large portion of time studying/researching
> 4- a reverse osmosis filter (so my parents dont have to keep buying the big 2.5 gallons jug things)
> 5- jack's webinar library (this will probably end up being the biggest expense....)
> 6- a tri-meter for radiation detection (this is the one I want most but it will probably scare me when I get it, I've already envisioned myself trying it and school, knowing that it WILL go off the chart, and just walking out of school .
> 7- emf protective clothing (I think a light jacket to wear over my first layer, under my second; a hat lining that I can put into any hat, and boxers to protect what might be my kids- although with overpopulation it might be better to become part of the solution...)
> 9- headphone extenders and low-radiation headphones so I can remain somewhat part of society
> 8- a "disposable" shielding tent (they only cost $13 on less emf) and a schumann resonator device (this is a much more hypothetical idea- it would be like a bubble from nn-EMF)
>
> Thanks for the help.
>
> \- Matt

Matt, here is my thoughts;

Nr. 1. could be useful I guess!

Nr. 2. Not necessary IMHO. Just use a battery driven alarm clock and as the sun rises just look into the sky instead. I am sure you can do that on the way to school, or at school on your first break!

Nr. 3 is great idea  I have been thinking about buying a standing desk myself....

Nr. 4. Sound like a great idea too.

Nr. 5. Great stuff

Nr. 6. I would not buy it, buts ask if someone on the forum could borrow you their meter... I would do anytime... I just live in Europe it is a bit far.... But let me know if you need to borrow it I will send it to you for sure! I only have a trimeter tho... but it works too

Nr. 7. IDK.... but maybe a canopy would be the first thing to go for? The bed canopies are very expensive tho

Nr. 9. Not necessary IMHO

Nr. 8. I guess a great idea.... are you gonna sleep in there? How can you get fresh air? I think it is air tight? I would need a canopy that lets air into it..... cant sleep without fresh air

I would not care too much about supplements.... instead... make high meat.. do fish head smoothies... go and pick wild edibles/herbs from the ground in the woods... they are the best supplements
and for sure daylight.. sunshine... grounding.. darkness... seafood..... CT...... water........
I guess... supplements can even hurt you! I would use the money for fish heads/seafood/oysters/seaweed instead, anytime..
That said.. some supps can sure be useful. I have tried MSM (organic sulfur) lately and I really like it.... but it is very cheap. 1 kg = 15 €

a smile... to care for someone... love... touch... those are amazing "supplements" too

I'm drinking probably 3/4 gallon per day of spring water, trying to increase. ### Penny — 2015-02-09T20:03:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/post-155144>

Or you can do the poor man's allergy test: stick whatever you're testing on the inside of your wrist - say an egg yolk (test yolks and whites separately) and see if you develop a rash/hive/itch - if not, it is likely ok to eat it. You are obviously still eating something you are reacting to or you wouldn't have a stuffy nose...

Here is how to make a "bone broth": Buy a chicken, stick it in a pot, cover it with water (about 8 cups), put 1/4 cup of apple cider vinegar in it, some salt, simmer for 2 hours, pour into mason jars and drink one cup prior to each meal. Done.
You can also stick seaweed flakes in it to add iodine - and spices like oregano/rosemary/bay leaves,thyme,turmeric
Eat lots of spices/garlic/turmeric/coconut oil - sauerkraut -

Did you read the Leaky Gut RX? It's great -
[http://jackkruse.com/leaky-gut-prescription/](http://jackkruse.com/leaky-gut-prescription/)

You might also want to read about the GAPS intro diet:
[http://www.gapsdiet.com/INTRODUCTION\_DIET.html](http://www.gapsdiet.com/INTRODUCTION_DIET.html)

I wouldn't go near nuts until I had done bone broths BTW - lots of coconut is great though -

The fact that your mom had thyroid cancer is a huge tell Matt about your environment - you might want to check out [www.k12.com](http://www.k12.com) - an online school - I would avoid your school like the plague it is... truly, it is killing you -

This is why I recommend k2/d - it keeps some of the calcium in the bones where it belongs... and it's cheap - also, eat kelp -

I will say it again, if you have parasites, you have low stomach acid ====> low zinc/iodine/B1 -

If you went to the site "unblindmymind.org" you would see how a woman healed her autistic daughter using green smoothies and avoiding glutamates - and they live in San Fransisco - one of the worst places you can live for EMF - it is similar to Terry Wahl's program - you can change the gut flora in like a day by eating greens and almonds - raw food -

If you have an infection, you should deal with it - I only recommended American Gut because it seems to be the cheapest option with the most taxa and you can actually see stomach bugs even though you use a stool sample - urrease producers are huge and very tough to test for - in fact, I would just assume you have one -

Before I did a fecal transplant, I would try changing the flora with diet - you also get a lot of flora when you go outside and play in the garden and in nature BTW -

Take care Matt and don't give up

### Inger — 2015-02-09T21:24:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/post-155146>

I would ditch the fowl (if not great quality pastured / wild ones)
I would ditch the nightshade veggies before the eggs. Are your eggs good quality, pastured and organic?
To ditch nightshades is very easy IMHO, I barely eat them ever without even thinking
Why do you want to try sweet potatoes in winter? Can you go more hardcore keto, ditch berries, less veggies, no potatoes..... more fat.... less nuts....

I get stuffy nose too if I react to something. Last winter my bro teased me and put some regular milk in my coffee without me noticing... i thought the color was a bit odd but we were on a trip and he got us coffee to go... and i thought maybe their coffee had this color. Immediately my nose got totally stuffed... it was so odd! I told it to my bro and then he admitted he put milk in my coffee...lol I then thought I will try to fight that inflammation with some canned mackerel we had in the car for the trip... so I had 2 cans of canned mackerel right after drinking the coffee. A wonder happened.... just a few minutes after I had finished the mackerel, my nose un-stuffed!

I thought that was a crazy cool experiment  Cause i did not know about the milk at all, but man I reacted really strong to it! I once in a while have a big piece (150-200g) of raw sheep cheeses like Roquefort.. and i never get stuffy nose from that.. go figure.... but regular supermarkt long lasting milk was like poison...lol

Amazing how anti inflammatory fatty fish can be  Maybe you should eat even more of it

Have you tried to spray MSM (sulfur) water or maybe sea salt water would work too, in your nose? Or to smell on a raw onion? Or to put half garlic gloves rolled in paper into your nose?
Maybe it could help a little too?

Thanks so much for the advice! I will try the allergy test with the nuts, nightshades, egg whites, and dairy. Per the functional doc, I will be strictly avoiding all dairy for at least 2 weeks, and following a seafood-based ketogenic diet with a few gut healing supplements from a company called APEX energetics along with a probiotic. The company seems like they really know what they are doing and the products seem to be great.

I have read the leaky gut Rx. I could take all dairy out easily (would ghee have to go?). Are nightshades without a doubt out of the diet? I do not do well when I really have to start limiting myself and it usually causes a much worse result (like a carb "binge" or just eating something I never would've otherwise") or just absurd cravings and unhappiness. Seafood-based ketogenic diet including eggs nightshades and nuts and no dairy is solid I think, and if after a few weeks with these gut healing compounds (GI Synergy, Repairvite, a good probiotic, Magnesium, Vit D., Collagen powder, a Turmeric extract, Vit C), if there is still a problem (which I highly doubt) then I will go full AIP and re-intro those foods one at a time.

As much as I would like to do the GAPS diet, I don't think I will. I have no reason to lay blame, however that was the diet that was so mentally challenging for me (likely because it was probably the most effective on my parasite, though I didn't complete it) that it was the first thing ever that made me completely disregard my health and binge on crap. That was one of the worst nights of my life. It began a really bad relationship with food that I continue to remedy (and have been doing very well for the last 5 months), so I'm just not prepared to go down that extreme path. To answer your question, though, I read the GAPS books a year ago now. Very fascinating, opened me to the concept of the Brain-Gut Axis. Cure autism? I thought it was witchcraft......boy was I wrong.

For the stool test, the functional doc had a Genova test ordered and that's what showed the parasite, so that's all good. I have been using Betain HCL and apple cider vinegar for stomach acid and continue to heal the gut to improve acid production. I feel like I'm getting better every day now after the antibiotic, it's crazy!

Thanks so much for your advice and perspective. I really appreciate it.

\- Matt

It might not make me optimal, but I want to heal my gut & flora, then when spring comes around and the sun is up when I get up for school, I will do the leptin Rx (with protein levels more fit for a 145 lb. 5'7" 15 year old, maybe 35-40g, and I have already begun the cold intro. Food and I have a bad relationship due to my carb cravings and other stuff and I am trying to repair it, but restricting myself really only leads to overthinking and eating crap crap I would never have otherwise. Please keep in mind I am constantly surrounded by people binging on the food that my brain still wants so badly. I know it is a matter of DOing or not doing, but if I limit myself to the point of feeling like I get no satisfaction from food (while I'm not very leptin sensitive and still pretty "into" carbs), it is much better to stay a little more open.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/post-155293>

> [Matty\_M said:](/goto/post?id=155290)
>
> Thanks so much for the advice! I will try the allergy test with the nuts, nightshades, egg whites, and dairy. Per the functional doc, I will be strictly avoiding all dairy for at least 2 weeks, and following a seafood-based ketogenic diet with a few gut healing supplements from a company called APEX energetics along with a probiotic. The company seems like they really know what they are doing and the products seem to be great.
>
> I have read the leaky gut Rx. I could take all dairy out easily (would ghee have to go?). Are nightshades without a doubt out of the diet? I do not do well when I really have to start limiting myself and it usually causes a much worse result (like a carb "binge" or just eating something I never would've otherwise") or just absurd cravings and unhappiness. Seafood-based ketogenic diet including eggs nightshades and nuts and no dairy is solid I think, and if after a few weeks with these gut healing compounds (GI Synergy, Repairvite, a good probiotic, Magnesium, Vit D., Collagen powder, a Turmeric extract, Vit C), if there is still a problem (which I highly doubt) then I will go full AIP and re-intro those foods one at a time.
>
> As much as I would like to do the GAPS diet, I don't think I will. I have no reason to lay blame, however that was the diet that was so mentally challenging for me (likely because it was probably the most effective on my parasite, though I didn't complete it) that it was the first thing ever that made me completely disregard my health and binge on crap. That was one of the worst nights of my life. It began a really bad relationship with food that I continue to remedy (and have been doing very well for the last 5 months), so I'm just not prepared to go down that extreme path. To answer your question, though, I read the GAPS books a year ago now. Very fascinating, opened me to the concept of the Brain-Gut Axis. Cure autism? I thought it was witchcraft......boy was I wrong.
>
> For the stool test, the functional doc had a Genova test ordered and that's what showed the parasite, so that's all good. I have been using Betain HCL and apple cider vinegar for stomach acid and continue to heal the gut to improve acid production. I feel like I'm getting better every day now after the antibiotic, it's crazy!
>
> Thanks so much for your advice and perspective. I really appreciate it.
>
> \- Matt

Remember not to hold too much weight on allergen tests. At best, they represent gut-mediated T-cell dysfunction at a point in time.

Because of the dynamism of the gut tissue (can turnover within 2-3 days), you can positive test for an allergic reaction on Monday, and be fine on Thursday, and vice versa.

Until we can non-invasive, safe, cheap, and continuous testing of such parameters, I will be biased towards watching for qualitative changes in gut health, as opposed to spending time and money on getting such tests.

Fixing the root cause, which is a low redox potential of the GALT which leads to immune dysfunction at the T-cell level, should be the primary concern.

In your case, the first two qualitative metrics to monitor are going to be the Bristol Score, as well as changes in mood (which is mediated by gut health).

\=====
**Dairy**

Most people's issues with dairy (myself included) stem from the casein proteins. Some do well with A2 casein, but personally I do not.

Ghee should contain no proteins at all, and should be fine.

Testing dairy intolerance is usually pretty straightforward. Eat on 4 separate occasions:

-   A1 casein Cheese
-   A2 casein Cheese
-   Whey protein powder
-   Milk (lactose + casein + whey)
-   Ghee (just fat)

Monitor for qualitative changes in health.

\-----

A more important question: Do you need dairy in your diet?

From a generalised health perspective, I would say the answer is no; you can live your entire life never eating any dairy and still be 100% optimal.

From a dietary adherence perspective, if you need some dairy to make a diet palatable, and it is tolerated, then I see good reason for inclusion.

From a biohacking perspective, I personally like Whey Protein as a means to boost glutathione function with an as-close-to-food source as possible.

\====
**Nuts**

As per the resistant starch webinar series, and the experience of many members on the forum, these can be used pretty therapeutically.

Make sure you get raw nuts that are as fresh as possible (as little oxidised fat as possible), and store them in the fridge or freezer.

### yewwei.tan — 2015-02-11T00:37:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/post-155294>

I am going to post segments of what I posted in PMs to you, because it will be useful for others on the forum as well.

Keep in mind to keep private information private

**\====== EXCERPTS START =======**

> I think the best move to minimize risk with EMFs is the grounding, and then to buy a silverell type light jacket and an EMF blocking hat lining that I can put in a beanie and wear often. In that case, I would also buy some fabric to sleep on, and some to cover myself at night, as my mom's house will not turn the wi-fi off due to a sister who has major sleep problems and is what I would describe as insane, although I truly believe she is sick and standing in her own way, but she won't let anyone help her. My dad's house is on a set of rowhomes all with smartmeters, wifi, and the rest, so it is probably a much worse environment and, although he might be disappointed because I recently started living with him, I might have to bring it to an end. I literally sleep within 500 feet of probably 40 smart meters. No joke. Maybe more. Do you think even turning off the wifi off at his house would be worth it if I am being blasted by all of the neighbors who are directly attached?

Let's break this down ....

\----
**Grounding Sheets**

Mileage is going to vary hugely based on your personal health context. Some people (myself included) have benefited of these. Others like Gretchen Bronson (Shinjin here on the forums) have seen symptoms go haywire when using these.

This is going to be a risky proposition, with the possibility of you getting excess nn-EMF due to the possible dirty-electricity amplifying effects of the materials used in grounding (which are almost always metals).

If you have the funds, you could try it, but I personally recommend that you don't worry about this option at all.

Remember the mechanism here: Grounding is used to get you free electrons from a more negatively charged earth (electrons are negatively charged).

Grounding doesn't work if:

-   (a) You can't make use of those electrons due to illness
-   (b) The source and wires that you're grounding with are not actually more negatively charged than your body (leading to electron steal)

\----
**Turning Off the WiFi / Measuring your EMF risk**

With so many smart-meters and EMF-emitting devices in your vicinity, this is going to be hard to determine whether or not your situation will improve by turning off WiFi.

Example: sometimes, your WiFi transmitter can actually create destructive interference with say your neighbours WiFi which is transmitting at the same frequency, thereby creating a bit of a "safe zone". We all know the strange parts of the house where the WiFi suddenly stops working. Note that this will not likely occur between WiFi and the smart-meters though, which typically emit a lower frequency radio-range EMF.

I will recommend borrowing or getting a trimeter ASAP and measuring your environment. There could be "safer zones" in your house that you are unaware of. Personally, I bought 2 meters for a total of about AU$300, and they definitely paid for themselves, but I know those funds are hard to come by.

Maybe you can see if you can do some school project related to EMF, and use that project as an excuse to either borrow some trimeters from your school's science lab, or to get your parents to actually buy you a trimeter.

**EMF Shielding**

> Also, what do you think of an EMF shielding tent (only $13) for a "disposable" one, and an artificial schumman resonance maker in there with me, and maybe a ground strap running to a rod into the ground directly outside of the tent in my mom's small backyard?

I think EMF shielding is a very good idea when implemented well. The tent is probably a good pickup.

The caveat is that most of these shielding materials also tend to amplify any signals that manage to get into the tent.

The only way to figure out is to test. This is where the trimeter will come in handy, but it is not needed at all.

Since the tent is cheap, I'd recommend you buy it, test a couple of nights (2-4) sleeping in it, and if your sleep gets destroyed, you know your answer right there.

> For sleep, I feel like a light alarm clock might be good to wake me up as opposed to a normal alarm. They really annoy me. Could this be a hindrance?

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...-positivity-is-coming-back.12814/#post-154748](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/leaky-gut-my-positivity-is-coming-back.12814/#post-154748)

Like I said in that post, the amount and type of CT I would recommend would depend on the person's disease state and constraints. If you had thyroid cancer like your mum, I'd recommend direct cold packs to the neck and carotids (while watching TV or something)

Predominantly gut-related symptoms will not benefit directly from surface CT, and it's more of a systemic energetic effect that you get while you are cold. I would recommend the same things that I told Dan:

-   Cold face dunks in a basin of ice water
-   Drink lots of ice water (direct CT to the gut )
-   Simply try to wear less clothing in the cold

\----

### yewwei.tan — 2015-02-11T00:37:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/post-155295>

> The hardest thing for me is always diet. I am working with a functional medicine practitioner and currently eating:
> 2-3 meals/day (usually 2), no snacks
> liberally: seafood, eggs, meat (rarely), fowl (rarely); many non-starch vegetables; fat (ghee, coconut, and olive oil)
> moderately: low-sugar fruit (maybe a cup of berries/day); nuts/seeds (primarily macadamia and coconut, some soaked pine nuts/pecans/almonds, cocoa and coffee)
> including: spices, bone broth (although not being the best with this), taking a variety of gut healing supplements including GI Synergy, Repairvite, a probiotic by the same brand as those two, some digestive enzymes, magnesium, vitamin D & C, and a turmeric extract
> avoiding: grains, legumes, dairy, sugar, vegetable oil, carbs- I have been occasionally having some banana (just 1 per batch) as a binder in these coconut egg cinnamon pancakes, and the rare sweet potato, however I don't want to eat them regularly as it is freezing here in Philadelphia (in the suburbs) and carbs should definitely be low.
>
> **I am questioning whether I should be eating nuts/seeeds** (besides macadamia and coconut), eggs, nightshades, and maybe even the ghee, given my situation and the stuffy nose that had been persisting in me.

Nuts can be very Helpful in healing the gut. The mechanism here involves direct delivery of probiotic starch to the distal colon (large intestine), which upregulates natural butyrate production.

Again, the only way to figure out if it is good for you is to test it out, and that means re-introducing nuts. The recommendations are to introduce as much variety as possible, but I'd opt for the lower Omega-6 containing nuts. Personally, I'd just start with:

-   Macadamias
-   Hazelnuts
-   Almonds

Eat maybe 10-12 pieces of each nut a day for a week, and see if symptoms improve.

\----

Then there is "Animal Fibre" -- [http://caloriesproper.com/animal-fibre](http://caloriesproper.com/animal-fibre)

Jack talked about this here -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...ntial-through-hydride-ions.10343/#post-118768](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/do-raw-foods-improve-redox-potential-through-hydride-ions.10343/#post-118768)

And I made some comments regarding this in the Molecular H2 thread -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...tochondrial-redox-compound.12674/#post-153724](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/molecular-hydrogen-h2-ultimate-mitochondrial-redox-compound.12674/#post-153724)

Personally, my recommendation is to just get some tendons and seafood shells into your system. Start slow here.

Recommended food sources:

-   Cuts of beef that are full of tendons
-   Prawn shells

Prawn shells are easy enough, just pan fry the prawns and eat the whole thing . I'd say 3-4 whole prawns (about 100+g / 4oz) produces a therapeutic effect.

For the beef, just buy a cut that would traditionally be used for slow-cooking or stewing, and then pan fry it like you would any other steak, cut it into decently large chunks, then chew minimally before swallowing. Cuts like Osso Bucco, Beef shins (usually called gravy beef), and oyster blade work well and taste decent. About 250g / 8oz of such meat is where I see a therapeutic effect.

Some people will get nausea a couple hours after trying this. In general, that is a sign that your gut flora is adapting. I would view it as a positive clinical sign, unless the nausea is either really bad, or sustained for >4 hours.

\----

Then there is the delivery of molecular H2 by the gut microbiome to the rest of the system. bio-fractal-soul-self and Jack have been talking about this -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...-ultimate-mitochondrial-redox-compound.12674/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/molecular-hydrogen-h2-ultimate-mitochondrial-redox-compound.12674/)

There are 2 ways to help deliver H2 to the body:

(a) Drink H2 enriched water
(b) Have a good gut flora

Personally, I think option (a) is possibly effective, but is expensive as hell, and doesn't have a high reward-to-cost ratio.

We're talking 14L of H2 production from a good gut flora, and maybe a maximum of 2L of H2 delivered through hydrogen water. That's a significant effect just looking at the numbers, but given how difficult it is to obtain for you, I'd discourage pursuing this option.

There's also the question of whether the H2 is getting into the body in the right section of the gut -- in a well functioning gut flora, you're looking at large intestine H2 production. Getting excess H2 in the small intestine is likely safe, but can also be a major problem if you have a candida infection (the yeast can utilise H2). Another reason why I'd advise against this strategy.

> Might a fecal transplant from a family member be good? My mom is the healthiest but did have thyroid cancer and takes the replacement hormone medication, but she follows a good paleo diet.

This gets into point (b).

I have zero experience with doing fecal transplants. Personally, I will strongly recommend AGAINST doing this, because of many possible side effects, and because you haven't tried out the low-hanging-fruit solutions yet.

Those solutions include the recommendation to consume nuts and animal fibre, as well as the recommendation to try out Intermittent Fasting, which was tackled on the blog -- [http://jackkruse.com/intermittent-fasting-and-leptin/](http://jackkruse.com/intermittent-fasting-and-leptin/)

Limiting food stimulus to the gut upregulates FIAF and a bunch of other factors that induce generally positive gut flora changes. Search for the term 'quorum sensing' on the forums if you want more info.

I don't see any symptoms that would suggest to me that you will be destroyed by fasting, so I'd encourage giving it a shot, either by skipping dinner or skipping breakfast.

Which gets to the next point ...

> Also, is it possible to do the leptin reset if I have to rise before sunrise daily for school?

The leptin reset isn't always for everyone. It is mainly for those who are dramatically Leptin Resistant, and who have messed up circadian clocks.

To directly answer the question, I would suggest AGAINST eating breakfast before sunrise, because this sets the circadian clock way too early, suppresses cortisol and ghrelin while raising insulin, and can lead to altered fat-metabolism through the day.

But the first thing I will look at is to see if you have dramatically altered circadian function.

How is your sleep and wake cycles? If they are say 6am to 10pm where you are in Philly, and if you don't wake up feel groggy as all hell, and if you actually start feeling sleepy when you're trying to get to bed, then you may not strictly need the Leptin Reset.

I am looking at your symptoms, and they tell me that the electron sensor that is your Leptin Receptor is still working well enough, while your gut is the one that is broken.

Given that sort of condition, I personally think that you will get more benefits from skipping breakfast and getting some intermittent fasting done. I would personally ask you to eat only from 12pm until sunset, and maybe drink some black coffee or tea in the morning if you wish.

Again, we're looking at tradeoffs here, and I think the tradeoff of you skipping breakfast and improving the gut flora is worth it.

As usual, you have to test this out. That means a minimum of 2 weeks trying out this Intermittent Fasting protocol

\-----
**Dietary Carbs**

Carbs tend to be thought of as fast energy. Personally, I view periodic carb refeeds as a potential strategy to heal a bad gut, provided that candida is not in place.

This will require a much larger explanation, which you may be able to piece together if you read ALL the posts in the 'reverse electron flow' thread -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/reverse-electron-flow.12744/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/reverse-electron-flow.12744/)

For now, I think you should continue doing what you're do, try to limit too many carbs, and just focus on keeping the fat high.

\----
**Vegetables**

Personally, I am NOT a fan of eating vegetables for someone with a damaged gut flora, unless maybe it is summer time (more sulfated Vit-D).

Mushrooms should be fine, but then again, you need to test this.

Personally, I do best when I don't eat any vegetables at all (Inger would concur ). Greens especially used to destroy my gut flora.

Keep this in mind as another variable to test out. Again, you'll require at least 2 weeks of removing vegetables to determine if this is working for you.

\=====
**IMPLEMENTATION**

You could technically implement all the suggestions regarding diet at once, and see how things go after 2 weeks.

Or you could implement them one at a time and see how things go.

It will largely come down to your personality, which will determine which method will be most successfully implemented.

Me personally, I tend to go to extremes and do everything in one fell swoop

....

> [Penny said:](/goto/post?id=155253)

> He's 15 - the child is not going to eat fish head smoothies... you can find antibiotic free hormone free chicken - but really, for Matt, I would just recommend Great Lakes gelatin:
> [http://www.amazon.com/Great-Lakes-Unflavored-Gelatin-Kosher/dp/B0008D6WBA/ref=sr\_1\_2?ie=UTF8&qid=1423589057&sr=8-2&keywords=great lakes gelatin](http://www.amazon.com/Great-Lakes-Unflavored-Gelatin-Kosher/dp/B0008D6WBA/ref=sr_1_2?ie=UTF8&qid=1423589057&sr=8-2&keywords=great+lakes+gelatin)
>
> Fowl is fowl - so eat the broth and toss the chicken - he doesn't have to do it for life... optimal would be wild duck, but good luck finding that...

I am 15, but the restriction is not in the age. I actually have significantly less free time than the average US person, and I wake up earlier (5:50) and then have many hours of work after I get home around 3:30. If fish head smoothies would help me THAT much, I'd do them. It just isn't practical IMO.

Could anyone provide an explanation as to why fowl is foul? It might not be optimal regularly, but I can't help but think about the native americans consuming turkeys and what not. I would only eat pasture-raised chicken from a local farm, if at all. It does make sense in some ways, given that birds are not something we are exactly "able" to hunt very effectively.

On the gelatin front, I am taking great lakes collagen, basically the same but just processed differently; and it works better in smoothies.

Thanks for the help.

\- Matt
