I really appreciate all of the strategies. As I am currently working with the functional medicine doc I think I will do what she is suggesting for the nest 5 weeks- basically seafood based keto w/ vegetables, few berries, nuts/seeds, and occasional starch (like a banana as binder in coconut-egg pancakes), while taking the supplements- "Repairvite" (similar to jack's recommendations of deglyc... licorice root and a few other compounds), vitamin D, turmeric extract, digestive enzymes and HCl for stomach acid, "GI Synergy" (a bunch of things to feed good flora, heal, etc. and a probiotic w/ L. Acidophilus, S. Boulardii, and B. Coagulans. These products are primarily by a company calle dAPEX energetics- they seem to understand health pretty well. I plan on following this protocol and not restricting my diet at all from where I am, because it really gives me a peace of mind not to be continuously restricting, unless I really seem to need to cut nightshades, eggs, and nuts/seeds, and maybe vegetables in general per your recommendation.

I already "fast" daily- I eat breakfast upon waking around 6:30 (now 15 minutes before sunrise where I live, however my eyes are exposed to artificial light the moment I wake up. If the sun isn't up outside yet, should I wear my amber goggles even in the morning? Also, my sleep recently has not been spectacular, I've been waking up groggy. I will see how this improves with the doc's protocol, and I will use bits and pieces of everything on this forum, but I think I will only start to completely implement certain ideas after the doc's "plan" is through. I believe then I will be healed in the gut to a great extent, and then I will really be able to begin minimizing EMF risk, and optimizing beyond normal paleo (because that is kind of where this doc stands). I am already loading on seafood, drinking almost a gallon of spring water daily (I got my half gallon hydroflask in the mail today, it's HUGE!!!! it's supposed to be a beer growler....- best part is that it is _so good_ I can bring it into a sauna and ice inside will not melt- great semiconduction and structured H2O!!!!)

### Matty_M — 2015-02-11T02:51:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/post-155310>

> [yewwei.tan said:](/goto/post?id=155295)
>
> Nuts can be very Helpful in healing the gut. The mechanism here involves direct delivery of probiotic starch to the distal colon (large intestine), which upregulates natural butyrate production.
>
> Again, the only way to figure out if it is good for you is to test it out, and that means re-introducing nuts. The recommendations are to introduce as much variety as possible, but I'd opt for the lower Omega-6 containing nuts. Personally, I'd just start with:
>
> -   Macadamias
> -   Hazelnuts
> -   Almonds
>
> Eat maybe 10-12 pieces of each nut a day for a week, and see if symptoms improve.
>
> \----
>
> Then there is "Animal Fibre" -- [http://caloriesproper.com/animal-fibre](http://caloriesproper.com/animal-fibre)
>
> Jack talked about this here -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...ntial-through-hydride-ions.10343/#post-118768](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/do-raw-foods-improve-redox-potential-through-hydride-ions.10343/#post-118768)
>
> And I made some comments regarding this in the Molecular H2 thread -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...tochondrial-redox-compound.12674/#post-153724](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/molecular-hydrogen-h2-ultimate-mitochondrial-redox-compound.12674/#post-153724)
>
> Personally, my recommendation is to just get some tendons and seafood shells into your system. Start slow here.
>
> Recommended food sources:
>
> -   Cuts of beef that are full of tendons
> -   Prawn shells
>
> Prawn shells are easy enough, just pan fry the prawns and eat the whole thing . I'd say 3-4 whole prawns (about 100+g / 4oz) produces a therapeutic effect.
>
> For the beef, just buy a cut that would traditionally be used for slow-cooking or stewing, and then pan fry it like you would any other steak, cut it into decently large chunks, then chew minimally before swallowing. Cuts like Osso Bucco, Beef shins (usually called gravy beef), and oyster blade work well and taste decent. About 250g / 8oz of such meat is where I see a therapeutic effect.
>
> Some people will get nausea a couple hours after trying this. In general, that is a sign that your gut flora is adapting. I would view it as a positive clinical sign, unless the nausea is either really bad, or sustained for >4 hours.
>
> \----
>
> Then there is the delivery of molecular H2 by the gut microbiome to the rest of the system. bio-fractal-soul-self and Jack have been talking about this -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...-ultimate-mitochondrial-redox-compound.12674/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/molecular-hydrogen-h2-ultimate-mitochondrial-redox-compound.12674/)
>
> There are 2 ways to help deliver H2 to the body:
>
> (a) Drink H2 enriched water
> (b) Have a good gut flora
>
> Personally, I think option (a) is possibly effective, but is expensive as hell, and doesn't have a high reward-to-cost ratio.
>
> We're talking 14L of H2 production from a good gut flora, and maybe a maximum of 2L of H2 delivered through hydrogen water. That's a significant effect just looking at the numbers, but given how difficult it is to obtain for you, I'd discourage pursuing this option.
>
> There's also the question of whether the H2 is getting into the body in the right section of the gut -- in a well functioning gut flora, you're looking at large intestine H2 production. Getting excess H2 in the small intestine is likely safe, but can also be a major problem if you have a candida infection (the yeast can utilise H2). Another reason why I'd advise against this strategy.
>
>
>
> This gets into point (b).
>
> I have zero experience with doing fecal transplants. Personally, I will strongly recommend AGAINST doing this, because of many possible side effects, and because you haven't tried out the low-hanging-fruit solutions yet.
>
> Those solutions include the recommendation to consume nuts and animal fibre, as well as the recommendation to try out Intermittent Fasting, which was tackled on the blog -- [http://jackkruse.com/intermittent-fasting-and-leptin/](http://jackkruse.com/intermittent-fasting-and-leptin/)
>
> Limiting food stimulus to the gut upregulates FIAF and a bunch of other factors that induce generally positive gut flora changes. Search for the term 'quorum sensing' on the forums if you want more info.
>
> I don't see any symptoms that would suggest to me that you will be destroyed by fasting, so I'd encourage giving it a shot, either by skipping dinner or skipping breakfast.
>
> Which gets to the next point ...
>
>
>
>
> The leptin reset isn't always for everyone. It is mainly for those who are dramatically Leptin Resistant, and who have messed up circadian clocks.
>
> To directly answer the question, I would suggest AGAINST eating breakfast before sunrise, because this sets the circadian clock way too early, suppresses cortisol and ghrelin while raising insulin, and can lead to altered fat-metabolism through the day.
>
> But the first thing I will look at is to see if you have dramatically altered circadian function.
>
> How is your sleep and wake cycles? If they are say 6am to 10pm where you are in Philly, and if you don't wake up feel groggy as all hell, and if you actually start feeling sleepy when you're trying to get to bed, then you may not strictly need the Leptin Reset.
>
> I am looking at your symptoms, and they tell me that the electron sensor that is your Leptin Receptor is still working well enough, while your gut is the one that is broken.
>
> Given that sort of condition, I personally think that you will get more benefits from skipping breakfast and getting some intermittent fasting done. I would personally ask you to eat only from 12pm until sunset, and maybe drink some black coffee or tea in the morning if you wish.
>
> Again, we're looking at tradeoffs here, and I think the tradeoff of you skipping breakfast and improving the gut flora is worth it.
>
> As usual, you have to test this out. That means a minimum of 2 weeks trying out this Intermittent Fasting protocol
>
> \-----
> **Dietary Carbs**
>
> Carbs tend to be thought of as fast energy. Personally, I view periodic carb refeeds as a potential strategy to heal a bad gut, provided that candida is not in place.
>
> This will require a much larger explanation, which you may be able to piece together if you read ALL the posts in the 'reverse electron flow' thread -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/reverse-electron-flow.12744/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/reverse-electron-flow.12744/)
>
> For now, I think you should continue doing what you're do, try to limit too many carbs, and just focus on keeping the fat high.
>
> \----
> **Vegetables**
>
> Personally, I am NOT a fan of eating vegetables for someone with a damaged gut flora, unless maybe it is summer time (more sulfated Vit-D).
>
> Mushrooms should be fine, but then again, you need to test this.
>
> Personally, I do best when I don't eat any vegetables at all (Inger would concur ). Greens especially used to destroy my gut flora.
>
> Keep this in mind as another variable to test out. Again, you'll require at least 2 weeks of removing vegetables to determine if this is working for you.
>
> \=====
> **IMPLEMENTATION**
>
> You could technically implement all the suggestions regarding diet at once, and see how things go after 2 weeks.
>
> Or you could implement them one at a time and see how things go.
>
> It will largely come down to your personality, which will determine which method will be most successfully implemented.
>
> Me personally, I tend to go to extremes and do everything in one fell swoop
>
> ....

Given that I am able to get a much better meal if I do eat breakfast (although it's before sunrise by 45 min at winter solstice, 15 min. before now, and after sunrise during spring and summer) would it be possibly negative to keep that up and just continue to fast from 6:30am to about 4:00pm? Also, I just began face dunks today. I did 5-7 for around 20 seconds each. I will up this as tolerance improves, and do so daily.

Thanks again for the help. Youre like JK except not quite as occupied cutting heads open (physically, at least)

\- Matt

### sjoshua — 2015-02-11T02:52:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/post-155311>

> [Matty\_M said:](/goto/post?id=155305)
>
> Could anyone provide an explanation as to why fowl is foul? It might not be optimal regularly, but I can't help but think about the native americans consuming turkeys and what not. I would only eat pasture-raised chicken from a local farm, if at all. It does make sense in some ways, given that birds are not something we are exactly "able" to hunt very effectively.

Consider that:
\- the foul available (i.e. in your native american example) was all wild
\- today, 'we' have significantly altered the genetic expression of foul to maximize meat and growing time through selective breeding. IF you were to choose it, the local, pasture raised would be your best option, but consider that unless they are some heritage breed that was never 'tweaked', then they are still quite different from wild
\- today, 'we' have significantly altered the environment and lifestyle of foul - barns, warehouses, cages, feed... almost every aspect of their lives are NOT natural
\-today, there is a significantly different environment present on the surface of the planet due to industrialization and everything that came from it. the chickens live in the same nnEMF fields as us, and farms are great targets for cell towers to the uninformed farmer.

### yewwei.tan — 2015-02-11T03:05:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/post-155316>

OK. Given new context, circadian signalling seems to be off. Continue with the BAB

4pm to 6:30am fast is a good protocol

\----

Amber Goggles upon waking until sunrise is probably not going to hurt. Not sure how much it will help, but since there is no harm you should probably do it.

\----

Let us know how you fare over the weeks

My personal bias is always to fix environmental issues first, and use as natural a means of fixing the body (and gut) as possible. IMO, it is your bad EMF scenario that is causing the gut issues, and not the other way round.

That said, I'm a fan of the:

-   Licorice Root
-   Turmeric Extract (but make sure to add some black pepper too!)
-   digestive enzymes and HCI

I am not a fan of the:

-   Probiotics -- either it's pissing in the ocean, with very little effect given your environmental issues. Or it's harmful, leading to gut flora alterations that aren't suitable for your current state of health.

-   Vit D -- oral Vitamin D is discouraged by Dr Kruse because it is not sulfated.

    ItsTheWoo also points out that Vit D in general can suppress adaptive immunity -- [http://itsthewooo.blogspot.com/2015/02/vitamin-d3-seasonal-shifts-are-adaptive.html](http://itsthewooo.blogspot.com/2015/02/vitamin-d3-seasonal-shifts-are-adaptive.html) . Whether or not this will help or hurt for your particular autoimmune condition is going to be very hard to say. Too many factors that can lead to massive shifts in TH1 and TH2 dominance and T-cell dysregulation.

    Oral absorption an incorporation to tissue receptor sites is also going to be compromised with an altered gut. The conventional methodology is to simply increase the dose of Vitamin D. Again, side effects are unknown, though I think in the short 5-week period that you're going to try this out, it wouldn't have massive downside.

    Again, I'm biased to say not to take this, and get a natural Vit D stimulus via UV light on the skin if possible.

I would add:

-   Vit K2. Daily: 200mcg MK7, anywhere from 10 to 25mg MK4
-   Trans-Resveratrol: at least 500mg daily. No max dose

\----

Good luck
.....

### Penny — 2015-02-11T03:05:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/post-155317>

I think the problem with chicken is it's high in omega 6 and you want to increase omega 3... plus, they are disgusting...

I like what Yew said - naturally, I just take chitosan in a pill before bed - also comes with chromium for that EMF glucose up regulation problem... I actually have some around because I was under the impression it blew off the cell walls from clostridia so I used to give it out with an herbal "bug bomb" - but I think some people add it to their diet to lose weight - maybe it blows off the cell walls of the bacteria that causes some people to be fat, or maybe it's just dietary fiber which slows transit time of glucose so maybe it's helpful for diabetes...

If you're already taking the collagen, you don't need to make bone broths... we did the repairvite thing and 2 others - actually, now that I think about it, my kid wouldn't drink any of it and there went another $200.00...

It sounds like you're getting there considering where you live - ask your functional med guy about zinc with carnosine - actually, I think Repairvite already has it -

Dr. K had a great resistant starch throw down where he took labs every week - I think the result of that was almonds ruled the day - curious, did you have the parasite when you were binging on the GAPS diet or had they killed it yet?

Yup, I used to get these suckers when I was in Melbourne . Asian butcher charges like $4/kg for this.

I would be really slack with preparation. Boil until the outside gets slightly chewy (maybe around 15-20mins), and then just chop into small pieces (need a sharp knife!), and then marinate in whatever sauce that tasted good (any vinaigrette works well), pop them in the fridge, and then literally eat it like candy  (chew a little while and then swallow)

### JanSz — 2015-02-11T21:12:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/post-155383>

> [Matty\_M said:](/goto/post?id=155326)
>
> this is in response to JanSz about the Asian market

in that store you can buy live seafood.
They will kill it and prepare for you.
Do not think they will flash freeze.
If it is close enough to you, just go there 2-3x/week

other than meat, think fresh roe and fish bones, often they have it,
often people buy part of the fish, usually just filets, so head, bones and roe you can buy if available.

Think of grilled salmon head.
Meat have extra fat, more than in filets. Eye balls. After you finish eating, put the rest in the pot, get some broth.
\=========================================

There is also Super^2 Asian supermarket,
[http://hmart.com/](http://hmart.com/)

### Penny — 2015-02-11T21:14:44+0200

Hi Matty

I don't know if I've already said this, but if you were a vegetarian, you would be low in zinc - which is required to make stomach acid - stomach acid is required to absorb the zinc - I think oysters would be a good move for you because they contain lots of zinc and if you take enzymes/betaine HCL with the oysters, eventually you will have enough stomach acid to digest all the protein you are eating - raw oysters would be the perfect food for you... pumpkin seeds also contain some zinc and are effective against parasites also...

The lack of zinc will also make you have eating disorders:
[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/2600063](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/2600063)

which might be why you went overboard on the GAPS diet?

I thought most seafood is flash frozen on the boat - but here in Ventura we have a boat that comes in once a month and they kill the fish right on the dock - I think they keep them in a hold on the boat? It's so good -

### Matty_M — 2015-02-17T04:55:39+0200

However, I really want to get a great education (this public school is called Harriton High School, its really good and offers the International Baccalaureate program, I would look it up real quick if you aren't familiar).

I really think that, wanting my future to involve fixing the de-evolution and possibly self-extinction that we are beginning. I feel that having a very credible education, in turn getting into a good university, would be the best path I could take, so I should protect myself in school.

Honestly, with 1400 kids and 1400 iphones and laptops and about 325 rooms, every single one with a wifi router, I would'nt be surprised if my environment is worse than Dr. Jack's workplace. Not to mention I have far less control over my home env't..................

I am beginning to do anything and everything I can to lower my risk.

1) getting good sleep in the lowest radiation place possible in both parents' houses

2) eating ketogenic seafood epi-paleo (avoiding dairy besides ghee and nuts/seeds besides coconut and macadmias because they both aggravate my leaky gut- including some berries and veggies for variety)

3) taking variety of compounds to help heal gut while working to restore flora with functional medicine doc (who is on ketogenic paleo level- I'm also looking into fecal transplant from family member in future- although probably altered by EMF still probably better than my current, low-diversity flora that I just use an antibiotic to kill a strong parasite) WOULD SOMEONE WHO IS ON SYNTHROID FOR THYROID HORMONES BE ABLE TO DONATE STOOL OR NOT? This is my mom and she is otherwise very very healthy for a 50 year old, and follows a mostly "paleo" template. Must include I was a C-section and ate crap for a long period of my life while a vegetarian...........

4) keeping phone on airplane mode almost always and having a grounding cord and wired keyboard and mouse for computer for distance (while at home- not enough space for distance at school....)

5) beginning CT intro- got up to 1:30 on face dunks after a 5-7x a day for a few days. Will continue this and begin to do compression shirt and ice bag. Also trying to stick to only coldest showers (tap water in PA very very cold in winter). I also use a steam sauan 2-4x a week because it feels great.

6) going outside when possible (maybe hour/day with how cold it is and I'm not cold adapted yet.....); getting grounded when possible- buying grounding leather soled mocassins soon- are there any snowboots I could wear in heavy snow that might be able to ground me?

7) What I WANT to do is get EMF protective light jacket and hat lining to protect vital organs and brain, maybe even some fabric for a scarf. Just need to know if this could IN ANY WAY MAKE THINGS WORSE- either by conducting something or another, or trapping in radiation coming from below me when I'm sitting (unless I get silverell boxers which are really expensive)???? I really dont understand how EMFs work very well, but I am trying to start reading the blogs regularly. It is hard with all the school and homework every day.

I'd really appreciate any input.

Thanks,

Funny story. I took measurements in school, and either something is horribly wrong, or it is not nearly as bad as I thought. No kidding, the measurements were better than both of my houses. It was extremely shocking. Save the wifi, computers, and cell phones, and electrical wiring, the school is on a HUGE property in a very wealthy area with large properties. Cell reception is actually pretty bad, now that I think of it, and LTE/3G/etc. are horrible. It just might be that it's fairly remote location (compared to my mom's and dad's houses) makes it MUCH less significantly affected by exterior forces, and the interior forces just aren't all that significant. I think people do realize that cell towers and lots of wiring could negatively affect health, but couldn't possibly consider the blasphemy of our technology being a detriment.............

I have much more measuring to do. But, even while sitting in a library with probably 50 laptops on and in use, it was one sixth of where I sit now at my dad's house (.5 mG vs. 3 Mg)

Staying at my dad's for the past few months has been HUGE in terms of helping me mentally to be able to focus and work on my relationship with family members. Things were really just becoming TOO much with how deeply preoccupied I was and sort of still am with the health situation. As described in my first post, it really took a toll on my mentality and everything and I just don't really get along too great with my family because I feel as though everyone has resentment against me for "thinking I'm perfect/being paleo/all the other crap that someone might be jealous or angry over with a brother/son. It really sucks, although I do see a good therapist and am continuing to improve it. Even though most hours of the school evenings my dad isn't home, I definitely still get to be with him far more often, and I am saved the stress of my other house 4 of 7 days a week

(seriously, my mom is very immature/has a TON of her own problems that she has trouble dealing with that really causes problems in our relationship because all of her constant stresses really affect me negatively, therapist used the term "enmeshment" and so I almost somehow end up in a fight with her at least daily. We have an overall good relationship, but I just feel like she doesn't act like a mother, more like a constantly complaining sister, to be completely honest. I love her, but the stress she causes me somewhat regularly noticeably affects my health. Until she is able to fix her problems involving health, relationships, family issues, divorce, finances......the list goes on........, our relationship may or not be good because she ALWAYS burdens everyone else and I am the LAST person who needs another burden. Not truly, but I feel that way. I have been working with the therapist on my own resiliency, because there is absolutely NO WAY that all these fights are my fault. I ALWAYS blame myself, but the more I look at it from a real perspective, the more I realize that a parent with less burden (or just better control) should not even be engaging in some kind of fight. She always raises her voice, etc. etc. etc. I REALLY could start a whole thread on this relationship but I won't unless it seems necessary. Any advice at ALL would be spectacular. Please, as I am sure all of you are sons/daughters, parents, and siblings.)

Anyway, although I love staying with my dad and originally thought it would be a good thing for the rest of high school- should I do whatever I can to stay at my mom's instead? I get almost a 0 mG reading at that house in my room, whereas my dad's is no lower than 2.5 mG ANYWHERE. I sleep on couch where it's lowest. He lives in row homes a bit closer to the city, so it makes sense that it is a bit higher. Or would staying here for my mental health be more important for the time being. Simply based on the fact that I can't get a reading under 2.5, unless I buy an EMF-proof disposable tent for $15 (they actually have them on lessemf.co, and it works well, I don't see myself spending the rest of high school at my dad's. The BIGGEST issue is that HE loves my company so much that I would really hate to disappoint him, although I will try NOT to let the pathos affect the logos of my health, and I will move out ASAP if the 3.5 mG vs. .5 mG where I sleep (dad's vs. mom's, respectively) would have a significant effect on my health. I already moved to the couch from my bed where the meter revealed I was sleeping with my head at 5 mG!!!!!!!!!!! No wonder I was sleeping like CRAP!!!!

I feel that the inevitable answer is to slowly begin moving out of my dad's. The real question is, would a few more months while working with the therapist and finishing the school year be more detrimental than the stress, anxiety, fighting, tension, and anger that is inevitable at my mom's (I've tried SO many times to just completely ignore, but somehow a fight will always happen. My mom is a great woman and really cares about me and I love her, but the way that she handles her many issues DIRECTLY HURTS ME and I could definitely use some advice on that too.

Also, I am committing to beginning to read 1 blog post per day. That is all I have time for, but it is pretty solid. I am on the path to begin my book. My first thought for a title is "Sense Zero" in reference to our primal sense, our innate ability to time using EMF, light & dark, and carbs, and all the other stuff. I would likely not end up releasing it until after I graduate, but I've no problem with that. Although I'm a highschooler, if I commit to this, and really continue to progress my (basic) understanding of the core concepts of physics in school, while reading tons of high level material, all of jack's book, blogs, webinars, all of the works he recommends of Gilbert Ling, Robert O. Becker, etc., and DEFINITELY a consultation with the man himself, I think I could write a book that could really work the masses while proving extremely insightful, credible, and eye-opening to the medicine, agriculture, government, and science communities, sparking additional research, at least. If anyone has constructive criticism, I'd LOVE it, however anything regarding my age, ability, or potential would be better kept to yourself. A kid in my school a grade younger died this weekend in a tragic accident, and I thought "If I died soon, what would I have wanted to do?", and this book type thing was way above everything, even traveling the world. So I am going to do it, and make it DAMN good. I will NOT do anything without thorough understanding of my claims, what I'm getting at, the references I am using, and how it all ties directly back to optimal health and life, especially disease prevention, treatment, and cures. The book title (although far from definite) is unofficially copyrighted here.

Thanks a lot, it is great to get out my thoughts........

\- Matt

### yewwei.tan — 2015-02-19T01:31:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/post-155999>

Those are referring to ELF EMF readings right?

You'll need to state the spectrum that your meter measures. I have 2 meters for example, one that measures <1000Hz (we call this ELF, or extreme low frequency), and one that measures in the >2MHz range.

The ELF meter would be picking up stuff like the EMF given off from electric equipment -- wires, refrigerators, CPU processors, etc ....

WiFi transmits above 2.4GHz, and will not be picked up by an ELF meter.

Then there's the question of destructive interference of waves. There are some regions that where 2 WiFi signals can interact and cancel each other out. Putting your meter there may give you a low reading.

Remember also to take a complete 3D scan of EMF readings, here is an example of how my body absorbs nn-EMF from a router, giving a low reading to the meter -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...ents-are-now-welcome.12628/page-5#post-155308](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/tensegrity-14-comments-are-now-welcome.12628/page-5#post-155308)

>

>
> \- Matt

Dr Kruse will provide you with lots of (1). As for (2), he can't possibly know personal contexts and emergent reactions to various interventions. Experiments need to devised bearing mechanistic effects in mind, and then based on personal resource constraints and disease contexts

Yup, that meter covers WiFi  -- [http://www.trifield.com/content/trifield-meter/](http://www.trifield.com/content/trifield-meter/)

Magnetic Frequency range is: **40 Hz – 100 KHz**
Electric Frequency range is: **50 MHz – 3000 MHz (3 GHz)**

Putting it to the "Magnetic (0-3)" setting should detect any fields coming off of electric wires, etc .... giving you a reading in Gauss (pray that you have have G readings )

Putting it to the "Radio/Microwave" setting allows you to power WiFi power output in MilliWatts per square centimeter (mW/cm2). You need to go sweep your school space using that setting and report back the power values. Pray that you get values under the 1mW/cm2 range that your meter supports ..... you want to see it below 0.1mW/cm2

### Matty_M — 2015-02-21T06:02:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/post-156198>

My Tri-Meter does not show any radio frequencies up againsy my phone or computer. The only things that produce a reading are the microwave at my dad's and the wifi routers.

I have officially begun the CT easy start. I've been doing head dunks for nearly 2 weeks and I can hold them on average for a minute and up to two minutes. I use fresh cold tapwater (which is very cold considering it is around 5° in Pennsylvania right now), and then I load in a layer of ice from the freezer. I got an infrared thermometer and the water does a solid job of getting my skin tempterature down to where it needs to be.

I got a compression shirt in the mail today and will begin the ice block on chest tomorrow. Once up to 60 minutes with no compression shirt, then I will hit the tub.

can I assume that, with following a ketogenic epi-Paleo diet (Also keeping out dairy, omega six Rich nuts, And possibly eliminating nightshades and/or eggs for a leaky gut), I will be able to adapt to the cold pretty quickly? The one place where I could do better is getting grounded more, but it is just impractical while not cold adapted because it is absolutely freezing here, temperatures hovering around 0°.

does anyone have any advice regarding food and diet in relationship to a stuffy nose that is still lightly persisting? What should be the first moves that I take in addition to a ketogenic epi-Paleo diet? I am taking a bunch of supplements with the functional medicine doc, very similar to the supplements that Dr. kruse recommends. I will have a look at the leaky gut prescription and make alterations based on that. Beyond that is there anything that seems like a no-brainer that I might have missed, for my situation?

how might I get grounded more?

how many grams of carbs am I okay to have? I know they should not be anywhere near a staple of calories in my diet given the season, but it very much helps to have the variety. at this point, I am consuming a decent amount of vegetables that are very low in carbs and maybe one serving per day of low sugar berries.

what can I do to try to make sure cold adaptation will be faster? I'd love to be able to burn fat as free heat before the end of this winter so that it is not completely miserable, and to show my friends and family that there is in fact merit to all of the stuff that I am doing.

I feel as though I look extremely good, and I am getting stronger while not exercising, so things are going pretty well.

what do you guys think I should be doing in terms of exercise? Given the fact that I was getting headaches from anything more intense basically than walking, I am not doing any weight training or sprinting like I would be normally. how should I proceed with this? Should I wait until I get certain lab results like an adrenal test which is scheduled for three weeks from now, or should I slowly ease into it based on tolerance, because I really haven't tried it in two months.

lastly, I bet a friend back in the fall that I would be able to break a five-minute mile when I am healthy. I am on track to that point, and assume that it will take at least a few more months to be in a good shape for that kind of run. is there anything I should watch for that would completely disqualify me from being sensible to run a mile that quickly, in addition to the many individual 400 m that I will run to train? back in eighth grade while I was in poor health, not quite as poor as nice grade, but not great either, I ran a 517 with a lot of training and very good competition. Based on my new circumstances I was willing to wager with myself that I could undoubtedly break five minutes at this point (I mean, where I will be when totally healthy). Would a lot of CT maybe be enough to give me the shape to be able to do this run,or would a good bit of training the necessary (I fairly certain that the training would be necessary)? Just looking for opinions, maybe based on experience.

Thanks,

– Matt

You may find a picture of yourself! Spend the next 15 -18 months becoming a master of the 50 minute cold tub! You are on the road to success. Stay on that road.

Definitely a sad truth that I should NOT run the mile or train for it.... The problem is that almost 6 months ago I had NO idea how long it might take to get healthy so I bet $50 on it, because I assumed I would be in rippin' shape. I've only just begun the healing anyway. The kid is a good friend and I have a feeling he will let me off because it's not me who doesn't want to, but it's my physical situation- or I will up the ante for a better time next summer.

Given my physical ability and my history, having been a pretty fast runner when I was younger, and more importantly, the optimal state of health that I am going for (good sleep, lots of grounding, epi-paleo, cold adaptation, lots of cold spring water, avoidance of EMFs to extent possible) do you think that next summer I might be able to hit a 4:45 or even 4:30 with significantly less training than a "normal" person? I trained with long-distance, intervals, sprints, hills, etc., for about 40 minutes 5 days a week for about 3 months, with infrequent training the rest of the year, and reached 5:17 (with a good competitor) in the spring of 8th grade (2013).

Also, what kind of general **exercise** do you think I should be doing, and when? I currently try to walk and bike lightly as often as possible, although the near zero temperatures here are a big hindrance to that, especially while not cold adapted (I do try to go out in little clothing as often as possible, but it seems that the pain-to benefit ratio is inferior to that of a cold tub (which I am nearing; I am beginning today the ice bag on chest w/compression shirt after having done face dunks for two weeks). I think that something along the lines of Mark Sisson's Primal Blueprint Fitness would be great for me. Also, when I'm at a bit healthier state I will bike to school daily to get more sunlight, fresh air, and movement, and I also attempt to stand rather than sit in the classes where possible. Sadly, especially going into junior year, unless I get accepted to go onto an exchange for next year, I will have absurd amounts of work, most of which I'll have to complete to learn a lot and get into a good college (I do get straight A's, though, so I'm not slacking).

It is so unbelievably cold where I live right now (oustide of Philly, PA) and I just want to be able to go outdoors and enjoy it and feel warm. If I do everything I'm trying to do, plus 5 to 7 face dunks averaging 1 minute each, and now add in the ice bag on the chest, then transition to the tub, how long could it _possibly_ be for me to **cold adapt?**\- just a general range. Could it be shorter due to the fact that I have only sustained 15 years of genetic discordance? Given my health situation, and possible adrenal issues (which I am going to test in a few weeks with functional doc), I know it could take a bit longer. I know the _more whole O3_ the better, and the _less EMF and blue light_ the better, and _more sleep_, and _less stress_, _more meditation_, _more earthing_ (although it's almost too cold...), and obviously _more cold water_, and _more cold exposure_. **What might I be missing** that could speed up the adaptation? Should I drink coffee to stimulate the epinephrines and whatnot (I really don't even know what those are), and take bitter melon extract? (I don't think I have much WAT, though it could be around organs or something)

Last, beyond ketogenic epi-paleo, should I take out **nightshades** while healing due to probably having a leaky gut (I have a stuffy nose most of the time)? I have already removed nuts/seeds (except coconut and macadamia due to low anti-nutrient and O6 levels), as well as dairy (besides ghee)- because I have noticed most nuts/seeds, especially chocolate, give me congestion, and cheese (even raw/grass-fed) definitely does, although I don't notice it quite as much with yogurt, I do a bit with heavy cream, and not really with butter, but I think it is best to only have the pure fat at this point, as even butter contains a noteworthy amount of milk solids. Also, how about **eggs** and **ghee**?

Thanks for the help,

\- Matt

It just might be that it's fairly remote location (compared to my mom's and dad's houses) makes it MUCH less significantly affected by exterior forces, and the interior forces just aren't all that significant. We have an overall good relationship, but I just feel like she doesn't act like a mother, more like a constantly complaining sister, to be completely honest. I love her, but the stress she causes me somewhat regularly noticeably affects my health. Until she is able to fix her problems involving health, relationships, family issues, divorce, finances......the list goes on........, our relationship may or not be good because she ALWAYS burdens everyone else and I am the LAST person who needs another burden. Not truly, but I feel that way. I have been working with the therapist on my own resiliency, because there is absolutely NO WAY that all these fights are my fault. I ALWAYS blame myself, but the more I look at it from a real perspective, the more I realize that a parent with less burden (or just better control) should not even be engaging in some kind of fight. Please, as I am sure all of you are sons/daughters, parents, and siblings.)
>
> Anyway, although I love staying with my dad and originally thought it would be a good thing for the rest of high school- should I do whatever I can to stay at my mom's instead? I get almost a 0 mG reading at that house in my room, whereas my dad's is no lower than 2.5 mG ANYWHERE. I sleep on couch where it's lowest. He lives in row homes a bit closer to the city, so it makes sense that it is a bit higher. Or would staying here for my mental health be more important for the time being. Simply based on the fact that I can't get a reading under 2.5, unless I buy an EMF-proof disposable tent for $15 (they actually have them on lessemf.co, and it works well, I don't see myself spending the rest of high school at my dad's. The BIGGEST issue is that HE loves my company so much that I would really hate to disappoint him, although I will try NOT to let the pathos affect the logos of my health, and I will move out ASAP if the 3.5 mG vs. .5 mG where I sleep (dad's vs. mom's, respectively) would have a significant effect on my health. I already moved to the couch from my bed where the meter revealed I was sleeping with my head at 5 mG!!!!!!!!!!! No wonder I was sleeping like CRAP!!!!
>
> I feel that the inevitable answer is to slowly begin moving out of my dad's. The real question is, would a few more months while working with the therapist and finishing the school year be more detrimental than the stress, anxiety, fighting, tension, and anger that is inevitable at my mom's (I've tried SO many times to just completely ignore, but somehow a fight will always happen. My mom is a great woman and really cares about me and I love her, but the way that she handles her many issues DIRECTLY HURTS ME and I could definitely use some advice on that too.
>
> Also, I am committing to beginning to read 1 blog post per day. That is all I have time for, but it is pretty solid. I am on the path to begin my book. My first thought for a title is "Sense Zero" in reference to our primal sense, our innate ability to time using EMF, light & dark, and carbs, and all the other stuff. I would likely not end up releasing it until after I graduate, but I've no problem with that. Although I'm a highschooler, if I commit to this, and really continue to progress my (basic) understanding of the core concepts of physics in school, while reading tons of high level material, all of jack's book, blogs, webinars, all of the works he recommends of Gilbert Ling, Robert O. Becker, etc., and DEFINITELY a consultation with the man himself, I think I could write a book that could really work the masses while proving extremely insightful, credible, and eye-opening to the medicine, agriculture, government, and science communities, sparking additional research, at least. If anyone has constructive criticism, I'd LOVE it, however anything regarding my age, ability, or potential would be better kept to yourself. A kid in my school a grade younger died this weekend in a tragic accident, and I thought "If I died soon, what would I have wanted to do?", and this book type thing was way above everything, even traveling the world. So I am going to do it, and make it DAMN good. I will NOT do anything without thorough understanding of my claims, what I'm getting at, the references I am using, and how it all ties directly back to optimal health and life, especially disease prevention, treatment, and cures. ### shah78 — 2015-02-21T22:12:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/post-156240>

IMHO the cold tub (40-50 degrees)is a hell of a lot easier than walking around Pennsylvania during winter in shorts and a t shirt. Hell ,I don't even like walking around South Florida some days in shorts and a t-shirt. I put on a pair of sweats. You don't have to walk around outside in shorts and a t shirt either. Start with a normal winter overcoat/sweater/shirt/tanktop outfit for your walks and then SLOWLY start going out with less clothing. You'll get into the tub soon enough. ....As for running a 4:45 mile. Wow! Your don't restrict your goals do you.  A FEW 100's, 400's a 800's /week ,right after you get out of your cold tub should do it. You may not make it down to 4:45, but you will blow your mind and everyone around you if can get even close by doing "almost no training". (No "normal" person considers cold therapy "training") Google: Wim Hoff. He runs marathons with almost no conventional training, and he even eats garbage!

Always, diet is hardest, probably because I "think" I need carbs. I always am seafood based w/good fat and veggies, but the questions (more like cravings) always come up with what I shouldnt eat- dairy (definitely shouldnt have the protein), chocolate and coffee (probably not either because I fit the bill for adrenal fatigue pretty well), nuts high in O6 (basically nuts besides coconut & macadamias), carbs (definitely should be ketotic for cold adapting), and nighshades and eggs. I don't know why. I will go the whole week avoiding chocolate and O6 nuts, and end up eating a bunch (usually at a friends), and then regretting it. Any thoughts on why I'm obsessing over this specifically a bit, why I feel like I can't stick to things I want to, and what I _should_ be doing to get where I need to go.

Maybe I will do the Leptin Rx/Leaky Gut Rx/Adrenal Rx soon.

Thanks for all the help. I will especially continue with the cold regardless of everything else.

\- Matt

I always am seafood based w/good fat and veggies, but the questions (more like cravings) always come up with what I shouldnt eat- dairy (definitely shouldnt have the protein), chocolate and coffee (probably not either because I fit the bill for adrenal fatigue pretty well), nuts high in O6 (basically nuts besides coconut & macadamias), carbs (definitely should be ketotic for cold adapting), and nighshades and eggs. I don't know why. I will go the whole week avoiding chocolate and O6 nuts, and end up eating a bunch (usually at a friends), and then regretting it. Any thoughts on why I'm obsessing over this specifically a bit, why I feel like I can't stick to things I want to, and what I _should_ be doing to get where I need to go.
>
> Maybe I will do the Leptin Rx/Leaky Gut Rx/Adrenal Rx soon.
>
> Thanks for all the help. I will especially continue with the cold regardless of everything else.
>
> \- Matt

Matty, have you stuffy nose 24/7 ? If not, try to figure out when...... you could get some clues why it happens. I get stuffy nose too. From pasteurized dairy.... etc. So I do not eat it.

If you cant avoid nuts.... I have realized the quality of nuts plays a huge role. I mean huge. If I get fresh, raw, organic or wild nuts, they suit me way better than regular nuts from the supermarket. I never eat those... except of some Brazil nuts here and there - they are always wild. Rancid nuts are real bad... and they almost always are. So for the beginning you could try to really watch the quality and freshness of your nuts? And do the one that does not get so easily rancid. Walnuts get very easily rancid BTW.. Nuts are always better bought in shell. They keep fresher that way. So fresh organic raw nuts in their shell would be the safest option. That is what I prefer too. That way you might be able to have some nuts and they would not hurt you..... maybe.

For chocolate you could try to make your own raw coconut crack, cause it is better than chocolate with sugar in it or heated chocolate. At least that is my own N=1........
Raw cacao powder does not give me as many symptoms as heated, like smelly armpits etc. and also I can avoid the sugar. Regular sugar is no good for me at all! Then I rather do a tiny bit of raw wild honey (bees that are fed no sugar in winter). I just use a tiny tiny bit, like 1/3 teaspoon. You can use stevia too (I do not like) or just go without any sweetener at all.

Eggs also are big difference.. if they are great quality or not. Maybe you can tolerate pastured eggs just fine? Try them raw? Raw eggs are pretty amazingly healthy

It could be, that there is something in nuts and eggs that your body wants and that is why you feel like eating them?
If you do all the above and still get bad symptoms from the stuff I would say, you just need to eat more seafood and meat and fat before you go into places with temptations  If you are really stuffed with good foods you will less likely eat anything bad for you - it just want fit into your belly

I do eat almost all organic veggies (will check for the nightshades), I eat pastured eggs, but the nuts are usually just normal, although I do soak overnight and dehydrate to remove most toxins from the skin, and then I usually roast them- could this be enough to make regular nuts OK?

I will probably just continue to eat all of the questionable things in moderation- just modifying based on which of Jack's Rxs I am doing. Right now, I am doing the **cold easy start** stuff and I want to be able to adapt quickly, so, **high seafood as always**, **low carb** (although probably 1-2 sweet potatoes and/or coconut flour pancakes on the weekend with lots of fat- or not if this could possibly slow cold adaptation a bit- and primarily just vegetables and some low carb berries for variety in general). Also, I definitely should stay pretty **low on high O6 nuts** (and other sources- though I don't eat vegetable oils....).

I have a feeling that I am somewhat leptin resistant based on my cravings and how I will eat a huge meal and just be able to keep eating and it takes a good while for satiety to kick in. Therefore, I think I will do the **Leptin Rx** at some point. I also think that I will need to do the **Adrenal Rx**, but I would probably wait until after the salivary hormone test with my functional doc. Also, the **leaky gut Rx** seems important for me to do, especially as I have a **stuffy nose**, use to have **bad allergies**, **don't fully absorb food** (yes, there's stuff in my stool...), etc. Should I do the Leptin Rx and Leaky Gut Rx at same time, while working with cold, or is there one that would be better to do first? My instinct says leptin Rx to correct cellular communications so a leaky gut will never come back, however, I'd like to do them together if it would work fine. If I do do the Leptin Rx, I have a feeling I will start getting a sour taste in my mouth and it will become dry because I still don't think my stomach acid function is on point and when I eat a lot of protein at once I notice this, along with other GERD symptoms that are more mild.

Given my circumstances, does it seem I should eliminate **nightshades** for sure- I can't tell if they cause a stuffy nose. To answer your question, the stuffy nose is almost existent 24/7 to some degree. It goes away a little when I strictly avoid coffee, cocoa, and O6 nuts/seeds, as well as all dairy sugar and protein, although I don't know what specifically is giving that effect. I KNOW that **casein** (especially from conventional dairy, but also from grass-fed raw) causes me stuffy noses, and therefore I do not play with **whey** either. **Lactose** is best kept out for me as well, as I am working on gut flora correction currently. I will definitely reintroduce good dairy when (if) I tolerate it, but I almost know I should be able to, if I heal my gut and body fully. I do eat some pure **ghee**. As for **nuts/seeeds** (that are higher in O6- almonds, pine nuts, pecans), how many would possibly cause a problem in terms of my O6/O3 ratio? I feel that these are better kept to a minimum. If I have noticed chocolate and coffee to give me stuffy noses, I should definitely avoid them, correct?- the only problem is that I am not certain if they are actually what does it. For now, I think I will probably just keep out dairy (besides ghee) and eat the others in moderation, while maintaining high-fat & seafood, epi-paleo.

The biggest question is- which prescriptions, and what order?

Any insight is great. Thanks very much for your help already.

\- Matt

Inger and Penny, I definitely agree. It's amazing what people consider food today! I'm seeing it more and more as little bundles of sunlight that are formed in specific ways that our body expects to consume at certain times (seasons), and not quite so much for pleasure, although we are lucky that we get to enjoy such good meals, and we get to make such good meals with good foods, here on the optimal side of things. I just had the most delicious piece of Atlantic pollock, seared in a little bit of coconut oil, topped only with capers, garlic, lemon, salt, and pepper. It melted in my mouth and tasted truly great.

Once I've got my gut healed, and my hormones and circadian signaling working properly, I think things will taste even better as there won't be the constant emptiness I feel that only carbs fill- and they don't even do such a great job of it (I think it will take a good Leptin Reset to correct, possibly alongside a Leaky Gut Rx, although I am not sure of this).

I have really been dallying with the bone broths because I can't get grass-fed bones at the current time (online costs absurd amounts to ship and many big sites are sold out, locally there is no one within easy range that I have found- not to mention I don't have much time being a student)- and I don't know if grain-fed bones are better than none at all. Do you guys have an opinion on this?

I do have a large spine of an 18 lb mahi mahi, and I been taking way to much time to get my dad to help me break it to use for broth. It is probably 1.5-2 feet long and 2 inches in diameter- so it will take a big mallet of some kind of device that could cut through a marble countertop- although he is a contractor so I shouldn't be taking so long to execute it...but I also have some shrimp shells to throw in, and I will add a bunch of spices and sea vegetables. If only my crockpot was bigger. Maybe I will, instead, do it on the stove in this huge pot we have. YUM! I never would have thought of that if I wasn't rambling on here.

I'm like the manager of a baseball team in the first inning who has almost got the other team's strategy completely down to a science. Once I jump into the second inning, it might be smooth sailing, although I'll always have to be ready for a few change-ups.

thanks a ton,

### Matty_M — 2015-02-27T02:56:55+0200

Hi Penny!

From what I've read-

Tigernuts are much higher in resistant starch than most nuts, much lower in O6, and technically are a tuber- although they're very low in absorbable carbs.

I think macadamias are almost devoid of O6, but I don't know if they are high in other inflammatory compounds, however, based on my experience, I think I could eat a ton of them with no problem, while the same does _not_ go for pecans, walnuts, almonds, pine nuts, etc., even if I soak them overnight and dehydrate them at a low temp (110 degress F).

Lastly, I think coconuts are also lower in certain inflammatory properties than most nuts, and also bring the immense benefit of MCTs and other stuff I do not know about. I currently have a half can of coconut milk (which is mostly fat) in a smoothie each morning and I think it does me nothing but good.

I am not very sure of all this stuff, so what do you think about them for my n=1, as I am looking at a Leptin Reset, and trying to heal my gut and build a good flora.

Thanks!

\- matt

### Penny — 2015-02-27T22:34:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/post-156828>

Dr. K had a great webinar on resistant starches and in the end he came to the conclusion that almonds were a great thing for the gut flora - however, you are a recovering vegetarian and so - as dull as you may find it - I would skip the 06's and concentrate on the o3's - believe it or not, IMHO, going out into nature and getting dirty - like when I play in the garden - is one of the finest things you can do for your gut flora - also, paying attention to the blue light from screens and curing GERD would be high on the list of of helping your gut flora - personally, I eat a lot of raw greens - and nuts and coconut meat and oil - if you read Tensegrity 14:

Bacteria make up the microbiome exclusively and their cell membranes are radically different than our own. They are able to release large amounts of light frequencies in the form of biophotons. The only part of biology that studies light is the biophysics of plants and marine bioluminescence with respect to photosynthesis. Because of this, I expect that the gastroenterology literature will not look or discover truly how the gut microbiome is capable of sculpting our epigenome with light through its interaction with our eukaryotic cell membranes of enterocytes. Today, on the clinical side of medicine, we are witnessing the emergence of more virulent strains of enteric pathogens, and are just beginning to explore the use of probiotics in the treatment of medical disorders. We remain in the dark how these probiotics can alter _light frequencies_ we use for communication between the bacterial and eukaryotic kingdom. What links both kingdoms is our mitochondria. Mitochondria were evolved from the bacterial kingdom and even today in humans they carry their own 13 genes that are completely separate from the 46 chromosomes in our eukaryotic nucleus. Everyday we are being made increasingly aware of the presence of gut pathogens through the widespread outbreaks of foodborne illnesses and autoimmune conditions. In short, this is the era of the gut, but we will not fully understand our microbiome until we understand how it uses protons and how it uses light frequencies to function! The recent developments in the science of epigenetics shows us that genes do not have discrete jobs at all, as we have believed. Genes have the capability to make lots of different proteins by a “slick cutting and pasting” method to creates diversity that is tied to redox chemistry of the extracellular matrix. Your gut lumen is an extension of the microbiome and gut’s extracellular matrix. This diversity is precisely how humans are able to make millions of different antibodies to protect itself from all forms of pathogens in our gut associated lymphoid system (GALT).

You might think a blog on the microbiome should not start talking about light.

So why are we beginning with light? **All food emits biophotons.** The gut is designed to process food, therefore, it must be designed to deal with light. This is why I begin where others end.
This begs the question, why are we all focusing in on the paleolithic dietary template, when the pot of gold maybe before this era, when our guts first were naturally selected for leakiness? This has to beg the question, is the current version of the paleo diet really the most optimal diet for humans? Or might we just merely function better on it when we compare it to a post agricultural diet of today? In my view, there is an [Epi Paleo Rx](http://www.amazon.com/Epi-paleo-Rx-Prescription-Disease-Reversal-ebook/dp/B00BIUAZUQ) for modern humans that must include a lot of DHA to deal with the light frequencies emitted in our gut lumen by bacteria. Might this be a reason why we can never really heal a leaky gut? Might it be because a leaky gut is a human trait built in by evolution to work with our light emitting microbiome? Could this be why the paleo diet works at times to limit the microbiome light emissions or the gut’s ability to be leaky? Might this also limit some from optimal levels of functioning? When you consider our brain has been shrinking since the late paleolithic era, this tells me our environment is moving our species away from what is biologically optimal, so we might want to reconsider what is “_good_” for us today.

In my view, there is an [Epi Paleo Rx](http://www.amazon.com/Epi-paleo-Rx-Prescription-Disease-Reversal-ebook/dp/B00BIUAZUQ) for modern humans that must include a lot of DHA to deal with the light frequencies emitted in our gut lumen by bacteria. Could this be why the paleo diet works at times to limit the microbiome light emissions or the gut’s ability to be leaky? Might this also limit some from optimal levels of functioning? When you consider our brain has been shrinking since the late paleolithic era, this tells me our environment is moving our species away from what is biologically optimal, so we might want to reconsider what is “_good_” for us today.

Anyway - Tensegrity 14 is quite readable and truly one of Dr. K's most earth shattering utterly cool posts IMHO -

But I differ from many in that I think anyone who is ill would probably benefit from an h pylori bug kill for a few weeks because I think once stomach acid goes (i.e.low zinc) things just start living in your stomach which shouldn't be there - low stomach acid is a gateway - and it is tough to test for some of these pathogens - that's why I go for the broad based systemic herbal bombs - but hey, I'm not a doctor but I have had success helping a few people out with autoimmune diseases - I think you're on an amazing right road - in fact, you are getting this stuff faster than most and are quite inspiring - it is just going to take some time to see results because of where you live and your past vegetarian diet - IMHO -

### Matty_M — 2015-03-06T20:09:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/post-157648>

Hi everybody!

1\. I am beginning the **Leptin Rx** today. I woke up, made a big piece of salmon, and then these egg coconut pancakes. Probably about 60-70 g of protein 100-150 g of fat (eggs+coconut oil in pancakes+butter on pancakes+coconut oil for frying), and probably 60g NET carbs (1 banana, 1 cup raspberries, 6 tbsp coconut flour).

I have decided to do this because I think, although I'm young and its probably not TOO severe, I definitely have a degree of leptin resistance and circadian mismatch (even though I've been wearing my night goggle after dark for months), as I don't sleep well (I especially have trouble waking up), and I have gotten horrible headaches and felt like I was going to die (I was laying down about to pass out and almost called an ambulance last time, a few months ago..) from any intense sprinting/weightlifting. Lastly, I feel like a drug addict for carby foods (the things mainly eat now are just fruit and starches- though I don't want to, sometimes I just feel like I need it and feel better after eating it. Rarely, I'll be at a friend's and eat a bunch of semisweet chocolate chips (or something else like excess dried fruit- but rarely to never grain/legume/conventional dairy stuff) and totally regret it. I know I have control over what I do, but sometimes my body wants carbs badly enough that I will (very willingly, yet completely in denial) end up eating something crappy like that.

For this reason especially, I will do the Leptin Rx as perfectly as I can and eat primarily just vegetables and berries as carbs, but if I ever _want_ them I will have a banana or sweet potato to be satisfied rather than eat junk at my friends. I'm slowly recovering from good being the enemy of perfect, and so having vegetables, berries, and starch (as needed) while getting through the leptin reset while totally avoiding sweeteners, etc. is the best thing I can do to avoid a binge that resets me. This is upon the assumption that, although it's winter, I can follow the under ~50g of carbs rule (with breakfast) and still reset my leptin function. Please tell me if this is incorrect or if there is anything I should tweak or consider.

Also, I am wondering about how strict the eating upon rising is?.. I woke up this morning and went straight to cooking and eating, but after making and eating the salmon and making AND eating the egg coconut pancakes, about two hours had passed. This is NOT the case on school mornings, where I have an hour to get ready, but I still am rarely able to get all 50g of protein cooked and down my gullet within 30 minutes of rising. It usually drags out closer to one hour, with having to brush my teeth, and fill up an ice bucket for my face, get dressed, etc. Is this a fatal flaw? I believe that the BETTER I can follow the program, the more quickly everything will work well, so I will do my best to do this, making changes to my routines where necessary.

My protein sources are canned salmon (and some tuna maybe, not too much), fish filets, grass fed ground beef and grass fed stew beef. Maybe shrimp at some point but not as a staple. Using eggs would definitely make things easier, but I will explain why I'm not in the next section.

2\. I have been, and continue to work with my functional doctor to heal my gut. I am primarily glad to have her because she can give me wisdom from experience, get lab tests, get prescriptions, etc.- stuff I wouldn't be able to do on my own. Also, my dad needs someone with "experience", because he is too close minded to accept things I say without furious challenge.

For example, I was at the dentist about a week ago and found out that I have my first cavity ever (which is not surprising, however, because based on my previous health, diet, and lifestyle, I would have certainly thought that I would have multiple cavities by now, like my siblings. This was not a shock, and might have been around for over a year (because I haven't seen the dentist in about that long). I exclaimed to my dad when we walked out "this should not happen. Our ancestors really did not get cavities, based on a good bit of fossil data. I want to wait out my gut healing and everything before getting a filling because my instinct is that my tooth should heal itself." However, my dad has some issues, especially a short temper, and when I say something he disagrees with, he'll always say "Oh, I forgot you know everything" (often with the f bomb). Because I have a good therapist, I'm getting better at handling this, as well as with improving my brain's function. However, it does suck to have emotionally unstable parents who don't really function like normal parents due to a divorce and other things, and these occurrences have definitely had their impact on my health, but the therapist is really helping. Despite many issues, my parents are both great people and really support me and try their hardest every day for me and my siblings so I really love them. I digress. I am beginning to take a K2 supplement, as I definitely should with a wacky gut and, now, a minor cavity. My teeth have always been pale, yellow-ish, and far from optimal in appearance, but that is going to change .

So this part, with the doc (who is on the paleo level), is **gut healing** protocol. I am basically working into it Dr. Kruse's Leaky Gut Rx, but I cannot afford many of the supplements together- FOS, aloe, N-acetyl-cysteine, brain magnesium (though I have natural calm), zinc, krill oil, and colostrum. I will try to slowly work all of these in though, as soon as possible. REGARDLESS, I am doing the first few steps.

\-These are reversing the things that cause a leaky gut (stress, EMF, "non-paleo" foods, and, for me- coffee, cocoa, O6 rich nuts/seeds, nightshades, all dairy besides ghee, and egg whites (butter and egg whites are going to be that last to go, and this breakfast was probably it for the pair.)

\-Then, coconut oil is the main fat (and I eat a lot of it, through coconut milk/cream, frying everything in coconut oil, and eating coconut butter.

\-Lots of probiotic foods, as well as the supplement form my doc, which has the exact three species that Dr. Kruse recommends, which don't use transition metals.

\-I am eating a lot of onions and garlic for FOS, and will try to buy the supplement eventually.

\-I am also trying to get grass fed beef bones for broth. It is very difficult during this cold winter, so would grass-fed, grain-finished bones be better than none at all????

\-I am taking betaine HCl with each meal, and a digestive enzyme pill, although the HCl is almost out and I will probably replace it with a zinc supplement to support the stomach acid production. I take a good bit of magnesium citrate from natural calm each night, with vitamin C (both originally used to keep me moving when I was constipated, but listed on the leaky gut rx so I will keep them up)

\-I am also taking products called repairvite and GI synergy by APEX energetics, given by the functional doc. They seem to know what they're doing. Repairvite on top. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

The next things I will get are Zinc and high-quality Krill Oil.

3\. I am using all the other vital aspects of epi-paleo, including **CT**. I go outside upon sunrise, per the Leptin Rx, I try to ground when possible (which is only a few minutes per day, as the ground is ice here near Philly, and it's freezing cold). I am trying to use my basement floor regularly, so I need to get a workstation for the room because I don't have time during the week just to sit around if I want to get to sleep around 9pm.

For the cold, I have been dunking my face in ice water for about 5-20 dunks each morning, each averaging about a minute. and the water is COLD tap water with a bit of ice. The infrared says the water is around 30 F, and my skin temp is consistently getting down between 50-60 degrees. For about two weeks I have been laying 2 7 lb bags of ice on my chest each night, and have built up to an hour with no shirt, and my chest gets all pink and slightly numb as Dr. Kruse said it might. Soon I will begin ice baths

I have even begun wearing my somewhat blue-blocking gamma-ray glasses in school, and will try to keep this up whenever surrounded by only artificial light.

C. I don't want to do too much at once, but I want to be optimal as soon as possible. I must confess, just last night I overate carbs in the form of semisweet chocolate chips, banana chips, and dried apricots, as well as a bit excess of pistachios while at a friend's. I KNOW I can control this, and so I am doing the Leptin Rx so I do not feel like I cannot control myself in those moments, and staying as low-carb as possible, but not avoiding a sweet potato only to succumb to something crappy later that day.

The trio is **Leptin Rx** (so, epi-paleo), a version of the **Leaky Gut Rx**, and **Cold Thermogenesis**. I am also going to work in the parts of the EPCOTx that I can, although my main focus will be on the Leptin Rx and Leptin Rx, as it's always harder for me to NOT eat something, than to include something. Last question: Any tips for very limited diets (now that I'm cutting all coffee/cocoa, nightshades, nuts/seeds (besides macadamias and coconut), dairy (besides ghee) and egg whites? I'm not even that limited, but I think it is the lack of carby foods that always has me feeling deprived at some level. NOT AFTER I DO THE LEPTIN RX RIGHT!

Thanks for any input everyone, as this is the ideal protocol I have developed based on all of your help.

@[yewwei.tan](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6824/)\- I tried the carb refeed, but it did not help reduce my cravings. ### yewwei.tan — 2015-03-07T02:10:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/post-157675>

Not responding well to an acute insulin signal means that the peripheral gut, pancreas, and liver circadian clocks are not working well. The AMPk pathway cannot be upregulated, and refeeds will be harmful rather than helpful.

So the suggestion is to stop the carb refeeds.

\----

If I were you, I'd personally cut all the carbs. I don't care about cravings, just cut them.

Yup, I know that's hard, because I've been in the same situation, and the only thing that helps is to create new dopamine-inducing distractions (whatever it takes ... good music, play certain sports, read some good fiction, go have a good conversation with someone, whatever ....)

\----

At this point, you know it's your environment that is screwing you over, and any dietary optimisations, and even stuff like CT, is just damage control , and not getting to the root of the problem.

That's tough, and besides taking a vacation, I don't see a way out.

The best advice now is to plan a camping trip whenever the opportunity next arises, and look to your schedule and see where you can find openings for getting out to a more healing locale.

....

Dear everyone,

I got accepted as a semifinalist for the Kennedy-Lugar Youth Exchange and Study Abroad scholarship. I am one of 110 kids out of hundreds who applied who have the chance to go to DC for a weekend and be interviewed and everything. I think I mentioned this exchange previously in the thread, but now the chance is much more real.

If accepted I have a shot at going to Bosnia, Ghana, India, Indonesia, Malaysia, Macedonia, Morocco, Oman, the Phillipines, South Africa, Thailand, Turkey.

Now that I have made it to semifinals, I have a 59% chance of being accepted (65 ot the 110 ger in), not factoring in the interview process at all. I am very confident. This would be for all of my junior year. I think it could be a good break from my bad environment within a two hour drive of the two of the biggest cities on the eastern seaboard, only 15 minuts from Philly, and in a school loaded with cell phones and wifi most of the day during the week.

However, if I get offered to go to a big city like Istanbul, could things be worse than my suburban area? Or is the US by far the worst place to be? I would want to go somewhere secluded in the Phillipines, but I dont have a choice of where to go and I still want to get a good education.

Please give me opinions on what to do if accepted, like which situations should I consider accepting and which might be worse than where I am? I dont have a choice on where, but I can say yes or no.

\- Matt

These two pictures sum up last night pretty well. The water started at 42 F, and I had my torso under for about 35 min, and legs under for about 10 more, and 5 minutes it took to remember about the timer.

I might have gone a bit long, but it felt great after 5-10 minutes of cold cold. After exiting, it took me probably 30-45 minutes to warm back up and stop shivering. Tonight I went 25 minutes torso under and 10 legs under.

Once adapted, does the extreme cold in the beginning go away? Will the baths feel more pleasant? How can I adapt more quickly?

I have the Leptin Reset underway and have been doing it near perfect for the last few days, minus eating a meal close to bed one day, and a bit higher carb another. Now, I have fully adopted my functional doc's advice for avoidance of coffee/cocoa, nuts/seeds (beside coconut and macadamia), nightshades, egg whites, and dairy beside ghee. Also, my sights are set on totally ketogenic, meaning my only plants while healing are macadamias, coconut, few tigernuts for RS, non starch veggies, and few berries. I have thought about having some starch to prevent possibly giving in to some crap carbs with friends, but I know I'm in control of that and @[yewwei.tan](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6824/) made a great point to me that cravings dont matter, in a way. It's about doing what will help me to get to optimal as soon as possible.

Also, I am doing as many things on the list of Leakt Gut Rx as possible.

\- Matt

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/post-158490>

I'm kind of feeling at a point of _desperation_...

I'm feeling **exhausted**, I can **barely get out of bed**, and I feel **pretty "depressed"** or down in the dumps. I think this definitely has to do with being strictly low-carb for the last week (within my conditions of existence).

I have read as many of the blogs as possible that relate directly to my situation, including "Why Ketosis Appears to Fail Some" as well as "EMF 4: Why You Might Need Carbs for Performance"- and have taken away that I live in a bad environment, I am dehydrated, I need lots of DHA, and I need to avoid/block artificial light, especially after sunset, as well as a few other things. The "What Might OJ Simpson and Casey Anthony Have in Common?" blog makes me fairly certain I have sustained a degree of adrenal fatigue.

So the question is, **how can I get through this more quickly?** Or **how long might this take**, **what changes might I notice**, and what will be the physiological change that will allow me to be well? Will it be entering the PPP, or healing my adrenals, or other things?

I am going to keep **CTing** (I've been doing daily 42 F ice baths for ~50 minutes for 6 days now but have only felt worse, I don't believe it was due to these at all, though). I'll continue with the **BAB**and **Leptin Rx**. I will undoubtedly stay **epi-paleo**, I will keep out the **autoimmune foods** (egg whites, nuts/seeds, dairy, nightshades) at least for a while, I will continue with the **healing supplements**, and **grounding on my basement floor** (and outside when possible), **getting real sunlight** in the morning and whenever else possible. I want to remain in **nutritional winter** (besides few berries, macadamias, tigernuts for RS, and loads of coconut, that's it- _unless these definitely ought to be removed_, though I feel very limited already), but I don't know how I am going to sustain _feeling like crap_. I know I could start eating carbs but my gut feeling is that it would very much hinder my getting to optimal, though I'm not very sure why.

I'd really appreciate advice. I think that, immediately, the best thing I can do is find ways (besides carbs) to increase my dopamine, although I don't know if I would even be able to handle something like a day of snowboarding with my current energy level.

And two specific Q's:

**Could wearing my earthrunner sandals (with copper conductors) while in my school be a bad idea- i.e. would the copper attract more EMF to me?**

**Could sleeping grounded on my slab somehow be connecting me to dirty electricity or something in my house?** (I just started this for the last two nights). I don't know very much about how electricity and EMFs work, but I don't have time currently to make it my first priority to understand. I have, however, begun reading the EMF series through.

Thanks,

\- Matt
