### drezy — 2017-06-13T01:53:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/post-216020>

> [Matty\_M said:](/goto/post?id=215882)
>
> Wow. I am you and you are me.
> I am love.

Hey me at 17! It' me at 40! Let me fill you in on what happens to us. We've been put through the wringer, found ourselves in the most surreal situations domestically and abroad good and bad, backstabbed in personal and professional relationships, and so many other odd interactions. We wouldn't take it back for anything. Why? because the best things in our life right now have absolutely ZERO in common with our life plan (especially sub 26-ish).

We also changed from "I am love" to "I am Kinda Like, but verify". It's all good.

Once we get to @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) stage we might change our moniker again. Let's see if he takes the bait.

> In regards to my other main symptom, chronic nasal congestion;
> \- it clears up slightly with sunlight
> \- it gets more congested if I consume coffee, alcohol, or chocolate. **What's the commonality?**
> \- seems like it gets worse after dark
>
> "If I'm present in every moment, how will I be able to plan for my _future_?"

Good. Want a cheap redox test? Occasionally use those food as your "check engine light". I do with my "kryptonite" and it works well.

The dichotomy of being in the moment and planning, other than the fact that the future is a progression of moments, has best been explained to me by my buddy Nick Machiavelli:

> I hold that it could be true that fortune is the arbiter of half of actions, but that she still leaves the other half, or close to it, to be governed by us. And she resembles one of those violent rivers which, when they become enraged, flood the plains, tear down trees and buildings, lift up the earth from one side and deposit it on the other... But this does not mean that men, when times are quiet, cannot take precautions with floodgates and embankments, so that, when the rivers swell up again, either they would move along through a canal, or their rush would not be so unchecked and harmful. The same happens with fortune, who displays her force where there is no prepared resource to resist her.

### shah78 — 2017-06-13T21:49:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/post-216095>

> [Matty\_M said:](/goto/post?id=216079)
>
> So, why do you think I get near throwing up after a workout?

Nausea: try doing the work out with 10% less intensity. Cut back on either,top end exertion, length of exertion or increase rest interval. If that fails, alter two of the above. If that fails alter all three variables.... Or start over with ONLY two intervals and add one only after each non "nausea" inducing workout.

### drezy — 2017-06-15T02:52:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/post-216218>

> [Matty\_M said:](/goto/post?id=216079)
>
> So, why do you think I get near throwing up after a workout?

Hmmm, do you have enough cops chasing you? I've never been a runner, but at that age the more cops that were chasing after me with the threat of mom having to get me from jail the longer and faster I could run, hurdle short fences, and climb high fences.

I suggest that whatever you're doing right before running isn't heinous enough and your body just knows it.

### Matty_M — 2017-06-15T03:21:36+0300

### Danny — 2017-06-18T02:01:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/post-216458>

> [Matty\_M said:](/goto/post?id=216457)
>
> I've had GERD. How could sprints bring inflammation to the LES?, and how could this make me want naseous?

All the jostling and exertion puts a lot of tension on the solar plexus area. This area is radiating w/ nerves and irritating these can give way to nausea. It will improve when your mitochondria improve. I have an issue w/ hiatal hernia where my stomach moves up and puts pressure on the opening of my diaphragm. This can bring on some nausea. I've had major improvement w/ this and it takes a lot to aggravate it now.

### Matty_M — 2017-07-04T05:26:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/post-217917>

Since I've begun traveling, I have begun to have some bad gas at some times. It is pretty bad and lasts for a number of hours when it comes.

It seems to happen when I eat when I am not very hungry, but sometimes it happens regardless.

**I have gone from eating two epi-paleo meals per day alone to eating with people and therefore on others' schedules.**

I was conceived in vitro and born via c-section so obviously my gut is fucked up. **Epi paleo seems to prevent me from having gas, but it obviously is not solving the root problem.
**
@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) what do you suggest as a real long term solution?

I have already tried probiotics, I am putting myself in damn good environments.

I have yet to be in constant strong sun though. Maybe this is what I need. After saving money for a few months I will go to the Yucatan and become a beach bum for a few months. But does anyone think this will solve my gut flora?

**I feel like a fecal transplant is the best option for a messed up gut flora. How can I find a doctor who will do this?**

I suggest that you figure out your fatty acids, more variety better.
Pay particular attention to
Short-chain fatty acids (SCFAs)
and there
Butyric acid (C4:0)
The short-chain fatty acid butyrate is particularly important for colon health.

Butyric acid's name is from butter.
It is good idea to eat butter.
but
which one?

@[SatoriHeart](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19303/)
@[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/)
**any clues???!!**
\-----------------------------
Side note.
This days butter does not go bad. Not sure why, but I think that whatever is added, **it may prevent butyric acid from forming** in the butter.
Way back then, on my parents farm in Poland, freshly made butter would not last long fresh.
It became smelly.
That smell is how butyric acid smells.
You can learn that smell when you get the above pills.

Maybe we have to learn how to make own butter.
Milk in NJ is homogenized, it will not get cream on top.

Hey Matt! Strong sun daily definitely helps. Eating and digesting in strong sun keeps my digestive system functioning well. Don't get hung up on the circumstances of your birth. I was conceived naturally and born via vaginal birth and I still managed a circadian mismatch of the gut. In this modern nnEMF soup world I do think it is necessary to be in the tropics, yuca tan, or at least a strong sun environment consistently for a length of time depending on your circumstances to overcome issues like these. Something that Jack said that sticks with me... If he had autoimmune or cancer he would move inside the tropics. Move not visit. It is going to take a sustained energy to overcome autoimmunity. I consider gut issues the first stages of AI. I am in playa del Carmen scouting out some rentals. Let me know if your interested in going in on something. I'm looking to do this on the cheap.

Bro I am so in.

Currently me llamo broke motherfucker.

Ahora yo soy en la Ecuador.

Me voy a regresar a Filadelfia para trabajar dos meses como un caddie para tener dinero $$$$$. Despues, voy ir a Europea con mi mejor amigo para dos meses. Entonces, regreso en Sur America.

Right now my name is broke motherfucker.

I am in Ecuador for 3 more days. Then I am going to Martha's Vineyard for a few days. Then I am going to work as a caddie for two months and make bread from rich people.

Then I am probably going to surf and hang out with beautiful girls in Europe until the end of October when Europe is getting dark. I might skip over Europe altogether until the spring. After that my plan is to come to Costa Rica with my friend in November, then go to Playa Del Carmen for the Event. I hope to stay in Mexico for a month or two. Then, I may hop down to the Ecuator and Tripc of Capricorn in January when the sun is directly overhead and travel around Columbia, Ecuador, Peru, Brazil, Argentina, and Chile.

No, traveling may not be ideal for the body. But, I want to do it and I will do it for a year in strong sunlight while my heteroplasmy is low and I still have the chance to go to college after.

Sure I could go straight to college and get a degree and start becoming a slave to my wishes for our temporary society, or I could have the experience of a fucking lifetime and live it up. Earth is a much better teacher than any college.

I cannot assume that I will have the income to rent a place in the Yucatan anytime soon, so I plan to take my dad's generous offer to pay for college and go to New College of Florida in Sarasota at 27 degrees 20' (less than four degrees from the tropic of cancer at 23 26') for as many as four years if I do not come across anything more exciting in this time.

I believe that the lawyers will be like dirty murderous bloodhounds when they realize what is going on with BIG TELECOM. I would like to learn the law and lay out the story as the teacher. If I do this well enough, I may avoid spending too much of my life in court.

A psychic I visited said that at age 20 I wont be doing what I think I'll be doing now. She nailed everything else so I'm quite interested. She even saw the same colors as Roxanne in Vermont @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) . Cool shit. So, I'm riding the wave for now.

All your wisdom is welcome. I may sound arrogant or something. Nope, I'm just under the influence and high on sunlight in the small Ecuadorian beach town Canoa.

I love you all

### Penny — 2017-07-07T21:55:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/post-218190>

I love you too, baby Travel while you're young I grew up on Cape Cod - you'll do fine I for the life of me don't know how or why the lawyers are sitting on this one... I mean, talk about deep pockets If you are broke, you could still feed your gut flora with the chitosan from shrimp shells plus add water and seaweed and make a broth... over a campfire... and coconut everything is cheap where you are and is great for the gut - bugs can't digest large fat molecules... for air travel, I like what Dr. Jonathan Wright does - he takes 10 drops of 5% potassium iodide before the flight... MB is also cheap - ask for a lot of water - I also found that when I gave blood, I didn't have a bunch of free iron floating around 1) acting like an antenna 2)floating around unbound ( a la Jablonski) - plus, bugs love iron for their biofilms - you can see how this goes to shit very fast... this is also why lactoferrin is such a hit - and naturally you already know about the jet lag RX Have fun and report back when you can - oh, I love garlic and the berberines for a healthy gut -I just scored 2 oregon grape plants which will be fun if they survive the heat here...

I think Pollack thinks the cellular water is made by drinking it, rather than being the output from the mitochondria - of course, if you drink more water, less magnesium is lost and more ATP is made, but I think it's important to get that point across - a la protein folding diseases... the other thing you might mention to him is how (Jablonski diagrams) light affects transition metals and calcium and vitamin A/melanopsin - you will find these biochemists think that the sodium/potassium pump is the most vetted idea in biology... unless you absolutely understand and can chat about the charge separation of water, most biochemists will think it's woowoo - but you can still learn a lot about heteroplasmy from him and it's cool he will talk with you

I looked at a map in my teacher's classroom of lights at night on the globe and I'm in basically the brightest area (NY, DE, Philly) besides the Netherlands and Tokyo, and I'm sure the lights accompany the EMFs. My school is part of the district that had a scandal a few years ago about pictures being taken of kids remotely with their school-issued laptop. It's so high tech. Every room has a projector, again, ~25 cell phones and 25 computers per room with 1300 kids- I'm seriously considering trying to go to school with some of my mom's family in another country, also awaiting acceptance to an abroad program I applied for, most likely to Turkey, but I could go anywhere (I really hope it works out).
>
> I really wonder whether letting time play out could be my best move with diet and all.

Hey Matt,
Another Matt here. I’m from King of Prussia PA so I know where Narberth is and was surprised to see a fellow Philly Suburban Matt. Just starting reading this thread I hope you’re doing great!
Best,
Matt

# Thread: Goodbye City Life!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 442
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nonchalant — 2015-02-05T04:25:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-154773>

It's been a month now since we have officially moved to the country. Lovin' it.
This morning I realized that I haven't been drinking water at night while in bed. I had previously been drinking up to a half liter of water nightly. I learned that if I drank only a mouthful at a time, I would only have to get up once for the bathroom. I was also a pro at drinking while laying down.

But in the past week, my water bottle beside the bed has been untouched, except for perhaps a swig before I climbed into bed. Really interesting.

I also checked my RF meter recently. The graph display while in the city usually showed a line about halfway up the screen. Now it almost doesn't read anything. My concerns about it not working anymore were alleviated when I took it to town. Oh yeah, working just fine.

Apologies to GreenAcres for the title of this journal, and hat tip to Audrey for her persistence.

### cantweight — 2015-02-05T04:38:09+0200

When I have high nnEMF exposure my thirst goes nuts! So does the childrens. We will leave a place and be completely parched, lips dry....like we just left the desert!

What a great sign  Enjoy your sleep sans bathroom trip!

### nonchalant — 2015-02-05T17:47:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-154816>

> [Linz said:](/goto/post?id=154797)

>
> Interesting thoughts on thirst. I have never been thirsty at night until recently but now I wake up quite frequently with a very dry mouth. Living well out of town but all too close to the big city.

I'll have to look into the biodynamic method again, Linz. My family has had a compost pile all my life, so I've only been without one when I've lived in an apartment or dorm. And I sprinkle wood ashes everywhere, including the garden and compost pile. It's a good source of potassium to increase the pH of the soil here. The bluebonnets seem to love it (it's a Texan thing, folks).

I have pics of DH's garden last year, but I think they are on my dead laptop. I should be able to recover them from the hard drive one of these days...

### Linz — 2015-02-08T11:37:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-155025>

Had to look up the bluebonnets - beautiful blue lupins!

We use ash, compost and plenty of manure here. And now added volcanic rock dust, charcoal dust and use a biodynamic calendar, copper tools to preserve the soil's natural magnetism and play with the water. Our vegetables have had a really intense flavour this year.

I'm sure you won't have any time to spare just now but when you do Alick Bartholomew's book Hidden Nature is absolutely full of fascinating information about soil temperature gradients, minerals - and very many more of Victor Schauberger's amazing ideas for the world. Easy to read too thanks to being here for a while.

### nonchalant — 2015-03-08T19:19:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-157848>

Ubi6 has come out at a great time for me, since I'm going to be eating more protein for a while. I've decided to do a round of homeopathic hcg, now that we've moved to a better environment. I'm new to hcg, except for 2 pregnancies, lol!

I first took the sublinqual pellets Thursday night and woke at sunrise as usual, but I felt more upbeat and energetic. And I noticed a reduction in appetite that morning and throughout the day. So I ate plenty for the two loading days, but probably not as much as suggested by the diet. I'm using the HCG 2.0 book as suggested in this thread: [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/good-time-to-do-hcg.10811/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/good-time-to-do-hcg.10811/) After reading it, I think I've been on the loading phase for years! Like others of the thread, I'm going to merge Jack's ideas with the diet.

Today starts the main part of the diet, phase 2. I ate a can of sardines (in water) within 30 minutes of waking, and followed it with a package of frozen raw shrimp, heated on the stove. Plus tea with MCT oil. 74 g. protein. I've started measuring my water intake again. I'll skip lunch, and have oysters and maybe a pork chop for dinner, if I can find some in the freezer.

I've been watching my nighttime EMF better lately, eating even earlier, wearing my orange goggles sooner, not using the computer after sunset, and going to bed earlier, after enjoying the fire. This winter we've been sleeping in a cold bedroom, which is often 40-50 degrees F in the morning. I sometimes wake up shivering, so I drag the second sheet on me, and usually one quilt, but before I'm asleep I usually toss the quilt off. And often the second sheet as well. So yes, the bedroom is gradually getting colder all night, but I must be experiencing fluctuating energy levels. Well, I'm probably gradually improving on my temperature regulation, so I'll be patient.

I am counting on sunshine, CT, grounding, water, & tensegrity to help me through the difficult parts of the diet, especially 5-7 days in, according to the book. We finally got some sunshine a couple days ago, it was heaven to bask in. \*\*

Need to finish reading Ubi6. I also re-read Jack's blog on [Adjuncts for the HCG diet](http://jackkruse.com/what-should-hcgpaleo-users-consider-as-adjuncts/). I have taken several of the supplements mentioned previously, but turmeric, seaweed, and green tea are the only ones I'm taking now. I've pared down the pills I'm taking; not sure I want to buy more. I am taking God's supplement now, after all -- oysters!

\*\*Now that I think of it, I may increase magnetism at night as well. Before we moved we had 2 craigslist magneticos, one at each house. I have promised to give one to my sister, but she hasn't said she wants it just yet. I may put one layer of her set under our bed on the floor, for an extra boost. Sounds like a great project for a rainy day like today!

> I've started homeopathic remedies this week aimed at the hypothalamus and hydration, crazy thirst for first day, a bit less day 2, and gradually more normal since. The difference now is that I probably drink my normal amount, but I drink because I am thirsty, not just because I'm trying to drink a minimum amount in a day. I'm feeling the cold a bit more, but not in a bad way. Its almost as if I am less numb to my sense of thirst, temperature etc IYKWIM? last 2 nights I've slept through, waking up when it starts getting light, between 7 and 8 hours sleep. I've been re reading Becker's book Cross Currents, and homeopathy comes under the "energy _reinforcement_" category .... I quite like that description, it opens up a way to make homeopathy less hit and miss ...... Reinforcing the energy coming from the 3 legged stool ....

Yes, I had that crazy thirst for a couple days. Definitely doing something for the hypothalamus. Also lately I have been getting a more dramatic response to my tensegrity brushing. Flabby skin is toughening up. Probably since I'm eating seafood everyday (often twice daily) again. DHA does wonders!

I've definitely lost more belly fat than usual, but I've weighed this morning, and still not lost any weight. So if my weight is the same, but my volume has decreased, my density must have increased ...? A bit of Archimedes principle in action ....? Definitely different signalling ..... my recovery after CT for the last couple of days has been quicker except that my spine seems much more sensitive to the cold, and takes longer to warm up. Going to see how that goes, and the results of tightening up my blue light exposure, before adding another dose of the remedy. I've got better blueblockers now, and have made a tray to go under my ipad, its a wicker basket, with 3 layers of a gel pack (that goes inside a pillow case) plus a bag with 2 pounds of oyster shell grit....It weighs about four pounds, but I don't have to touch the ipad when I move to another room, and if I do occasionally have it on my lap, there's a bit more protection  Had lots of  for that one, but I did notice after I had explained the reasons that a couple of usual habits changed

Sue, I was just thinking about you the other day. I'll post in your "The Senses" thread.

Giving up blue light has been a long-term process also, like slowly descending a staircase.

For tensegrity purposes, I sometimes brush my skin in any direction with a hairbrush like this:
[https://breezyk.files.wordpress.com/2013/01/hairbrush2.jpg](https://breezyk.files.wordpress.com/2013/01/hairbrush2.jpg)
I've found that I get more response with skin that is already taut, like on the hands, feet, knees, elbows, shoulders, back, etc. But the skin that needs it more is the flabby stuff! I also use other sides of my fingernails, like scratching with the tops of the nails instead of in the normal manner. Fingernails are more effective than the hairbrush, for me. A good brushing will give me lots of goosebumps.

Tensegrity brushing is great for a burst of energy when the sunshine is not available. The energy effect seems similar to what CT gives me, though it doesn't last as long.

There was a bit of a cold wind, but not too bad, and clear blue skies this morning. I did my CT, first sat on the step in the bath, leaned back, and kept my wet stomach out of the water. I wet my hands and arms and then had them out in the sun, with my palms and wrists upwards. When they were nice and warm in the sun I then placed my palms on my stomach and moved so that I went under water, then massaged my stomach, then came back up and repeated. When I got out my fingers had gone white from the knuckle to the tips, so I tried just tapping and massaging them, but that didn't seem to do much. Then I had the idea of using my cold fingers to massage, tap, and apply pressure to my scalp and forehead. That seemed to do the trick, but I need to repeat the experiment doing it sooner rather than later. But it had me wondering, if my fingers gave something to my head, did they also tap into the SQUID of the brain and get something back?

### nonchalant — 2015-04-03T18:37:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-160228>

Ubi 8 is a nice, easy to read blog for me. I was interested in the part about the tibial compression test.
I have noticed dramatic responses to sunshine in my spine. Several days of completely cloudy weather ends up with back pain for me, where my usual stretches do not bring instant relief anymore, grounding doesn't help much, and even ice packs only give partial, short-lived relief. I get irritable and pouty, and I don't want to do anything. The first day of strong sunshine, however, and my back creaks back into proper position, and the pain vanishes. I'm all smiles and I want to do a bunch of work. Do chiropractors get more business in the darker months?

If I build up my redox, though, I probably won't see so much change in health from a week of clouds. But hey, I've come a long way. The scoliosis is history, I think. All those nights of laying on large pads of ice are a thing of the past (though I probably will reinstate the practice this summer for CT purposes). I haven't seen a chiropractor in 6-8 months. I do remember one night this winter when my back was hurting but I didn't want to get up for an ice pad. I rolled to my side and removed the sheet from my back, but kept it on my legs and arms, because it was COLD! I soon noticed relief in my back, and I was able to get to sleep. So yes, at least for me, air CT does do some good.

I woke up so many times this winter, shivering. I think just about every night. We stopped using the wood heater at night. So I would put on a second sheet, and some times even a quilt, but I usually removed the quilt, and often the second sheet, after a few minutes. DH didn't like to cuddle with me because my skin was so cool, but I was comfy inside. And every morning my armpits were so sweaty! Even sometimes my inner arms, and palms of my hands! TMI, I know! Glad Jack told us about this, because it is counter intuitive to sweat when you are cold!

I'm just glad DH is taking Armour thyroid now, so he can tolerate the cold much better. He still piles the blankets on, but he is willing to let the house get quite cold. We would wake to temps in the 40's quite often. It's been a good winter.

### fitness@home — 2015-04-04T04:10:25+0300

Great update Sally. It seems your new life fits you well! I always throw off the blanket part way through the night.

I haven't got to Ubi 8 yet, but will sometime this weekend after we get home. We fly back to Houston tomorrow.

I've had a wonderful sun filled week...

### Sue-UK — 2015-04-04T11:37:56+0300

Ubi 8 has me thinking I need to work harder at creating an upgraded battery.

Because any available solar energy makes it sooooo much easier and nicer I've felt that the benefits I've had from being able to tolerate the cold acutely isn't the same thing as being cold adapted. I've been wondering if being fully cold adapted needs the battery upgrade (a polar bear must have one helluva upgraded battery ) but a sunlight and water battery helps me as a woman in the cold ..... Maybe at my latitude I don't need to get to polar bear  and I think I have moved beyond alpine plant (water chemistry changes happening ....)... but with our regular lack of sunshine I think I have to work out how to have an upgraded battery for cold dark and boring, plus a sunlight and water battery for when the conditions are right. DHA, DHA DHA.....

### Inger — 2015-04-07T09:30:33+0300

next day his wife come and had a talk to me how she is afraid I will leave them when they install it, cause they will anyways.. and she was kinda upset....

### nonchalant — 2015-04-09T19:48:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-160745>

DH has been busy in the attic rewiring the back half of the house. I now have a grounded plug for my washer, yay!
The veggies are popping up like crazy in the garden. Asparagus, green beans, peas, lettuce, cabbage, onions, tomatoes and red potatoes. I still have some herb seeds to plant. Elsewhere, the elderberry bush is leafing out nicely, I have purchased yet another blueberry bush (this time I'll be here to water it) and the dewberries are flowering like crazy. The mexican plums have finished blooming, along with the peaches and pears, and the two little Indian peaches that were on the property when we bought it. I haven't noticed much activity from the poke salat, though. Can't wait for that asparagus-like taste. The sour dock is popping up everywhere. It becomes an annoying weed, but it tastes great to chew on the stems. They have a nice citrus flavor.

I have stopped taking the homeopathic hcg. It was working just fine, and I lost a few pounds, but then we got a week of cloudy weather. I could tell my energy level was very low, and I just didn't want to push myself. The sun came out, and I restarted the diet (though I had kept taking the tablets all along) but then more cloudy days did me in. I think my redox potential needs quite a bit of work. My energy level fluctuates widely.

My mother, who is in her 80's, seems to "get it" about sunshine now. Amazing. She has Parkinson's, but she has noticed how much better she feels when she gets outside. And she sleeps better that night, also. I mentioned to her this weekend that morning sunshine is even better, because of our circadian rhythm of hormones, and she seemed to listen, and not reject the information. And I told her that just sitting next to a window cures a stuffy nose for me. Perhaps one day I could get her to do some grounding, but that will be almost impossible. She never goes without shoes. Period. But, hey, never say never!

### Sue-UK — 2015-04-10T12:47:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-160799>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=160745)
>
> I have stopped taking the homeopathic hcg. It was working just fine, and I lost a few pounds, but then we got a week of cloudy weather. I could tell my energy level was very low, and I just didn't want to push myself. The sun came out, and I restarted the diet (though I had kept taking the tablets all along) but then more cloudy days did me in. I think my redox potential needs quite a bit of work. My energy level fluctuates widely.

The weather makes such a difference ........ We had the first really warm day of spring on Monday.  I changed drops, I was feeling the ones I was using were becoming "same" so took advantage of the real change in the weather to introduce something else. I am using ones more for energy, and have started my "14-10 battery recharge diet"  The 14 is the number of hours a week minimum that I am going to recharge my battery either by CTing at 10C or less, grounding, or morning sunbathing when the light is most therapeutic. (If I sunbathe later I won't count it). So an hour stretched out sunbathing in the morning on the grass counts as 2 hours to including the grounding, but an hour sunbathing on the patio (out of the wind) counts as 1 hour as I am not sure its grounded. When I'm working or when the weather is bad I think I might struggle to do 2 hours a day, but making it a weekly minimum makes it doable.... I only managed 45 mins CT early yesterday and 15 minutes grounding before being away all day, but Monday I did 3 hours, (1 hour CT and 2 hours sunbathing and grounding), so I'm hoping it will even out .... the 10 in the 14-10 is my target for seafood portions, I'm having 2 meals a day, eating to appetite, but not counting anything except my seafood portions and my "battery recharge" time.

### nonchalant — 2015-04-12T01:33:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-160935>

I've been buying seeds from smaller towns lately though, instead of the big city. Perhaps that helps some. We have lots of volunteer tomatoes coming up from last year's heirloom plants, and some winter onions (green onions, walking onions) that have been in the family for over 100 years.

I think we are having a lot more success gardening in the country rather than the city. And the water is another big part of it. It comes from a large underground reservoir and seems to be quite energized, to me. I find that when it is stored overnight, like in the pressure tank near the well, it has less "zip" to it. I remember trying to get 'elephant ears' to grow in the city. In the summer's heat, though they were in the shade and I'd water them daily, they would always wilt around noontime. Guess fluoride doesn't do them much good, either!

### nonchalant — 2015-04-15T19:49:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-161312>

Ubi9:

> Our brain is designed to sense all types of wave forms from our environment. Our brain summates, subtracts, and reconstructs the three dimensional waveforms and their frequencies to simulate the fractal geometry of life. The geometry of life is captured in the [3 dimension fractals found within a cell.](http://motherboard.vice.com/read/what-fractals-can-teach-us-about-cancer) _Geometry links to quantum mechanics._

So many times I have filled a pot with water to heat on the stove, but I become mesmerized by the quivering surface water. Occasionally I'll see it in a glass of water also. But my big round tank outside is where I really get a show. If something disturbs the surface, like a leaf falling or me blowing a breath, the entire surface will react at the same time, not just the expected ripples of effect spreading outwards. And if there is some dust on the top it helps me see the reactions to my movements. I'll be submerged to my neck, and I'll keep my head still but move my body gently to the right side, and all the dust/pollen on the surface moves to the left, for a few seconds, and then drifts back to where it was. It's like the water is slightly viscous.

A couple weeks ago the trees were releasing lots and lots of pollen, and when I stepped in the tank (which was filled a few days before) I splashed much of it outside the tank, trying to get rid of it. I ended up churning the remaining pollen up pretty good. I finished getting in, and after a couple minutes I noticed that there was a layer in the water, where the pollen suspended on the top was moving in one direction, and a much larger layer about an inch underneath, which was moving in an opposite direction, still in response to the churning action. Interesting. After several minutes, the movements stopped, and I couldn't see any layers in the pollen.

When I first started doing CT baths, I would bring a book with me. I never do that anymore. Nature is fascinating.

### nonchalant — 2015-05-09T01:24:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-163650>

We had a lesson in thermodynamics recently. The country house we bought has a new roof, but no vents in top at all. So we put in vents for the plumbing and a whirlybird turbine. It still got hot in the attic, so we cut a hole in the ceiling and put in a whole-house attic fan. So we turn on the fan, but we start getting hot air blowing back down into the house! Putting in a second whirlybird helped only a little.

Yeah, you have to be able to exhale as much as you inhale. We removed siding from one end of the house and put a large louvered vent in one gable end. The large hole was already there, but the vent had been removed and siding (with tiny holes) put over the opening. When DH was standing on the roof and removing the siding, he was almost overwhelmed by the hot air exiting. We bought another gable vent for the other end of the house, but it will have to wait for cooler weather. The weather is too warm.

Now when we run the attic fan, we get lots more outside air coming in the windows, and absolutely no blow-back of hot air from the attic. This actually helps me understand the electric eating bacteria that need to be near metal to "exhale". You can't consume energy without being able to move it around and eliminating the waste. That must be why we feel the need to socialize. We have to give away energy in order to pick up more. Heh, it's like a full closet. You gotta donate clothing in order to go on a shopping spree! Yay for recycling!

### nonchalant — 2015-05-11T20:24:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-163862>

My love-hate relationship with dewberries has blossomed into love, sweet love. I have forgiven them totally for all the scratches and thorns they have inflicted on me for the past year. They are everywhere, ready for the picking. Have to get them before the deer and rabbits realize they are ready.

As I picked them this morning, I realized they were a lesson in photobiology. The berries start out small and green, then slowly turn white, pink, red, and then suddenly dark violet. Some berries are a collection of dark purple, except for one or two spheres/seeds that are still red. I suppose when they are green they are like the plant, wanting the same color spectrum of light. When white, they are taking it all in. As they slowly turn red, they are needing less and less of the red spectrum (and IR) and when they are ripe, they don't need all that ultraviolet. They also take on a glow of sorts, that really catch your eye. They are ready to be eaten and to grace the digestive tract of some passing animal. Yum! I also noticed that I am not eating as many berries as I have in previous years. They are still good, just not irresistible.

I noticed while picking that I seemed to be able to see the berries better than last year. So while walking back to the house I decided to experiment and take off my sandals, in an area with forest to the west and few berries and cactus. Sure enough, I soon discovered that I was able to confidently walk because I could see the ground quite well, after a brief moment of stress. When I got away from the trees, and into berry country, I put my shoes back on until I was near the house and barn. I had to smile.

### caroline — 2015-05-12T03:34:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-163899>

the awesome life of a country girl! xo

### nonchalant — 2015-05-26T20:30:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-165451>

I have started being more methodical with energy accumulation. At least once/hour (usually several times an hour) I am seeking to raise my energy level. If I'm outside, fine. I'll be getting sunlight and grounding, and CT sometimes. But when I'm indoors, I'll use water chemistry, tensegrity, and CT to boost my levels. I'll be brushing my skin or hair, dunking my face in cold water or inhaling air from the freezer, stretching, sniffing steam, cold shower, or getting the heck back outside where I belong. Or eating, even. Yeah, eating is actually another way of getting energy. We mammals are so flexible that way!

Last year I was able to luxuriate in the morning sunshine most days, but this spring has been so cloudy! I'm not going to let it be an excuse for low energy levels any longer.

### nonchalant — 2015-05-26T22:31:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-165470>

Today while cooking, I noticed that my left hand was again getting into the action, using tongs to turn the bacon. In the last couple months I've noticed my non-dominant hand occasionally pitching in, instead of just hanging around assisting the other hand. Like pouring a pitcher of water, or something like that, just because that hand is closer to the pitcher. I don't consciously choose to use it, but perhaps I should be giving it more work?

### nonchalant — 2015-06-11T02:46:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-167481>

Got my test results. My hsCRP is still high, even after leaving the city. Very disappointing. I realized my raw milk consumption has gotten out of hand, after a winter of drinking coffee with cream in it daily. Then the cream turned to milk. Then joints that were not problematic before (like my elbows) started to ache. So I have cut it out recently, and discovered that coffee isn't bad watered down a bit. Still taking molybdenum. I do eat butter at times. No cheese anymore. My elbows are getting better already.

At the end of April DH finished rewiring the house so that everything is grounded. I managed to convince him not to put any new outlets in our bedroom. We just have one, far from the bed. The Trifield indicates that the refrigerator and electrical panel are the biggest sources of EMF. Well, recently we started running the AC so that's more. Not much shows up on the RF meter. We do take trips to Dallas every couple weeks, but don't stay overnight.

So maybe my chronic inflammation will decrease, with the avoidance of dairy. I have had high inflammation while strictly avoiding dairy, but I was living in the city at the time. I'm avoiding blue light at night fairly well, but I need to put on more clothes at night to protect my skin more.

But all this talk of nicotine gum has me intrigued. Jack said it was best in Winter, though. Probably because it increases UCP thermogenesis. So more ice is in order. And my maternal line has no history (back to Grandma at least) of smoking. And I have never taken a puff. And niacin breaks out my skin in small, weepy sores. Even niacinamide, in higher doses, causes the (non-itchy) sores. And niacin makes me flush like crazy, disturbingly similar to hot flashes combined with strong sunburn. Probably all the inflammation/histamine.

I was going to wait for the cruise to start the gum, but I just can't wait! Lol. I'll put one piece by my bedside and chomp it in the morning. Probably spit out the first products though. 2 mg will probably be quite strong for me. And I'll have my usual cup of dilute coffee.

If I break out looking like I've got some awful disease, well, it will make the cruise interesting anyway! Assuming they let me onboard...

### nonchalant — 2015-06-11T17:14:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-167623>

OK, day #1 on the nicotine hack. Don't notice any difference.
Lol, is that a letdown or what?

I woke up early, eager to start. Opened the shade and saw it was too early, so went back to sleep. Tried a couple times more, finally decided the pink clouds constituted dawn.  Chewed the piece of gum for about 10-15 minutes, and got up. Did the usual, starting the coffee, and bacon and later some of my yummy guinea eggs. Yes, I was hungry, but just the usual sensation.

Outside the sun wasn't even peeking thru the trees, so I grabbed some shears and trimmed the grass around some newly-planted shrubs. Not a usual activity, but it was cool out still, might as well use it.

Now I've had my sunning session (really have to get it in early, sun is strong) and I can justify getting on the computer.

That's it, folks. Sorry, just the same-old...at least so far.

Oh, yeah, I chewed the gum for a total of about an hour. Not sure, because there is no clock in the bedroom.

### Lahelada — 2015-06-11T18:27:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-167630>

I tried it too. Can't say much difference,more layered thoughts shooting off in all directions at once. You can see it in my diary as I wqs writing under the influence  Lol about the cool kids .
Oh forgot I owe you an explanation about the rafter . A rafter is a collection of turkeys. As you can see my knowledge of country life is limited as I thought your guinea fowl were turkeys.....lol but a group of guinea fowl is called a mob,rasp or confusion of guinea fowl. Thought it might add a "dirt road cred" to your rep as a country dweller

### Lahelada — 2015-06-11T18:32:51+0300

Ah,did you cut yours in half ? I used the 2mg one as was. Also I had it after a BAB and after coffee and it did give me a buzz. I left 20 minutes between the coffee and gum though as caffeine limits uptake of nicotine.

### Sue-UK — 2015-06-11T18:34:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-167633>

Sally I get a completely different response if I use it outside ..... Day 1 I sat in bed first thing with the gum .... nothing interesting to report. Day 2 I did the same, nothing, so had a second one mid morning, just before I had to walk across the field to one of the outbuildings. I went barefoot ... looked up at the sky and wow ... sky bluer.... the clouds amazing ... colours more vibrant .... Last couple of days I've got up and gone straight out to CT at the same time as getting the first morning sun, I drink some well water, get in the bath and sit and chew the gum whilst I am in there. Although the water is getting warmer, it feels colder than it should do..

My n=1 is that its as if the predominant sense I am using at the time (or at least very soon after) gets a boost, but if I'm not using them actively I don't notice much. I'm going to try chewing the gum whilst reading a blog .....

### nonchalant — 2015-06-15T01:39:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-168153>

I've been continuing the N. gum. Only thing that I've noticed are the small skin sores near my elbows, same thing niacin/niacinamide give me. If they get bad, I'll stop for a while.

### Sue-UK — 2015-06-15T10:49:32+0300

I'm still having a morning gum, but only in the CT bath and I think I'll know when to stop using it at that time. Energy in the evening seems a bit better, and sleep good....

I do actually have one thing to report, as I'm sitting here chewing it. I have noticed that I'm a little tipsy! The effect seems to be growing each day. This morning first thing I got my cold glass of water and went outside for a bit of sun. Kneeled in the grass and did some more weed trimming. Then I came inside and got my gum. Thanks, Sue, I might try it at night. Actually last night we just came back from a 2 day hot trip in a bouncy truck without air conditioning. It's my opinion that I have difficulty sleeping when my energy is low.

### Sue-UK — 2015-06-16T10:21:05+0300

If I'm not having vinegar in a prepared dish, I'm having a little in water before the meal.

The evening gum and glass of red wine is still going well. It's as if the nicotine amplifies the relaxation/alcohol signals from the wine and one glass is enough. ### yewwei.tan — 2015-06-16T11:57:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-168364>

Just some personal observations ....

I literally get high on Nicotine, and start giggling like a schoolgirl at every positive stimulus  (Warning to all Cruise participants ). Brain starts working very well, and mental clarity is very good (I can write software very well during this period).

The effects on my body are "negative", in that I lose endurance exercise capacity. Peak strength is fine, coordination is perfectly fine (I can tumble around and still find a center of balance easily), but I will get gassed much quicker than normal.

Some people probably steal electrons from the periphery to give to the brain when under the influence of nicotine. I guess that's a good thing, but as usual, precaution is advised.

I have also never felt any addiction to Nicotine at all (a topic that was discussed in the June 2015 Q&A). I have taken 15-20mg for a full week, and then not take any nicotine for a week afterward with zero cravings.

Note: I have not been drinking Coffee for the last 4 months, and I do not feel like I want coffee at all -- this was another topic covered in the June 2015 Q&A, whereby a correlation was drawn between "caffeine-philic" and having more nicotine-related side effects.​

Contrast that to one of my friends, who feels great with 2mg of nicotine at one go, but gets a headache with a one-shot 4mg dose.

Very individualised dose-response to nicotine that's for sure. I think we'll never more community stories to determine if one should be using it, and to what degree.

(For completeness, these days I would probably use anywhere from 4-10mg on a typical day. But I get a lot of sun everyday too ...)

.....

### nonchalant — 2015-07-06T18:00:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-171157>

Last night I shocked DH. I went to bed soon after sunset, but instead of a dark bedroom, I turned on the lights and laid there for about 15 minutes in the amber light. "Honey, do you want me to turn off the light for you?"

I noticed a distinct sleepiness just before I turned off the lights for the night.

Something I finally realized today -- we have our best solar panels in the palms of our hands and soles of our feet. Really helpful, for the most distal parts of the body. Well, those and the fingernails and toenails.

### nonchalant — 2015-07-07T21:31:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-171284>

Eggshells are rich in calcium and hydrogen carbonate. And lined with a collagen membrane.
Since getting my guinea hens, I've been feeding their eggshells back to them, along with oyster shell. I've been rinsing and drying the shells in the sunshine before crushing them. They should be soaking up the UV. I wonder if the noon sun gives the shells too much exposure to UV, frying the collagen crystals? Skipping the rinse would probably be better, if ants and flies don't become a problem.

Not that the birds need more UV light. They absolutely adore sunbathing for hours in the hottest part of the day, stretching out their wings and taking dust baths. Nice to have birds that don't pant in the Texas heat.

Oh, and they just adore the old fish I found at the bottom of the freezer.

### nonchalant — 2015-07-12T17:21:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-171753>

Yesterday I caught my right hand in the car door. Fortunately I had ice on hand, which made it bearable. When I got home I used some more cold, but the hand was barely usable. I had developed a swollen area on the back of the hand, with a small dark spot on the palm opposite of that swollen spot. My thumb was also sore. I had to elevate the hand to relieve pain.

Later I got out my trusty LED lamp for exposure to my back, but I also put that hand near the light. I was amazed when afterwards my right hand could help when I squeezed a wet washcloth. Nice! Such a quick improvement! Closer to bedtime, I gave it more red light, and iced the hand for bedtime. This morning it got more red light, and I went outside to ground it -- I watered the grass and flowers in the morning sunshine. At first that hand was only able to carefully hold onto the hose while my left squirted the water around, but soon I tried the right hand. My thumb didn't do too good at first, but in no time it gained strength. By the time I was finished, that thumb was strong and could spray the water far.  I should get more ice for it soon.

Right now the swelling is still there, with a small nick in the skin on top, and I can make a fist. No discoloration yet. I don't see the small dark spot on the palm today.
Have I mentioned that I love my water well? And my LED light? And ice? And the incredible sunshine?
I haven't used any conventional "medicine" on my hand, and I don't think I will.

### nonchalant — 2015-07-30T18:48:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-173538>

I have occasionally experienced a phenomenon of "vibration" since I started using the UFO light. It seems to happen early in the morning, a little before sunrise, or in the evening, an hour or two after sunset. If I am in bed, and close to being asleep, it feels like half of my body is quivering or vibrating. It's a pleasant feeling, and lasts for 5-10 minutes.

It reminds me of the "smoke rings" I used to experience in my eyes, which were a much slower vibration. But I haven't seen those for a while now, perhaps because I finally got blackout shades for the bedroom in this country house. (Left my other ones in the city house.) I still sometimes see a fluttering effect when coming inside to a dark room after being outside in the sunshine.

It also reminds me of the vibration effect I got the first time I used a grounding sheet, but that was only one time, and it lasted for hours, not minutes.

Possibly this "UFO vibration" effect is caused by our window A/C though, lol.

### nonchalant — 2015-08-08T00:34:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-174214>

I just got my copy of "Life on the Edge" by McFadden & Al-Khalili, and read the introduction. I am reminded of a phenomenon I imagined or experienced this spring. We had weeks and weeks of wet cloudy weather, and I was often disappointed that my AM sunbathing was dimmed by hazy skies. Often I had just a few minutes of sunshine before it got cloudy (solar heating created the haze), and if I took too long to get outside, I was disappointed.

However, if I got outside just as the haze was starting, and I looked at the sunshine, within a minute or two it would clear up. I could sunbathe for 20 minutes or so in full sunshine before the clouds finally rolled in and it got cold. This happened many times, but only if I was outside. If I ran inside to get my blanket, happy to see sunshine, I would find cloudy skies when I got back outside. So of course I figured the sun was shining to reward my attention.  I learned to skip the blanket.

Yes, I have a big ego.  But I noticed this in the previous year as well. Well, at least I'm consistent! Did my measurement of the sunshine lead to a sunny period in an otherwise completely cloudy day? So many times I've read of solar or lunar eclipses that were obscured from telescopes until just right before the event. Coincidences, I suppose. And what about those old civilizations that would gather large numbers of people to worship the sun at sunrise? Were they measuring the quantum potentials of the changing atmosphere and collapsing the waveform into a sunny sky, at least for a while?

Stop staring at me in disbelief! Ok, I better hush now...

Found this today:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?posts/189264/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?posts/189264/)

### caroline — 2015-08-08T09:38:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-174227>

You are awesome Sally ...... for so many reasons!

### nonchalant — 2015-08-21T17:41:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-175295>

Nice change of weather the past couple days. Gone are the 100F temps, and we got RAIN! Over an inch!!!
Grounding on damp soil is so much better than that hard dry stuff. I could feel the tingling again in my lower legs. And I kept up my energy levels without direct sunshine by grounding in the rain, the N. gum, and by using the red light followed immediately with cold packs.

But my use of the gum abruptly changed with the change in weather. I had previously noticed that I didn't want the gum in the morning, and I'd wait until near noon to have my first piece. But as summer ground on, I started wanting more and more. I was taking 4mg doses, and sometimes I'd chew it 5x day! But that was before the temps cooled. Now 2mg doses are sufficient, and I want it less often. Good thing, because my TMJ is finally getting a rest!

### fitness@home — 2015-08-21T19:30:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-175302>

Blessed rain has been wonderful. The trees are loving it!

### nonchalant — 2015-09-06T17:42:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-176607>

Love my new suntan spray. So reasonably priced and convenient! I've been laying out in the morning sun as usual, but a few spritzes of this new stuff seems to be working marvels. The niacin gum is wonderful for energy, but it took a little magnesium sulfate to really kick things in gear, though I've been eating plenty of sulfated foods for a long time. I just put about a tablespoon of epsom salt into a small spray bottle and fill with well water. Occasionally during the day I spray some more on, if my skin is feeling warm in the sunshine. Of course the water cools the skin, but I think I feel the sulfur helping as well, because the cooling effect lasts for a while.

Don't know if I'm losing weight, but it sure looks like the fat is starting to disappear.  Love the light physics, Jack, bring it on! I still want to know how silver solution changes skin optics.

### nonchalant — 2015-09-07T19:53:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-176680>

A cold? Really? Me?? Excellent time for a biohack!

I haven't had a cold for such a long time. I laughed, the other day, when DH expressed concern that I drank after him from the water bottle. He had a cold, but I knew I was immune to such stuff. lol.
Soon after the beautiful rosy sunset last night, I had my usual session with the red led light. Then I lay on the bed to read by that same light. After a bit, I started to feel bad, and realized that I had a cold. I turned off the light and went to bed. I kept the runny nose at bay by drinking water. During the night I woke often, but brushing my skin and drinking more water helped. Until I started coughing. Time for some real energy. Nicotine gum stopped it quickly. After a bit I removed it and went back to sleep. That held me for a long time. Next time I woke up I got an ice pack and put it under me. Finally, I awoke at dawn. Sweet, glorious sunshine!

Today I find that my tolerance for the hot sunshine is less, even with the Mg spray, so I've just been going outside for short periods. I also used silver spray in my mouth, up my nose and a tiny amount in one eye. I tell you, the thrill is gone and I just want this over. :/

### nonchalant — 2015-10-15T20:43:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-179603>

Prahlad Jani doesn't pee. The full bladder is a nice resonant chamber. Water forms an EZ next to a hydrophyllic surface. The bladder's inner surface must be able to absorb clean water. The magnetico instantly helped me with an occasional bedwetting problem. About once a month I would wake during a "bathroom" dream and barely make it to the bathroom. Sometimes I wouldn't make it. That stopped completely after installing the magnetico. The augmented magnetism helped my bladder make EZ, which concentrated my urine and left the bladder roomier.

After a year or so, and a move to the country, I stopped having to go during the night. In fact, sometimes I awaken during the night with a fullish bladder, but I just roll over and go back to sleep. I know the problem will go away by itself. Often I'll nonchalantly sip water during the night. ;-)

Magnetism quiets the chaos, which would help the EZ form. Magnetism is also a form of energy, which would enlarge the EZ also, I think.

Additionally, during the day I have noticed that nicotine gum will allow me to put off a bathroom break for a couple hours. So the additional energy augments the process as well. CT has the opposite effect, however, and sunshine also often seems to have the opposite effect for me. Tensegrity seems to work both ways. Vibration from an automobile makes it worse, probably because the frequency is not right.

Awesome news! I've been fortunate too. My dentist was shocked last time when comparing the gum-to-tooth distances. Mine has reversed over the past year. I wore braces in my teens too. Also in this time period, not one filling needed and I only brush 1/day before bed (always soft bristles). More CT to the gums I'm thinking leads to better electromagnetic current to draw in repairing mechanisms for regeneration into the gum tissues--I have a habit of sucking on ice. That's my thought at least.

### nonchalant — 2015-11-28T23:38:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-182789>

I was given an Icy Hot tens unit. I like it, though I use it on a low setting, since I've read Becker. I happened to wash some dishes while using it, and I noticed the pulse sensation seemed to go up a few notches in strength. Ain't grounding great?! The cool tap rinse water gave me the greatest grounding, but the warm soapy water (which I had in a crockery bowl-insulated from the sink) was almost as good. I was a bit surprised at that. While washing a dish above the water, the increased sensation would gradually decrease, until I reached back into the warm water, or I rinsed off the dish. If I kept my hands under the running water, the sensation never diminished.

Then I went outside in the rain and stood in a puddle with the unit on (under clothes). I felt an increase in the sensation, but not quite as strong as I felt while washing dishes. Guess it's because my feet are farther away from my spine? But my arms and legs were getting a bit wet... Need to work on that redox!

So anyway, I think I have a good grounding meter now. Too bad I can't use it while immersed! I need to take it to the beach next...

### nonchalant — 2015-12-09T19:42:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-183806>

A curious thing happened last night. I was shivering, though I wasn't cold.
Let me back up. You see, I had a roller shade malfunction in the middle of the night. My blackout shade raised itself all the way. The tension wasn't set high, so it didn't make too much noise, but I did awaken. I decided to just turn over, away from that window, since we are almost at a new moon, and I still had another, light filtering shade on that window, closer to the glass. All I was getting from it was a glow.

I didn't get back to sleep right away, and after a bit, my back began to shiver. So I pulled up my second sheet. But the shivering didn't stop, so I pulled up the quilt. The room was getting colder; I hadn't realized that the blackout shade insulated that well (we have single-pane windows in our bedroom). Then DH woke up for a bathroom break, and he fixed my shade for me. At last, time for sleep again.

Or not. I was shivering away, under the blanket, though the night wasn't as cool as it has been in the past week. But I noticed my hands and feet were plenty warm. I began to suspect the light from the window, dim as it was, had messed me up. I took me at least two hours to get back to sleep. In contrast, when I first went to bed, the sheets were cold, but I heated them up nicely in about 2 minutes, so that I had to remove everything for a bit, to cool, before falling asleep under one sheet.

So that soft glow from the still somewhat covered window was enough to make my melatonin drop, affect other hormones, and mess up my ability to control body temp, or at least my perception of it. Next time, yeah, I'll get right up and fix it! To think that I used to sleep in there with those old-fashioned shades even during a full moon!

If JK agrees that we once may have been able to accurately sense altitude, sensing pressure and CME's don't sound too far off. I know aboriginal peoples feared northern lights--with good reason, it seems. Good time to hunker down.

### nonchalant — 2015-12-13T21:34:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-184023>

The info we are learning here is so useful in so many ways. We just got a new kitten, Dusty, from a nearby farm. We are keeping it indoors for a few days so he/she learns that this is home now. We'll let it outside soon to face the terrors of our flock of guineas, lol. Perhaps we should wait until it can climb trees quickly? ;-)

Anyway, she has never used a catbox. So, inspired by JK, I went out and dug up some damp sand for the box. When the kitten was placed inside, she plopped down on her side and back and she stretched and played around. Love those electrons! When I dug in the sand with my finger she followed suit, with the predicted results. Success! Yesterday I kept putting her in the box, with pretty good results. Today we tossed out the now smelly sand and put in some regular cat litter. I probably should have gotten plain clay, because the scent was strange to her, but she seems to have the idea fairly set already. This has been the easiest training that I have ever experienced.

I plan on taking him/her outside tomorrow for a bit. We really don't want him to be a housecat. No more neurotic cats for me, thank you very much!

### nonchalant — 2015-12-13T22:24:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-184033>

And one more thing. The morning after we got the kitty, I put her on an eastern windowsill. She looked outside for a while, watching the birds and the leaves moving in the wind. Then she started meowing and looking around the room. So of course I took her to the cat box, where she did her thing. These outdoor kitties have a nice strong circadian rhythm!

### nonchalant — 2015-12-15T21:55:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-184224>

I have been using a Sunlite 365nm LED bulb, for over a week now, during the day and evening. I like it. I feel ... not noticeably more energetic to an observer, but I want to do more. Better mood, doing crafts for Christmas, baking, cleaning house and such. Got all the Christmas cards out early, something that has not happened in many years. Sexy dreams sometimes. Actually, I am more energetic, on cloudy days. No pouting, heh.

It's light is almost unnoticeable during the day, so DH doesn't object. And at just 2 watts, no complaints about the constant electricity use. I do have a slight ringing in my ears from it, though, but the good effects outweigh that, for me. Nothing on the RF meter, but the Trifield does show significant electrical and magnetic field, near the bulb, since the led needs a transformer.

Near the led is printed "Do not look at UV LED", but I glance at it periodically, because I know better.

I really like the sunlite brand lights. I have a black and red....the first night I put the red bulb in our family room lamp I was amazed how everyone wanted to congregate under the blanket of that light....I never got that effect from other red bulbs. The black took me a bit to get used to....it bothered my eyes at first, I was keeping it to the right of me on an end table....I think too much eye exposure. Slightly behind me is good and I like to use it when using my vielight. I have been grossly inconsistent with use, even though the benefits are easily recognizable. I need to prioritize it more. It s just tricky for me to get the light without being sedentary which I try to avoid.

### nonchalant — 2015-12-25T22:39:41+0200

I took my TENS unit to my parent's house in Dallas for Christmas. After helping prepare the feast, I needed some help and put it on. Then I discovered the tap water has good grounding! Nice to know that fluoridated ~crap~ water is good for something.

As usual, my back was soon rehabilitated, and I continued working on cleaning and wrapping gifts.

### nonchalant — 2015-12-28T18:03:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-185044>

The 3 legged stool is much more than sunlight, water & magnetism. It's much more than what we take into our bodies for health... More than getting lots of sunlight and staying dark at night, more than drinking lots of good water, and more than grounding, CT, visiting the crater, or laying on a Magnetico.

The 3 legged stool is also how light infiltrates through our bodies, and how our cells absorb and emit that light and signal with it. It's how water works in our nanotubes and mitochondria. It's how we build our mitochondrial magnetism. It's how all three work together to give us life and health. The light comes in, changing our proteins and DHA and water structure. The changes in those items ends up affecting our magnetism, our direct current, and our light emitted. Each leg of the stool, inside our bodies (as it does in our environment), affects the other two, which in turn affects the others again.

### Jonathin — 2015-12-28T21:25:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-185050>

Nice, Nonchalant. The three legged stool is the simple version of the multi-dimensional tapestry of life.

### nonchalant — 2016-01-07T01:25:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-185585>

2015 saw an improvement in my sleep. First, I gradually stopped having to get up to go to the bathroom during the night. I think that was because of added energy, so that my bladder became more hydrophyllic, and I was able to concentrate urine, instead of having to go. Currently, even in the morning I don't feel too much need to go, until I stand up, lol. I hope to improve further on that.

Also, I stopped sleeping during the day. I haven't felt the need to nap, though I now have plenty of time for it, in many months. A couple times last year I laid down for a nap, but didn't fall asleep. Also I stopped falling asleep while riding in the car for 3-4 hours.

Very recently, in late December and January of this year, I have experienced deep sleep a handful of times! I hate to jinx things by talking about it, but a few times I have fallen asleep very quickly, like in 5 to 20 minutes, and have awoken quite startled that it was dawn already! I don't remember waking at all during the night. I felt like I had just gone to bed, but now it's light and I feel rested. For years now I would usually take an hour or two or even more to fall asleep, and wake several times during the night and take another 20 to 60 minutes to fall asleep again. I'm not sure what I can point to for this wonderful new development. Perhaps it is the UV light? Oh, I know, it's the new kitten that likes to cuddle in our bed on cold nights! Thanks Dusty! ;-)

Last winter our house also got really cold at night, after the fire in the living room stove burned down, but I still experienced a downturn in health because of the low sunlight levels, even with lots of sunbathing on the cold, sunny days and even on the cloudy ones. But this year I haven't had bad back problems or low energy levels. I really like my Sunlite UV bulb.

### TerrierMom — 2016-01-07T01:49:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-185589>

Your sleep improvements are so encouraging and inspiring!! Reading people's successes really makes me want to fight harder to make changes in my own life. Never underestimate the effect sharing your story has on others- a ripple effect you may never know the reach of!

I fall asleep quickly but will wake multiple times in the night, as in, awake! - like at 11:30, 3, 4:30, then the alarm goes off at 5:30 (to give dog her medicine) and I attempt to fall back asleep until 6:30 (Bedtime is 9:30-10:30). I do fall back asleep quickly at these times but am annoyed at waking up in the first place. Sometimes I can blame a dog (who seems to have a 3 am habit of getting up to go outside, and my mama hearing detects this every time and wakes when he stirs). I can count on one hand the number of times in the last month that I have fallen asleep and just woken at dawn without recalling waking up. Those nights are glorious but tend to be dreamless which seems odd. I already black out my bedroom and tightly control blue light after sunset, but only just now ensuring I max out getting my morning UV. With the rain this week I am craving the sun soooooo much. Guess it may be time to get a Sunlite UV bulb as backup for this El Nino winter. The times I can remember sleeping all night are when I did a lot of walking in the sun, like 6-8 miles at a rambling pace (like out shopping, not like out training for a marathon). Was it the walking or the sun? I am guessing sun....

### nonchalant — 2016-01-08T21:20:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-185788>

I'm reading my Van Wijk book now, but it is difficult to make out some of the graphics, while reading at night in my red light. I look back, during the day at what I read previously, and I'm amazed at the red and pink parts of the charts that were invisible the night before. Well, the red parts are just visible, if I hold the page at an angle. An amber light would be better.

Part A was mostly review (I'm surprised Becker wasn't mentioned), but in Part B it is getting interesting! Amazing what they were finding out in the 1920's and 30's about UV emissions in biology. And interesting how that research was suppressed in the 40's in a manner similar to what we still see going on today. Not with arguments, like is usual in any science, but with blitzes of "negative" research results indicating a hidden agenda.

### nonchalant — 2016-01-09T19:03:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-185862>

I gave a weather station to DH for Christmas:
[http://www.amazon.com/Ambient-Weather-WS-YG501-Thermometer-Hygrometer/dp/B004GF7C6A](http://www.amazon.com/Ambient-Weather-WS-YG501-Thermometer-Hygrometer/dp/B004GF7C6A)
We filled the globe barometer with water on a cool day that was partly cloudy. No cold fronts were approaching, so I thought the air pressure would be about average. Sure enough, the water level in the spout would raise and lower periodically. It's fun to watch. Once it even went down to the very bottom of the spout. But after a week or so, the water level was staying very high in the spout, up in the small globe at the top. We had to remove some water so that it wouldn't overflow.

Since then, we've had a variety of weather, sunny days and cold fronts have come through, but the water level is always now way above the level in the reservoir. I wonder if the water has lost the density it had when it first came from the water well, and is more like bulk water. But the globe is glass... Perhaps in another couple weeks we will wick some of the water out.

Since I've been hand-feeding canned mackerel to my only chicken, she has developed some beautiful coloring. Her neck feathers really shine in the sunlight. Her eggs have a glossy coat on the shell exterior and the yolks are fabulous. She is laying 5-6 times per week, though it is winter. And the 30 guineas don't seem to pick on her like they used to. ### nonchalant — 2016-01-27T00:19:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-187432>

Jack recently stressed looking at the EARLY morning sunlight. I have, for the past few months, been sleeping late, getting up only after the sun had been up for an hour. Probably because I no longer have to run to the bathroom. I considered taking some of my leftover hHCG, which popped me out of bed with the sunrise long ago. Instead, I asked DH, who always gets up with the chickens, to open my blackout shades when the sky starts to lighten. That made a difference right away. I'd turn my head upwards and look out the window behind the headboard, and pretty soon I'd feel like getting up and going outside to greet the day.

The past few mornings I've been waking up on time, before DH remembers to open my shades. I open them myself, and then I open the window. I hunker down beneath the covers and stare at the chilly morning blue sky and reddish clouds. Then I see the sun rising between the trees. So lovely. Soon I'm eager to go into the heated kitchen, grab some water, get outside and start the day. DH always fires up the stove to make some coffee, which heats that half of the house, bless him. If it's cloudy, I often have some coffee too.

### Inger — 2016-01-27T15:08:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-city-life.12817/post-187461>

I never cover my open window in the bedroom/horse barn.. so I wake up when it starts to get less dark all the time.... and then I just lie there thinking.. until it gets lighter! It is funny
It is like my body just wake up when the dark fades...... Maybe you need to try to stop covering your windows? Do you have street lights outside? I am so lucky to have it completely dark here... which is such a blessing I think... I just love to wake up watching out the window how it gets lighter.. from my bed  I let the light catch my eyes as it slowly increases

Waking up and watching the light change is magical, isn't it? Just as nice as a good sunset.
