# Thread: Seafood, seafood, seafood!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-seafood-seafood.12933/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 48
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2015-02-15T02:28:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-seafood-seafood.12933/post-155660>

Jk has pretty much drilled home the importance of oysters and seafood.....

I am wondering a couple of things.....

\- Have you always enjoyed seafood?
\- How do you generally prepare it?
\- Did you eat seafood growing up?

\- if you hated it before ..... are you starting to enjoy it? Did we hate it because we were made to eat it once a week? maybe it was a religious thing?

\- do you only eat it because you know it is good for you?

\- What is your favorite seafood? if money is no object what would you buy?

\- do you worry about mercury????

Dr. K. will be speaking for Roy Palmer in NOLA in a couple of weeks - hopefully he can share some stuff with us.... when he stops partying at mardi gras of course!

### Inger — 2015-02-15T08:19:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-seafood-seafood.12933/post-155667>

We did not had much seafood at all.... cause we were poor and animal flesh was rare... but I craved it! We sometimes had salted herring and I still remjeber how crazy good it was... and I craved cod liver oil. We just got one spoonful each morning but I had weird cravings of wanting to drink the whole bottle......
I guess my body really needed seafood and I had way too little of it.
I have always liked it.
I do not worry one bit about mercury
I eat it raw.
My favorite seafood is.... oysters... plain and raw. Au naturale. They are super sexy

### Jenny S — 2015-02-15T09:21:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-seafood-seafood.12933/post-155669>

Until started following Jack last year only ever ate white fish with no flavour like whiting or tuna about once a week.
Now I eat (& like) sardines & anchovies often which I didn't think I could do - mind over matter.
Had mussels for lunch for first time today - didn't enjoy but edible.
Tried oysters couple of times but struggled to get them down raw. Must try Caroline's idea of lightly grilling - sounds better but plan on learning to like them raw.
I now love canned red salmon & eat lots. Easy preparation - none!
Bought tin of mackerel last week but still unopened - just can't seem to bring myself to eat that for unknown reasons
I do try & only have lowest mercury fish.
We were brought up in Port Fairy & my Dad was keen fisherman, but none of my brothers or sisters or Mum would eat anything except for the white stuff. Probably because that's what Mum ate & cooked for us. Sad bit is Dad had cray pots in sea all the time but he gave most crags away as he was only one that would eat them - if only I had all that available now so fresh & free! Maybe if had eaten that then would be healthier now.

### Lahelada — 2015-02-15T11:26:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-seafood-seafood.12933/post-155674>

I never liked it ,still don 't. We lived in tbe middle of. Germany so fish while not off was a smelly affair. I was a picky eater anyway.
I do have strange notions like when contemplating Yew's seafood pics from Japan but I think the like is an artistic thing.

My favourite if I can say that is white fish broiled with salt and pepper. Raw oyster is okay with me,lemon juice
spoils it. Octopus was best raw,cooked with garlic-yuck. Oh there is sometbing I do like Calamari but as breaded is a nono...
I don't worry about mercury.

### Lahelada — 2015-02-15T11:29:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-seafood-seafood.12933/post-155675>

PS: If money was no object I would engineer myself a DHA in the sn-2 position fish that tastes of pizza....failing that wild heavily smoked Scottish Salmon.

### yewwei.tan — 2015-02-15T12:57:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-seafood-seafood.12933/post-155679>

Yay, time to dump some of the pics from my Dec-2014 Japan trip.

Best Mackerel ever .....

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Would eat these little suckers every single damn day if I could get them in Australia

Hnnnngg!!

I'll honestly say that I wish I could eat this every day , combo of Raw oysters, raw marinated pollack roe, raw salt-fermented fish liver (Shiokara)

\-----

So yeah, I've always loved seafood. Haven't found any seafood that I don't like (regardless of raw or method of preparation). Even as a child I'd love cod liver oil and oysters . Fermented salted fish is awesome!

Actually come to think of it, there isn't any naturally occurring food that I wouldn't mind eating .... including any fermented stinky nonsense you can possibly find. So I'm just a glutton for any food in general . Probably stems from being exposed to that sort of food as a child, since fermented condiments, strange herbal tonics, foods with weird textures (eg: fish maw or jellied fish), were all part of the dinner table.

\----

Mercury would be a concern if I were eating lots of predator fish like:

-   large Tuna -- Bluefin or Yellowfin
-   Spanish Mackerel (any other mackerel is fine)
-   Whale or Shark

Other than those, all other fish are fine, including "large" fish like Cod or Salmon.

\----

Well, from the pictures above, it's obvious that money would solve the problems that I have; can't get that sort of seafood in Australia

Favourites amongst the Aussie seafood:

-   Coffin bay oysters
-   Salmon (NZ King is best, but the large Tassie ones are pretty damn good too)
-   Any medium greasy mackerel
-   Sardines!!!!
-   Red Snapper
-   Cuttlefish (like squid, but smaller and more tender)
-   Tuna heads (only the heads!!)

If I could ask for non-Aussie seafood that isn't from Japan

-   Female Sri-lankan Crabs with roe
-   Norwegian Mackerel
-   Grouper Heads (the eyes!!!!! )

\----

But well, for now it's lots of canned mackerel for me .....

(can of woolworths mackerel, smash it up, add any number of herbs I can find, always add a tablespoon of turmeric, always have lots of chilli sauce, wrap with seaweed and eat)

.....

### Valerie — 2015-02-15T15:15:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-seafood-seafood.12933/post-155681>

Parents were vegetarian. Did not eat (chick-beef) meat till about 18 yo. Discovered fish in my mid-forties and just started eating shrimp about 3 weeks ago. Have yet to try a raw oyster.

All my seafood is cooked generally in some type of sauce (curry, etc) or in a fish cake recipe (highly concentrated with fish). Learned not to overcook fish. Dislike smelly fish.

Less concerned about mercury than before (selective on fish types eatten)

I force myself to eat more seafood because I know it is good for me.

Accepted the fact I have to spend more on high quality food if I want to maintain my health so that I can keep working.

### Martin — 2015-02-15T15:59:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-seafood-seafood.12933/post-155682>

I remember trying to eat more fish as a teen to try and get good nutrition. Bought a fillet of flounder and broiled it in the oven for an hour at about 9000 degrees cuz I was afraid of "undercooked" seafood. Silly moi. Now anything seafood related is fair game. Half the appeal is knowing what an excellent evolutionary package it is! And the flavor... wow!

practicing dinner plate-licker,

NSH

### fitness@home — 2015-02-15T16:57:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-seafood-seafood.12933/post-155683>

Grew up on the Great Lakes and always loved walleye and perch. My parents never had any ocean seafood on the dinner table except canned tuna . Mom hated the smell of fish.

Didn't get exposure to most other seafood until I moved to Florida in the late 80s.

My freezer is currently stocked with salmon, gulf shrimp, cod, tuna, crab legs, and Mahi mahi . We get our oysters at local seafood restaurant on a regular basis. My favorite meal...

### cinnamon — 2015-02-15T22:10:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-seafood-seafood.12933/post-155707>

We sometimes had frozen fish sticks (mystery white fish, processed and breaded, of course) or "fish and chips" battered fillets (usually haddock or halibut) when I was growing up. The best part (to my child mind) was the french fries and ketchup that were served with either of these.  There was usually green peas too, but I was pretty indifferent to those.

I remember Mom making salmon sandwiches from canned salmon and mayonnaise. I definitely must have tried them - I have a memory of not liking the texture of the little bones. I think I stood by and said "Yuck!" when she made these sandwiches for herself! LOL

And once in a blue moon, we would order in chinese food. This happened probably only 1-2 times per year, so it was pretty special. There was shrimp there - well a tiny bit almost overwhelmed by deep fried batter! But yum, I did like those.

In my adult life, I identified myself as someone who did not like seafood. I rarely ate any, and had exactly none during the 13 years or so that I was pretty much vegan. My poor brain!

[I ate my first oyster](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/share-your-success-of-today.668/#post-82655) not even two years ago. In my mind, it was going to be icky. But I loved it!  Now, I do eat oysters, though not often enough. I am not that good at shucking. Last time I bought a big box (33) of Malpeques (PEI) and ended up finishing them off as breakfast appetizers over about 3 days. I laid several of them out on a cookie sheet and then put them into a hot oven just until the shells opened. Then I dumped the warm liquor and the oysters into a bowl and ate it up with a spoon. With just a drop of lemon juice, it was very yummy!  I still ate my usual bacon and eggs after.

I eat mostly shrimp, salmon and sardines. I'm not ready for a MHS yet, but I tried some canned mackerel this week and really liked it, so will add some more of that in. Oh, and lobster tails are nice!

### nicld — 2015-02-16T00:35:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-seafood-seafood.12933/post-155721>

Started off with tuna casserole, fish sticks and salmon patties with saltines., YUK!!!

Now fresh salmon, cooked and raw, raw oysters (when I can get them) and even cooked canned oysters. Have always liked tuna, just not warm. Did have lobster tails last night and oh so good.

### endless — 2015-02-16T02:29:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-seafood-seafood.12933/post-155729>

I don't remember eating seafood as a small child, but as san older (and very poor) child I remember eating a fair amount of sardines. As a teen I would eat fish sticks and McDonalds fish sandwiches. I graduated to full on seafood lover in my 20's with prawn souvlaki and it's just increased from there. When I lived in Vancouver I ate sushi frequently. Now I have wild salmon, sardines and mussels often, the occasional MHS, but will eat pretty much anything from the sea if it is available. Sea urchin isn't my favourite....

### caroline — 2015-02-16T02:29:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-seafood-seafood.12933/post-155730>

This is so interesting to read all your replies - thanks so much!

So it doesn't seem to matter that much if we grew up inland or on the ocean?

We mostly had pre conceived notions that fish was smelly and we didn't like it???

But I am guessing if our parents had loved it and it was well prepared we would of been okay with it?

if you cook fish ...do you worry that it smells up the house?

### JMO — 2015-02-16T02:32:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-seafood-seafood.12933/post-155732>

We didn't have seafood growing up...just frozen fish sticks and salmon patties during lent. Mom hated fishy food so didn't even taste shrimp til I was in high school. DH ate a lot more seafood than I did and introduced me to crab, shrimp, and other seafood. He ate herring and anchovies as a kid and acquired a love for those. Since epi-paleo we get at least a case of oysters a week and eat them daily. Also stop into the oyster bar prob once at week when they have oyster Wed. We also have wild salmon once at week and shrimp and scallops often. I like to have canned crab omelet for breakfast. Lunch is usually canned salmon or sometimes canned sardines but I don't really like them. I smash them and add some raw vinegar and get them down. Lately if I get carb cravings, I open a can of anchovies and that takes away the urge to eat bad.

### caroline — 2015-02-16T02:36:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-seafood-seafood.12933/post-155734>

Sushi shops are springing up like crazy ...so most folks seem to love it....is it because it is in a small manageable package? and someone else makes it for us?

I have tried a lot more things from the sushi shops than I ever would have before ...and we have found a very nice Japanese restaurnant about 15 minutes from us.

I had eel there the other day and loved it! Thanks to Yew for introducing me to it! - actually I think he made me try quite a few things I hadn't gotten around to0!!
I really never thought I would ever eat eel!! altho my DH grew up on the east coast of Canada and always talked about cod tongues and cheeks and eel!
I really thought he must be joking! of course now I know that he was the smart one...

### caroline — 2015-02-16T09:59:14+0200

soooo - some things we have learned so far........

\- don't bake fish at 9000 degrees

\- Nicole can afford lobster tails for dinner!

\- frozen fish stix were in most of our past

\- Fitness has a freezer full of yummy seafood ...address????

\- can of mackerel ...cheap and good - really good in fact with lots of fresh herbs and chillies and hot sauce!

\- Lah is going to engineer a fish that tastes like pizza! it will enter the sn- 2 position and be absolutely perfect!

\- Martin licks his dinner plate!

Is anyone catching their own fish? besides Dali - who takes a fishing rod when he goes to the ocean to do C/T

### Linz — 2015-02-16T11:05:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-seafood-seafood.12933/post-155766>

Many holiday memories of catching small brown trout and cooking them over a bonfire with any natural implement we could find or taking them home to fry in oatmeal. Same with prawn/shrimps from the sea but not so easy to find by the shore now.

Not such happy memories of mackerel - too many futile hours sitting in a wallowing boat feeling a bit ill and an occasional glut of fish we didn't want to finish. Jellyfish stings from the lines. Just holidays - for the rest of the year it was mostly meat, chicken and veg plus bread, sugar etc with less frequent white fish, smoked salmon or canned tuna/sardine sandwiches.

My children were always much braver than me about shellfish. I'll never forget the expression of my 5 yo dd's teacher when I told her dd missed school because she had eaten a bad oyster. Now regular family fights over the last oyster!

### SeaHorse — 2015-02-16T15:15:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-seafood-seafood.12933/post-155779>

My husband stocked our pond with rainbow and brown trout a few years ago….this summer we got of few and they were huge….and tasty.

Twenty years ago, one winter in Newfoundland we had a freezer full of cod that we had gotten ourselves, a lamb and a pig that we bartered for…..and no money to buy much of anything else, I remember people laughing at us with our "luxury" meals…meaning all proteins and no carbs and extras. That was a good winter and was never repeated again until recently because we thought it was a sign of poverty! Now that we have more resources, we're back to freezers full of fish, lamb and pork with the added bonus of FAT and regular injections of oysters.

Growing up we ate a lot of fish in the summer in New England as my father was a dedicated fisherman and loved to cook…I never minded the gutting and that has served me well as I've gone on to explore and eat a variety of animal and fish offal and "parts" without a big yuck factor. But, like a lot of us, fish fingers were a staple outside of fishing season. Heinz chilli sauce was our ketchup.

In Newfoundland traditionally lobster was used for bait….many people still think of it as a poverty food and won't eat it for cultural reasons….that's changing as younger generations are perceiving the "surf and turf" glamour of steak houses etc. Locavores are also big boosters of eating all the variety of fish/shellfish that is available…but still most of the interesting stuff is shipped to Asia.

One of my favourite fish dishes is simple….any fish covered in a lattice of bacon, lots of pepper and baked at 350 until done….works every time.

I was in Montreal recently and came back with a jar of whelks…..anybody have any ideas of ways to use them? (I could just try the bacon again…)

### JoeBranca — 2015-02-16T21:45:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-seafood-seafood.12933/post-155811>

Funny, when epi-paleo came out I already had quite an affinity for seafood and shellfish, so I simply turned it up to 11 a few years ago. My father came from a Sicilian seaside community in Gloucester, MA, his father being a deep sea fisherman who had come from a fishing village in Sicily. Needless to say, appreciation for fresh seafood runs in the family. Though I grew up in Arizona and southern California in a large family so seafood was a rarer special occasion, but for instance when we visited Gloucster the lobsters crabs, urchin, clams and shrimp were ON. My father has a story from childhood that my grandfather supposedly brought home a live **~12lb** lobster from one of his deep sea outings.

My favorite seafood has always been live urchin straight out of the shell, close runner up is spooning the tomalley out of the front of a steamed lobster's cavity. The only item I couldn't get into was fermented sea cucumber, but that was quite a while ago when I tried it, I'd try it again.

Mercury's orbit does not intersect earth's orbit so it does not concern me much.

I miss pizza a lot but instead of going for the real thing I make the low carb one with the cream cheese, eggs, parmesan cheese and sometimes put 1 or 2 T almond flour(good without too). I'll do the traditional one with red sauce and lots of meats, red pepper, olives and the kids even like it. It's good and pretty much taste like reg pizza (you can pick it up). Sometimes I make the seafood one with Alfredo sauce (raw cream, butter, and Parmesan) and use crab, scallops, and shrimp. It satisfies the pizza urge and prob not as bad.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-02-17T05:13:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-seafood-seafood.12933/post-155836>

We've got a big problem with the rest of the country thinking all seafood in the world is tainted. It's a belief that is not true. Th saddest reality from EE4 not too many have commented upon: 90% of the seafood present in 1950 is now extinct. This is why I need to speak for Mr. Palmer. Our best Rx is almost gone..............You have to live somewhere where that situation is going to fight that reality because of their culture.......The water captured in our cells is like a window in an aquarium.........which makes us the seafood in mother nature's game of life.

I wonder why so many people hate seafood? I have a bit of a theory but that is all it is - a theory. Here goes-
Seafood is really the only "wild" food supply in most peoples diet. (unless you hunt)
Because it is so healthy it goes bad VERY FAST because the bacteria also want those nutrients. As a result it starts to smell fast which turns people off of it.

What do ya think?

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-02-23T05:05:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-seafood-seafood.12933/post-156342>

I'm kind of new to this seafood thing as well but I have been eating I'd say a can of either sardines, oysters or herring on work days for several years because I knew that the omega 3's are good for brain health however, honestly, I haven't really noticed any real improvement mentally BUT - there have been a few days in the last few months where I felt like my brain was just running great! Things that would normally be overwhelming were such a breeze to accomplish and the work day was like nothing!
On those days I usually ate more servings of seafood than normal but it is probably more than just that.
I've had quite a bit of seafood in the last 36 hours or so and I can't say I feel any better than normal.
I think Jack would say "the more seafood the better" and his logic behind it is good.
The DHA in seafood is HUGE for the brain and you can only get it in seafood. DHA is the reason that we have a large brain in the first place so seafood is crucial. Plus it is highly anti-inflammatory.
I like using the term I heard from Dave Asprey: It will still move your needle in the right direction even if you don't feel anything. (not his exact words but similar)

### Sandra — 2015-02-24T12:49:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-seafood-seafood.12933/post-156462>

We always had fish on Friday's as kids - usually tuna mornay from canned tuna. We were quite poor, not a lot of luxuries, but lived on a farm so had free range meat of all kinds. Special occasions saw us have prawn cocktails, but rarely did we eat a fillet of real fish. I remember those fake seafood sticks and scallops ... makes me puke now thinking about it. I didn't eat a lot of fish since leaving home, partly because of the price and partly because I always used to stuff up cooking it (and there was no way back then you could convince me to eat it raw!). It took Nick buying me raw oysters to even try them, and now I don't mind them at all. I eat sashimi about once a week, but would love to have it more often - actually I think I will now that we have an awesome fish shop that's opened up in our suburb with sashimi grade tuna and salmon at a fraction of the price it is in a sushi shop. My husband and kids don't like fish much at all and turn their noses up if I cook a piece ... I don't mind - I just keep cooking. I've tried a few recipes to get them to eat it, and although my husband will tolerate it, it's so hard getting a child to eat something when they just don't want to or have totally talked themselves out of it. I'll keep trying ... one day they will get it!

# Thread: Impressions soleil levant
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1267
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Paleodocteur — 2015-02-15T19:48:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-155693>

Impression Soleil Levant (Sunrise Impressions)

It is the title of Monet’s first exhibited painting which figure a morning sun peering through the foggy waters of a seaport.

The painting initially was not well received. The critics sarcastically mocked the loose unfinished brushstrokes and used its name to ridicule the group of painters who later became known as the Impressionists. They where aiming to capture Nature’s Law. Monet was passionate by the elements of changing light and color perception.

An impression can be defined as a printed picture but it is also a thought , a fragile intuition, a feeling.

I happen to experience most of my impressions in the morning, either in bed or in the rising sunlight. Alas, they often evaporate with the dew.

### Paleodocteur — 2015-02-15T19:49:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-155694>

Lately, I decided to try to render them a little more solid so they would stop escaping me. So I grabbed my old brushes and started to paint.

With large strokes on big paper I tried to realize the visions. The funny surprise was that by materializing the knowledge, I had to leave the scientific literature aside and my freed mind started to build.

The simple act of focusing on the brush movement, enabled my intuition to connect things and make sense of some of the concepts/colors that laid here and there in the cognitive impressionist Quilt.

### Paleodocteur — 2015-02-15T19:51:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-155695>

I thought I would start a thread where I can upload ongoing thoughts that are not really related to a current blog. I have been following the forum since the beginning but seldom expressed my own opinion. Part of it is shyness, translation problems, personality trait, grain brain, lack of DAYtime, dopamine crush, or the feeling of running behind so I HAD to try to catch up with the ongoing subject by reading even more.

Compelled by _curiosity_ I have been reading blogs and commentaries and links and abstracts and articles and papers and papers and books and other books. This never-ending marathon leaves me with a sense that, “yes, I gained much more knowledge, that’s for sure, but my paper-bulimia didn’t lead me to a firm understanding of the world.” It ‘s foggy, cloudy at best. There are big shadows of doubt, fears. I do see a glimmer of light over the horizon. As me 6 year old son says “In the darkness, there is a little light.”

Each of your testimonies feel like sunlight to me and I feel indebted to some of you for your courage to follow the protocols, the insight you give us. Now is my time to share.

### Paleodocteur — 2015-02-15T19:53:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-155696>

As for my thread, the picture quality may vary but it’s less about their appearance and more about what it means or what came up in my mind when the paintbrush passed there. They are freshly made from that week or that day.

Don’t hesitate to post comments…especially if I seem to go the wrong way with concepts as I am not sure I can afford to wander too much. Unfortunately, I most likely won’t answer for the reasons listed above, but I’ll read you and think about it.

I haven’t opened my OJ yet. This is similar, since you can read my mind just by looking at my posts.

Beware that pictures can be more sticky than words and somehow a beautiful picture can be awfully wrong.

### Paleodocteur — 2015-03-29T13:37:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-159724>

2 years ago the initial measuring were awful, kind of like sleeping on a cellPhone looking for a base. Over the range of the reader,
like a regular in town measuring….
ELFs where not good , by my chair at work, and on my side of the bed , by my head.

### Paleodocteur — 2015-03-29T13:58:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-159726>

So we moved ... and we moved… and we moved again…
This place looked decent on the EHF side , with a little garden. to ground and sun bath, at last.
But there was a problem . I could feel the ELFs, and there were not the kind you can get rid of by turning off the switch or the fuse or the board.
It became apparent in winter evenings that the source of that field was the power line on the street . I know it isn't much ( 150-80nT) , but enough to bother and keep worrying cause nobody really knows what is the limit.
It decreases a bit while going away from the line. Although it doesn't follow square law reduction , so I guess there some kind of induction on the house frame work.
I get son to sleep as far away as I can . But I worry.
DW, got fed up to sleep in living room so we moved to the front bedroom . I must say sleep good there. Tinnitus arising in the evening though...

### Paleodocteur — 2015-04-02T17:20:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-160139>

I thought then you have a densely packed proton filled egg where mitochondrias would love to go swim and gobble whatever protons are swirling around there… and prosper and shine some IR…

### Paleodocteur — 2015-04-02T18:31:17+0300

likely their charges will launch them to the surface

### Paleodocteur — 2015-04-02T18:53:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-160145>

Where they would cross the positively charges descending ones….attracted by the nucleus?
Like mitos are?
When mitochndrias sare happy they release IR and protons for free, increasing the EZ increasing energy.
but like piezo-psychrophiles they need the right pressure and temperature, so they can adapt their size, shape…. and distance to their center of gravity, the nucleus.

Or nucleosome?

That will also mediate what kind of REDOX couple for them to use… the easiest being EZ… but I guess you need a little oxidative burst to jumpstart that process..

### Paleodocteur — 2015-04-02T19:09:05+0300

Anytime a difference of tension happens they would adapt to it.
It would happen on every ebe and flow of the Schumann wave , and day and night and days and nights and food and starve and moons and seasons and….

### Paleodocteur — 2015-04-02T19:10:44+0300

But I guess when you dimerize , you change the EZ around your protein, it changes charge, and you double your mass…
and then it sinks….
to the nucleus….
where on a side of an euchromatic groove it finds a tune it likes.
"Lets play that jukE-Box" ...

### Jack Kruse — 2015-04-02T19:43:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-160151>

Current flows positive to neg under the ionosphere.........but above it is goes both ways. And then there is the effect of semiconduction within a plasma state............the direction of the current is a huge factor as Becker discovered in bone. When you see a reverse bias current you know a semiconductor LED is involved with a light source at some level because the Hall effect will be found if you look for it. He did and found it.

### Paleodocteur — 2015-04-06T20:52:42+0300

So I wonder if that had any meaning at all.
Those proton filled vesicles transport their neurotransmitter cargo to the synaptic cleft. But how do they get there?
They travel from the perikaryon to the synapse along the MICROTUBULE, like our good mitos.
They see to be temporary restrained in an actin web for storage.
That little bubble is all quietly bathed in tight water warmed by IR releasing mitos.
Until depolarization rocks this little world .
Or so I thought

### nonchalant — 2015-04-09T22:27:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-160752>

> [Paleodocteur said:](/goto/post?id=160307)
>
> Or almost full circle?
> [View attachment 1699](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/1699/)

Yes, bent into a circle, but also flat, so that lots of tightly-packed DHA becomes a lattice, so we get those quantum waves like on water surface. The Crawford et al, article quoted in Ubi8 has a great pic, Figure 8. To me it looks like the magnetic fields from that "Broken Magnet" video, where he used a plastic film to reveal the jumble of fields. Wish I could find that video again. The DHA molecule looks like alternating positive and negative fields. If you can imagine a flexible cam shaft from an automobile bent into a circle, that is what it looks like to me.

According to that article, DPA, with one less set of double bonds, cannot be made into a planar structure, so it has high electrical resistance. So DPA fails miserably at the quantum game.

### Paleodocteur — 2015-04-12T20:19:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-160997>

I hate friday evenings
And that peppery feeling
on my face when we go to bed.
Everyone else must be on the line...
You quote Feynman
"fill your heart with love
not with science"
How will I know then
if we must move again?
Our bodies resting in a 2mG alternative field
right below or above the limit of what the Swedes deem safe.
Are we tinkering some enzymes?
Are we splitting some photons?
I have so many questions…
My mad brain can't let it go.
I hate fridays and I love you.

### Paleodocteur — 2015-05-03T15:52:28+0300

### Paleodocteur — 2015-05-03T15:55:25+0300

What exactly happens to the atoms? How are the orbital’s rearranging? Do Fe III become Fe II in a synchronized glimpse. Do the electrons come from within the rock? Is the light released from electrons falling from the paramagnetic Fe d-shell to O2 sigma shell?

### Paleodocteur — 2015-05-03T15:59:48+0300

### Paleodocteur — 2015-05-03T16:09:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-163164>

The neuro-shaman may have tried some plants or other early biophotons technics , she was grounded, the skull bone was regrowing. But the wound was too deep.

And she had not received her tetanus shot.

### Paleodocteur — 2015-05-03T16:13:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-163165>

It ended badly for Pierrette.
Laser probing of her tooth show that she died at age 25 possibly leaving three kids. Nobody knows how her skull got cracked open.
What we know was after she received this deathly blow to her parietal, she lived one more week and we can assume that she was cared after.
She was buried in the cave.

### Paleodocteur — 2015-05-03T16:15:44+0300

### Paleodocteur — 2015-07-05T19:15:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-170911>

earlier this week I was chocked because I figured I couldn't last more than a few seconds under water.
I mean the real salty big one , not the sink where I used to reach 60seconds somewhat ok.
So I was puzzled and a bit worried.
What did it mean?
I kept diving in various ways.

### Paleodocteur — 2015-07-05T19:20:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-170912>

Felt like the bottom of the sea was refusing me...
I gained weight in june, It's hot and I was probably more hypoxic than I thought….

What a dumb doc, It took UBI18 for me to learn that I was more likely to be hypercapnic.
Somewhere in my hypothalamus some neurons are given the info that I have too much CO2 floating around….
We might have the same problem than the sea.
dropping pH too.

### Paleodocteur — 2015-07-05T19:33:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-170913>

So I kept diving deeper, where the water feels tight and cold (around 4 feet deep... for now)
and by repeating it a few days through I had the chance of brassing once , twice, then more , in the deep cold .
I still have a long way to go but that's a happy breakthrough to my biology.

### Paleodocteur — 2015-07-05T19:36:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-170914>

### Paleodocteur — 2015-07-05T19:46:33+0300

But with UBI series there is something blatantly refusing to be understood.
I don't know what needs to be taken metaphorically , as an illustration of the case being made, or true intertwined cycles….

### Paleodocteur — 2015-07-05T19:51:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-170918>

anyhow this morning the brush gave me some kind of a lead

CO2 in the sky , CO2 in our blood...

### Paleodocteur — 2015-07-05T20:02:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-170920>

Coming from our mitos , where TCA cycles.
the mito get the protons , give us the CO2 to discard , we get the electrons..
More CO2 in blood means TCA cycle went faster > we need to provide more O2
Or else electron and proton overload> ROS generation, acidification…

### Paleodocteur — 2015-07-05T20:17:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-170921>

To get rid of them we need the blood to flow properly , to breath well,
….and good ability to proton tunneling? because anhydrase carbonic is the key enzyme that is going to unable us to transform apolar CO2 in HCO3- and back to exhale that CO2. Once again need N filled porphyrin ring and Fe and ... H+….

### Paleodocteur — 2015-07-05T20:25:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-170925>

Or we can uncouple , with food ( ketosis) , or Complex IV decoupler ( NO, CO, CN) , or other electron acceptors ( SH2)
or cold ( swimming deeper more often)

or try to overturn climate/ionic flux change...

### Paleodocteur — 2015-07-05T20:35:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-170927>

through TCA?

Or is it through increased protein reshaping? which requires more ATP, hence more rounds of the TCA….
Ubi Houba!
I can now go to the beach!

### Paleodocteur — 2015-07-06T21:00:08+0300

### Paleodocteur — 2015-07-06T21:11:42+0300

Now the new waves are quickly sulking our bones and PVN to meet in the next big melting pot….

### JanSz — 2015-07-28T15:56:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-173295>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=172691)
>
> Structured water, does it flow?
>
> //

[http://www.tbiomed.com/content/pdf/s12976-015-0006-1.pdf](http://www.tbiomed.com/content/pdf/s12976-015-0006-1.pdf)

In a series of
in vitro
experiments, Pollack and coworkers have established that water
adjacent to hydrophilic surfaces (interfacial water) differs dynamically from bulk water
in several respects, including exhibiting up to ten times higher viscosity \[55, 56\].

Pollack and coworkers proposed the term
“exclusion zone”
for this interfacial water,
based on the observation that solutes and suspended particles were excluded from this water at distances up to a few hundred microns from the hydrophilic surface \[56\].
He
proposes that the structured water along biological membranes forms hexamers \[61\]
that closely resemble the structure of ice.

### Paleodocteur — 2015-08-01T19:36:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-173706>

So I was there with all these little nagging questions last year. That I could'nt answer because deeper physics is out of reach for me.
So I let them simmer , because honestly the blogs were flying at an increasing rate and with them tons of cites and refs to dig through
But you asked me and reignighted my curiosity , so thank you again.
So I realized that my favorite biochemistry handbook didn't explain the quantum shells to well
so I had to borrow another one which is awesome because , after a few pages from the surface I got this kind of picture that stroked me:

It s' about hybridization of the s and p shells
I don't say it explains EZ but it's seems to go in the right direction.
It probably explain why a lot of biochem world remains ignorant of this topic: " Obvioulsy everybody knows water hybridizes in sp3" which is why it is a polar molecule….

### Paleodocteur — 2015-08-01T19:41:49+0300

could it be the missing p ones? I guess not, it is not ice, so probably loose bounds there… but who knows?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-01T14:36:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-178354>

> [Paleodocteur said:](/goto/post?id=178347)
>
> I was really trying to understand

Simple........Rats are eukaryotes.......and all eukaryotes use DHA to harvest purple light photoelectrically. This means the rat stores UV light in things like carotenoids and melanin and other photoreceptors int he retina to use when it is dark and their wakefulness is abundant. Split things in parts like light that and you won't make sense of it...... Use a systems-perspective and it starts to make more sense... ........Sunlight has a particular spectrum that is in ITSELF a context for any person. It is the job of the clinician to realize that context differs for every patient we see.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-01T16:14:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-178363>

NAD+ and water are a bridge that adds charge to a cell. The sun provides the charge and DHA is the middle man dealer.......that charge is contained (DC electric current) in purple light and we harvest it using the pi electrons of DHA in the eukaryotic kingdom. The mass of a body can be altered with the presence or absence of DHA within cell membranes. Only eukaryotes have this ability. This makes them more complex because they can add more charge from the most powerful part of the solar spectrum that reaches earth's surface. This gets to the essence of EMF 2. When you realize that the sun energy carries mass, you begin to see the beauty in E=mc2 written in reverse. The sun adds current to all life to cause it to regenerate in all kingdoms. We have experimental proof of it already in plants and all animals thanks to Becker and others. This all implies that : **The mass of a body varies with the surface electric charge. Since purple light carries the most charge it has the most affect on mass. This implies that the addition or subtraction of light changes the charge of our cells and tissues, and thusly, affects our BMI most.

We know BMI is not a universal constant on Earth and varies person to person but we do not appreciate the implications of the same BMI number on different people. We ignore the effect of light they can assimilate and collect from the sun in this equation. It is akin to why cosmologists and astrophysicists wrongly assume ‘big G’ is a constant on Earth. It cannot be a constant on this planet because the mass of any body varies with the surface charge given to it via the environment. The surface charge on Earth is affected by the quantum yield of the sun impacted to the Earth. nnEMF's in the ionosphere repel that charge of the sun's power, thusly reducing the photosynthetic capabilities of everything living on this planet. Photosynthesis links to all food webs and all food is stupid to electrons in mitochondria.

**This is why BMI can be the same in an NFL football player and a morbidly obese person. The number is relative to the charge contained within the body. That charge determines health or wellness, and not the number contained in BMI. Charge is a function of how much or little purple light a body/cell contains.**
**

Be mindful of the light stress you allow your body to sense.

**Our natural sleep cycle is nothing like what we do now**

[http://www.ted.com/talks/jessa\_gamble\_how\_to\_sleep](http://www.ted.com/talks/jessa_gamble_how_to_sleep)

\--------------
So, what would our natural rhythm look like? What would our sleeping patterns be in the sort of ideal sense? Well, it turns out that when people are living without any sort of artificial light at all, they sleep twice every night. They **go to bed around 8:00 p.m. until midnight and then again, they sleep from about 2:00 a.m. until sunrise.** And in-between, they have a couple of hours of sort of meditative quiet in bed. And during this time, there's a surge of prolactin, the likes of which a modern day never sees. The people in these studies report feeling so awake during the daytime, that they realize they're experiencing true wakefulness for the first time in their lives.

### Paleodocteur — 2015-10-04T17:41:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-178600>

May be the hydrogen photon comes from the heart of our star
finding it's way through its core then piercing its magnetic field,
then space,
then finding it sway through the bending hearth field ,
swirling around nitrogen atoms , all the way to the surface of the planet
piercing our body M field,
through the layers of our skin,
until it enters the vicinity of an electron swirling around an
exogenous double or endogenous quadruple carbon cycle
And then unites to it
to slowly get ferried to another
water vortexing hydrogen bound
in us

### Paleodocteur — 2015-11-01T18:54:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-180917>

wednesday sleep delayed by car gazed on freeway
then next day overload of carbs cakes rice lentils….
I could blame it on the host WIfI streaming ambiance music …
but should I?
the weather was gloomy too.
And then to top it off we lit too many candles, ate late
and play with red LEDs through my face bones as if they were candies >>>couldn't sleep

### Paleodocteur — 2015-11-01T19:05:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-180918>

friday I was feeling good though , the god SUN had come back.and my skin had absorbed enough of it in the morning to work indoor the rest of the day. ( albeit with UV and Magnetic clutches )
we even went evening fishing for the first time
what a joke , the line got entangled

The designers of the new bridge had chosen BLUE LED for lighting the all structure.
Was it the light or the fish and chips or the cold breeze? > I felt compelled to RUN like I never do.
Can the blue LEDs have the same effect than potatoes?

### Paleodocteur — 2015-11-01T19:09:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-180919>

Magic

When I lit up a candle I may already be methylating some promoters.

### Paleodocteur — 2015-12-03T17:21:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-183176>

One month into UV13W+IR light ten to thirty minutes a day (morning) around computer screen(fluxed)…

Tan is good , sleep is good .
And mood is good, once cakes and birthday foods are pooped out
and the long autumnal tensegrity mashup cleared to a cold bright sky.

So far no benefit on eye sight , if anything, it seems my myopia is getting worse ….

### Joe Gavin — 2015-12-04T15:35:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-183275>

> [Paleodocteur said:](/goto/post?id=183176)
>
> One month into UV13W+IR light ten to thirty minutes a day (morning) around computer screen(fluxed)…
>
> Tan is good , sleep is good .
> And mood is good, once cakes and birthday foods are pooped out
> and the long autumnal tensegrity mashup cleared to a cold bright sky.
>
> So far no benefit on eye sight , if anything, it seems my myopia is getting worse ….

I've found that almost constant use of Lizard Light at work (Exo-Terra 200) has done wonders for my eyesight. It was a little tough at first, but eyes adjusted pretty quickly. My night vision has improved drastically, very little halos now from oncoming cars at night, (use skypers now when driving at night, been an interesting experience). Left my glasses home yesterday and found I was still able to read somewhat comfortably both printed and computer based literature. Again, that was somewhat of a surprise to me since reading was always tough without glasses last few years. And I did have Lasik surgery 17 years ago too. The halos were there almost from the beginning so I feel the UV has healed whatever imperfections were created by the surgery. I'm astounded frankly.

### Paleodocteur — 2015-12-20T13:35:57+0200

### Paleodocteur — 2015-12-20T13:39:31+0200

### Paleodocteur — 2015-12-20T14:02:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-184567>

And english wasn't my native language , let alone Krusich. A lot had been lost in translation.
there was a lot of background (paleo) noises
and my antennas didn't seem to be well tuned.
Actually five years ago I was quickly loosing signals.
Likely my already slow tall clock was getting even more inefficient.
I had always been slow and inefficient .

### Paleodocteur — 2015-12-20T14:23:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-184568>

Especially at teenage : Last one asleep , last one to get up ,low days , low drivers, last one to get laid , low moods , last one to laugh at a joke., weak muscles, last one at the finnish line, if ever. Clumsy and slow.
It was so strikingly different from my pears that I have vivid memories of the few moments when Time accelerated and i got strong:
\- At the end of a soccer game when suddenly I could outrun everybody else: it was cold
\- at the end of summer season windsurfing in…the cold
\- in love (UV effect?)
\- abroad ( UV effect?)

### Paleodocteur — 2015-12-20T14:32:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-184569>

### Paleodocteur — 2015-12-21T20:45:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-184712>

I have been listening all along, and it sinked in . The trouble was I couldn't upgrade my education fast enough and cope with the deluge of blogs while the basic concepts were escaping me. FOMO. Each new info was exciting my curiosity. I would see glimpses of Nature's beauty in many corners of the literature and marvel and one abstract would lead to the next and open another unexplored avenue… So LONG conceptually marvelous sidetrack, may be worth it , but I need to strengthen my core knowledges if I want to stop sliding.

### nonchalant — 2015-12-21T22:52:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-184718>

### Paleodocteur — 2016-01-03T16:56:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-185328>

Was feeling queazy the last day of the year, before midnight. .DW said "shouldn't you go to the garden and ground.?"
Indeed , feet in the grass and eyes in the stars, my stomach rapidly found an answer .
It started a firework of its own. I vomited my last meal of the year.
I hate to think it could be the oyster I smashed open on the beach.
good riddance 2015.

### Paleodocteur — 2016-01-03T16:57:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-185329>

What did I get wrong in 2015? …
that I can change.
Not doing well on the last half of this year.
No wonder .
Got the nadir results.
Lowest vit D ever: 25 ng ,( gonna have to get more photons than last spring )
Testosterone dropping back to 4,9 . (Hope that is seasonal too )
(On the surface I have more muscle mass and libido than 20 years ago)
. but I guess part of it gets aromatized (subcutaneous fat, blue food blue light?)
Stable DHEA 366
TSH went up a bit: 1,06
HbA1c stable 5,3 fBG ,92….
Chol 2,42HDL ,93 ….trig 0,5

### Paleodocteur — 2016-01-03T17:04:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-185332>

Goals for 2016
A Understanding how bad are my COE, to me and to loved ones, which parts are the worse
B Fix the COE. Get more photons , better grounding.
C If COE not fixable and/or too harsh on them . Finding our way out. but Where?
D Study enough biology and physics to finally get it and be able to act
E Stop freaking out. I shouldn't be thinking the world is ending each time a cloud is passing.
F Work hard enough so B) and C) can become a possibility
G Work as little as possible, to keep bad environment at bay, to study , to get photons and better sleep.conditions

### Paleodocteur — 2016-01-03T17:07:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-185333>

D one of the first thing I should have done long ago is to finaly ask wether my EMSensitivity is inherently really bad or if that is a "good sign" as I was told 2 years ago. .
Is it a good telling sign that I am cooked? or a good sign that I am sensing my alien environment better ?

### Paleodocteur — 2016-01-03T17:21:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-185335>

Akin to a cigarette quitter who finally realizes how stinky the bars have become.
Likewise, spending a little time in the smoke won't kill anyone ( well except if you have heart failure or asthma or are a bar tender)
But if you know that smoking is deadly you might "over sense" it and freak out and trigger stress response and have weird reactions.
Can one have these same reactions without the concious knowledge of danger to feed them? Likely .if the stressor directly act on the body .
Does the analogy hold true ?
In the case of exogenousEMFs, the stressor is either imaginary or a stress at every level of physiology.
or both.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-01-10T07:51:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-185890>

those lines and charts = this :

Time 6 showed you how UV light and aromatic amino acids make dopamine. Serotonin and melatonin are also biologic amines. Did you know this? Serotonin is synthesized from tryptophan, another aromatic amino acid. Tryptophan is transcriptionally activated by UV light as well vitamin D3 sulfate. UVB Sunlight makes Vitamin D3. EPA increases serotonin release from presynaptic neurons by reducing E2 prostaglandins. DHA has huge pi electron clouds that draw light to them photo-electrically. This is one of the more amazing abilities DHA has that few people even know. DHA also has a massive influence on the serotonin receptor action, by increasing cell membrane fluidity and improving tunneling of electrons and protons in postsynaptic neurons in the gut just behind the enterocytes. These cells face the microbiome. The microbiome is made up by prokaryote bacteria that are designed to release 5000 time more light then our cells. In the gut surface the story is still a light story. The light is then changed to chemical, mechanical and optical signals by atoms in our cells. These signals make connections in our gut neurons and head directly to the floor of the fourth ventricle in the brain, at the area postrema to create strong parasympathetic signal. This balances the stress responses from the paraventricular nucleus in the brainstem that is the guardian of the sympathetic response. The central retinal pathways however govern the PVN because of their light relays. Suprachiasmatic efferents to the paraventricular nucleus (PVN) and the supraoptic nucleus (SON) give the eye the power over the gut relays to the area postrema. This is how the brain gut axis is organized for optical signaling. The SCN also sends direct and indirect neural projections to the arcuate nucleus (ARC). This projection consisted of both excitatory and inhibitory components and may contribute to the entrainment of the rhythm in growth hormone secretion to the day-night cycle. Some SCN neurons project to both the SON and the ARC. The SCN in turn receives excitatory and inhibitory inputs from the ARC and the peri-nuclear zone of the SON (peri-SON). The extensive two-way connections between the SCN and its target nuclei contribute to the generation of day-night neuroendocrine rhythms. These complex control system also allows for non-photic entrainment of the SCN, for example, in response to feeding and meal times. The Leptin Rx uses these relays to re-entrain the SCN. Today modern man faces globally low sulfated Vitamin D3 levels because we continue to bury the sun many ways. Dermatology beliefs, clothing, sun screen, and lack of exposure to AM sunlight are important variables to consider. But society has also been depriving themselves of vital EPA & DHA in seafood for 70,000 years since we moved away from shorelines, built cities and homes indoors with alien sun's as humans left Africa.

BOOM

### Paleodocteur — 2016-01-21T16:19:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-186985>

Home measurements
So 90-120 nT across the house at 45° likely coming from the cable. Only showing up once the public ligths switch on so I infer its 50 Hz or 20 kHz.
not a nice face skin feeling in the evening although it depends of:
the weather ( do worse on mid season mid temp gloomy days)
the type of strain I had at work,
the moment of the day: worse of at dusk but I don't really feel it in the early morning
the day of the week ( frying fridays, but is due to my work day or the line picks up whatever aerial signal)

It's around the range of Nordic norms but it freaks me out every evenings.
Yet we've been in this position for 18 months now...

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-191379>

And new hurdles to mitigate
Like: first two nights were sleepless
So I pull out my meter and there it was the neighbor's appliance pulsing 200nT right across our bed head
Managed to shield it and turn the bed 90°
I had to go against my Feng Shui Mistress' opinion, but I believe nnEM Feng Shui trumps classical TCM one, but who knows
And finally we sleep and wake up with sun rise that gets through the blinds

### Paleodocteur — 2016-04-17T19:21:28+0300

### Paleodocteur — 2016-04-17T19:33:06+0300

Especially at dusk after cloudy day …. when light and "pressure" are low.

### Paleodocteur — 2016-08-25T18:57:17+0300

### Paleodocteur — 2016-08-25T19:10:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-200220>

this summer I find it harder to get up at sun rise
that is the conundrum of town dwellers :
I mean minding our sleep cycle four years ago gradually made us very sensitive to extraneous lights at night,
first we closed our curtains , then it was not enough so we went for full light blocking ones
then those would let some through on the corners so we had to tuck them.

Now we have ( i believe) Sodium Vapor Street Lamps around the house
so we need to turn down the electric shutters , and the bedroom gets pitch black...

### Paleodocteur — 2016-08-25T19:13:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-200221>

... and stays like that a little too long
and we miss sunrise

### Paleodocteur — 2016-08-25T19:21:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-200222>

4 things seem to have help lately

1 going camping for a few days( even though their 'eco'lightings at night were nasty)
2 turning the mattress with head north instead of westward
3 actually getting up and go swimming and do more stuff outside
4 weather stays sunny so it helps too...

### JanSz — 2016-08-25T19:28:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-200223>

We are creatures evolved on Sunlight.
Sunlight is a light that is balanced from UV to IR
it also contains **Fraunhofer lines**.

The best substitution that I was able to find is:

[https://www.amazon.com/Exo-Terra-Solar-Glo-Intensity-Self-Ballasted/dp/B00101JII8/ref=sr\_1\_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1472142283&sr=8-1&keywords=exo terra solar glo 160](https://www.amazon.com/Exo-Terra-Solar-Glo-Intensity-Self-Ballasted/dp/B00101JII8/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1472142283&sr=8-1&keywords=exo+terra+solar+glo+160)

**Exo Terra Solar-Glo High Intensity Self-Ballasted Uv/Heat Mercury Vapor Lamp**
by [Exo Terra
](https://www.amazon.com/Exo-Terra/b/ref=bl_dp_s_web_3027941011?ie=UTF8&node=3027941011&field-lbr_brands_browse-bin=Exo+Terra)
That light does not have **Fraunhofer lines.
**
When getting of the bed I switch two of these lights
and that is my sunrise.

So far it works.
My
cortisol,serum(7;30AM) =19.4(6.2-19.4)ug/dL

/

### Paleodocteur — 2016-08-28T18:34:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-200357>

so far I am trying to make a "perihelioscope "

### Paleodocteur — 2017-02-04T17:50:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-207925>

Would be ideal if it weren’t for the tall twin towers a yard away ( one holding 5 TV relays, another 7 cell antennas, still 2 and 3G). didn’t bring my meters but preferred to assume their directions and height were somehow sparing us …

### Paleodocteur — 2017-02-04T17:52:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-207926>

Initial eastward 12 hours jetlag recovery took about a month, way too long. not a good sign.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-02-04T17:55:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-207927>

Despite sticking to solar pattern, spending most days outside, swimming in the sea, lots of seafood , local carbs. Not that I was hungry but there is no way to politely refuse a meal in this kind of family gatherings. I tried to stick to two meals a day. I rapidly gained 15 pounds, mostly around belly and moobs.
I guess that is being insuline sensitive., messed up cortisol cycles, Low GH, High PRL , low dopa…

My mood was good but cognition, injury recovery, BP , HR, patience , will, creativity, and memory were low. ( unable to understand/remember well biophysics book , unable to finish cosmosapiens)

### Paleodocteur — 2017-02-04T17:59:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-207929>

I am a northern haplotype who seems to be unable to handle the tropics anymore. That’s what I was thinking until the clouds cleared out a bit.
It seems at the end , that when sky was blue I had much more stamina. There can be gloomy skies in the tropics too . As we became very sensitive light sensors it may affect me more there, where cold can’t back me up.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-02-04T18:02:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-207931>

I wanted to avoid the house CFLs LEDs and other wiring. But red incandescents and candle light were considered impolite retrograde annoyances by the elderly. So we went sleeping in a breezy bamboo hut. (Yeah! closer to Nature!)

### Paleodocteur — 2017-02-04T18:03:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-207933>

That was a mistake.
Sleep quality tanked.
Half a mile across the river a neighbor leaves his CFLbulb on. It appears bright when I’d stood up at night to pee. But I don’t think that was the main cause for our shallow sleep.
I believe it was Nature itself. Not the winds blowing the palm roof, but THE MOON. She was messing up my sleep. For four years at home we have been sleeping in pitch black bedrooms and had lost sight of her brightness, and her cycles.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-02-04T18:05:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-207935>

Finally a typhon tail blew up the tarpaulin and DW’s patience . We headed back to the confort of a bed. And even used air conditioning as a sleep starter. Finally got solid sleep. That coincided with bluer skies too and shedding back the extra weight.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-04-10T21:23:37+0300

### Paleodocteur — 2017-04-10T21:24:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-212121>

Haden’t been there in a while
My colleagues go « wow you got tanned over there! but I ‘m thinking it’s my MSH going on overdrive during the train ride"

I had to give a speech on hormonal development …and how light would affect it.
I was nervous but thought I could mitigate, stayed fasted for 48h, grounded, cold, hydrated.
felt good but nervousness increased when noticed the ceiling was laden with old CFLs spots
Quickly my skin turned pink an dry , HR in the hundreds
As for the speech,I was not very articulate ( blame the frying light and RF mic or my shyness )
but I think colleagues got the picture.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-06-29T20:46:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-217501>

Summer Solstice labs after 10 days of sunrise gazing
lost 4 pounds
Hb 15,4g/dl
ESR 5
urea low down to 0,15g/l
creat 9mg/l unchanged
FBS 0,87g/l HbA1C 5,2% stable
trig 0,6 g/l chol 2,5 HDL 0,9
K+4,4 unchanged
hs CRP 1,5 (ouch but down from 40 in april )
testo 4,6 midrange (down from a year ago5,2)
S-DHEA 386 (down from a year ago 405 ) still in upper quartile
unsupplemented vitD 39 ng/ml ( descent midday sun grazing, down from49 ng/ml post winter tropical refill)

### Jack Kruse — 2017-06-29T20:57:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-217506>

Nice.....no new paintings?

### Paleodocteur — 2017-06-29T21:50:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-217509>

Not much and that bugs me ....

### Paleodocteur — 2017-06-29T21:55:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-217511>

If I didn't know better I'd say grain brain

Not optimal labs, far from optimal food choices for the last 5 months , cravings .

mirror test : middle age man, hair getting whiter, stronger biceps , wider shoulders, more days with moobs than without, more fat on upper belly.
Mood: weather dependent but luckily mostly sunny here so I get good morning rays.
I still tank when atmospheric pressure drops.

But I feel much more resilient than during the equinox.
Late afternoon population movement and cell tower peaks seems to be a major driver for cravings , not so in the morning when my electron flow is still steady.

EMFSensitivity is varying but seems less than before or I am becoming more indifferent to it ( whatever it does to me, i find it is VERY unhealthy to freak out each time someone is zapping me)
Sleep: solid, mostly spontaneous wake up with first photons peaking through the blinds (or right as the street lamps shut down). Doesn’t happen when weather gets gloomy but it’s hard to know if that is due to delayed photonic rise or uneven nightly repair because of stormy weather. When I wake up and am able to catch surya rising I feel great, and the day feels MUCH longer , by that I mean longer than the extra awake hour it allows. Qualitatively longer.

Creativity: down . less creativity for about a year . very few drawings, correlates with weight gains, correlate with the move to this « better" house. Was frying my skin in the previous one but at least my drive was much stronger. I guess artists need to to be slightly tortured to produce something.

Cognition: down . I still print blogs, cites, and "cited by » articles, hundreds of abstracts a month. I find some great books. But its becoming increasingly hard to commit to read them all. It feels like my "hard drive" is full and adding another piece to the puzzle only adds to the clutter…

### Paleodocteur — 2017-07-30T17:48:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-220445>

Is sunrise flickering ?
That was my impression a few days ago , when I'd stare at the disc it was shimmering
about ten times per second....

### Paleodocteur — 2017-07-30T19:09:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-220458>

Now if I imagine a sailor filled with electrons, going back to Hamburg. Is he going to have an acute rise or drop of dopamine ? Is it going to last? Will it be the same if he flew back home instead of a monthly journey?

In a way I already experienced that twenty years ago when I came back from a three year stay on the equator to a cold wet northern harbor. I was happy, filled with energy, wanted to have talk and party . But by the end of winter energy seemed to have receded and I ("We") badly needed a sun refill.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-07-30T19:39:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-220461>

So there was a prolonged high
but then was it because my UV reserves had been refilled to the rim and enabled me to interact or because my HPA axis was on overdrive to compensate for UV deficit?

### Paleodocteur — 2017-08-03T20:37:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-220849>

SNAP
hackbump
For four years I have been trying to ground regularly with some good effects on my posture, my chicken calves grew , my soles are getting more leathery. I feel better resilience to weather changes.. (I also got minor cuts, spindles, glass, dogs, beestings, but that’s peanut…)
3 month ago I wanted to up it from about 1 hour a day to about 3 hours a day. To do that I decided to walk barefoot as much as possible. I received a new pair of minetonkas of which I removed the insole last week. since side walks and streets are petrol based I try to walk on the grass, and curbs.
With makes for a funny walk as if the front part of my feet had to adapt to a new function.. I was getting more confortable with my prehension and confident I was strengthening my tensegrity.
Monday after such a walk back home I did a minor jump of joy and fell in pain to the ground.

I snapped some fibers in my calf. no big deal . makeshift IR and icing has decreased my limping already. .

### Paleodocteur — 2017-08-03T20:39:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-220850>

For the last two weeks I had seen a lot of mysterious spontaneous tendinous injury amongst elderly. A few minutes before my own demise I was already wondering what kind of weather event led to these serial snapping .

### Paleodocteur — 2017-08-03T20:40:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-220851>

We had two weeks fo stormy weather. now I have to find what was going on , above. Thank you OptimalBound for that paper. I have to find those logs

### Paleodocteur — 2017-08-03T21:02:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-220858>

nope , not, that would do it but I don't think it's related in these cases. I believe tensegrity took a hit from above.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-08-03T21:35:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-220864>

It’s vacation time ( 5 weeks in the sun make for a good chunk of the french paradox). This town population density is at it’s lowest. Except for downtown and the beach , laden with foreign tourists and their cellphonecamera. ( Both places I spent time before the rupture. ) . Now adda small heat wave prior to that change in microwaving landscape and it may require an overadaptation and that may be be taxing to triple helices...

### Paleodocteur — 2017-08-25T13:37:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-222225>

10 day vacation
Drove the old convertible south to the 44th to grab last bits of purple Bees
Nice lakes, pine trees, more O2. Long sand beaches, but tight sand spaces for climatic refugees (NL,B,D, UK…) clumped in small pockets of ‘safe beach’ . Beware of waves between the two flags of the swim guards. Waves are strong and current treacherous..
It seems that sand is a reflector for nonnative waves > and higher local population density > less O2, > first sun burns of the year.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-08-25T13:38:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-222226>

Camping, at the dedicated climatic refugee camp sites. In tent with a grounding cable. Sometimes there were more nnlights than at home and it’s a mess to try to block it all on the spot. But overall good sleep and easy dawn wake up. Like at home I find myself unable to catch dawn when cloudy.
Unlike at home I seldom heard the faint tinnitus.
Mostly good moods, even by cloudy days

### Paleodocteur — 2017-11-23T21:06:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-228758>

A short journey back to northern H1c3 motherland. A day trip in a zapping train. Doing ok on the way up .

### Paleodocteur — 2017-11-23T21:08:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-228760>

Staying there would usually turns me back to default mode.
By that I mean the low drive coach-potato that I used to be. It didn’t happen too much when I went back in the last few years, because I tend to do better.
It even seems to have a beneficial effect...on me.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-11-23T21:21:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-228762>

My native field has become as polluted as anywhere else , the nearby church is beaming 0,500mW/m^2 at the bathtub window. Where I could see sun rise...
ELFs are « bouncing » to twenty microT . the cornet jumps , green,orange, green, orange even when central breaker is OFF, I wonder what that’s from.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-11-23T21:23:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-228763>

This old light fixture in the kitchen stinks.
Induction plates must have had their share in her cancer relapse too.
« Mum did you know a 2005 prospective study with EMF holters showed higher breast cancer rates in the higher tertile ? » - doesn’t work
« Stay away from the induction plates Mum » - doesn’t work
« Please block the street lights » - doesn’t work
« Go to sleep early please Mum » - does work a bit , she may have gained 2 hours over 5 years.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-11-23T21:25:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-228764>

In five years of advices , I could only make her go to bed a little earlier, but I suspect it’s because "you know they talked about it on the radio". So when we come to visit and all lights go off early , it is a very early curfew she as to endure. May be not so good either.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-11-23T21:26:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-228765>

I was unable to prevent her aortic stenosis, nor the open heart afternoon surgery. But she is recovering well.
Incidentally the local newspaper just released a local but large cohort study « Better to have your heart open in the afternoon : twice more likely to get complications when open in the morning . Doctors scratching their head»

### Paleodocteur — 2017-11-23T21:27:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-228766>

Now also when she walks in the park she glances at the sun.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-11-23T21:44:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-228771>

My home has much lower readings than hers. Yet my creativity there seems to have bounced back. I made more in three days than I did in three months. I am unable to see why.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-11-23T21:50:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-228773>

Geotropic healer? Even if it is now polluted, and has a poor Yeld - we had filtered sunshine; It would make sense that my mitos and I get better on the land my maternal haplotype was doing well. GranMa was in good shape and some of her 6 sisters made it to their nineties.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-11-26T17:42:43+0200

### Paleodocteur — 2017-11-26T17:48:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-228889>

The boost only lasted so long.
On the way south stormy weather.
Ruching to buy a meridian man.
In a bookstore, looking for Vital question .
"No sir we don't have it but you can check our science books over there..."
I wish I didn't
inadvertently stumbled on book , just glanced on some way old photographs and patents that I can’t seem to be able to erase from my failing memory.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-11-26T17:49:03+0200

### Paleodocteur — 2017-11-26T18:03:14+0200

Luxury shielded from ionospheric interferences (was it written 0.25 Farad?) Speedy metallic tube going south . Zap Zap Zhappy train . Faster Faster ….

### Paleodocteur — 2017-12-22T21:55:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-230867>

Dear Santa
This year I have been a good 50Hz boy
I took my cold garden shower all autumn and winter , every morning at dawn ( except when the hose gets frozen or I have time to go to the beach) .
From his window the neighbor can witness my steaming skin (hopefully he can not see that I am peeing blue, he would think I am crazy).
After I feed the guinea pigs I grab most sunrises .
It is tricky as they recently replaced the old sodium vapor street lights with crispy LEDs . Trying different scenarios.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-12-22T21:56:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-230868>

Although everyone around complains this autumn sucks and really started midJuly , I seem to have gone through it better than previous years.
I can go with one or two meals a day provided EMFs conditions are met. 6 weeks ago I ramped up nuts cheese an oysters….And dark C3 theobroma cocoa.
Cravings are getting rare.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-12-22T21:57:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-230869>

Moobs are gone, and I have been loosing upper belly fat fast. too fast. so I up the cheese. I have more muscle mass without exercice. It’s like 2011 all over again.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-12-22T21:58:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-230870>

But that wasn’t my goal. I have so many articles and so many great books (I know they are wrong but one got to start somewhere) in the Library to read .
And with candle light, it's slow.
Dear Santa , I want to gain a better functioning brain so I can make complete sense of all this. Sure lately we have connected many dots, and with Great Amazement! But it still seems like a beginning more than a conclusion ( another Zeno effect ).

### Paleodocteur — 2017-12-22T21:59:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-230871>

I don’t think I ever was a fast nor a good thinker, It’s just that my curious large head would sens things. So I don’t know if it is even possible to rewire that cortex. I guess I should just be thankful I escaped the escalating brain fogginess I use to be in. But I want more.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-12-22T22:23:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-230876>

P.S.
Dear Santa I also was a very patient boy, a good indigogoboy . I wish I would have received my Q-toy before Christmas, because yeld is lowering here too and I have trains to take and some leftover deutons to lift.…
But its’ okay, I know you’ve been busy. May be Christmass 2018 then?

Feliz Navidad ! Muchos Besos

### Paleodocteur — 2018-01-20T12:33:23+0200

Social carbing fallout. I may have stained the phone with oyster juice and lemon.

Now I want to see what to do when I « Perform » in the Titanic belly, or more likely right after it. Will chose wellness again tonight.

handled it ok

hypoxic grasses had to upregulate their LDH too
