### Clayton — 2015-02-23T19:58:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-156409>

Hello Mike,

Good to see you are making some positive changes...have you added good fats to your template as well? By that I mean grassfed butter, duck fat, MCTs or ghee?

Inger mentioned blue blockers to mitigate your blue light exposure..these are the ones I use

[http://www.amazon.com/Uvex-S1933X-E...24713948&sr=8-1&keywords=skyper+blue+blockers](http://www.amazon.com/Uvex-S1933X-Eyewear-SCT-Orange-Anti-Fog/dp/B000USRG90/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1424713948&sr=8-1&keywords=skyper+blue+blockers). You can also pick them up at Acklands-Grainger or any industrial supply store

At some point you are gonna want to really dissect your EMF exposure at home and at work and take steps to lower your exposure risk. There are a few EMF consultants in Vancouver you could look into:

[http://www.emfsafehome.com/emf\_vancouver.htm](http://www.emfsafehome.com/emf_vancouver.htm)
[http://healthyhomesenvironmental.com/](http://healthyhomesenvironmental.com/)

Scot Appert out of Seattle also does this and comes to Vancouver periodically

[http://www.biohealthyhomes.com/](http://www.biohealthyhomes.com/)

Hope this helps!

### nicld — 2015-02-23T20:29:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-156411>

Hi Mike,

Get some of these ASAP [http://www.amazon.com/Uvex-S1933X-E...1424713695&sr=8-5&keywords=blublocker+glasses](http://www.amazon.com/Uvex-S1933X-Eyewear-SCT-Orange-Anti-Fog/dp/B000USRG90/ref=sr_1_5?ie=UTF8&qid=1424713695&sr=8-5&keywords=blublocker+glasses)

This has been the best investment I have made for my sleep. I put them on shortly after I get home (about 6 pm) and by 9 pm I can hardly keep my eyes open.

My DH does not have the same issue so he will not partake in the light out at night but does let me turn the lights as low as we can. We try to keep the bedroom as dark as I can get it at night and I use a sun lamp right away in the morning when I wake. For me, it is still dark out but I have to get up earlier for work (Neolithic life) and really don’t get to see the sun much in the winter.

I am on a computer all day at work so I try really really hard to stay off of it at night when I am home. Try to limit it to 1 hour a day. I know that you want to learn as much as you can so it may be a challenge.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-02-24T07:51:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-156444>

Hi Clayton and Nicld!
Thanks a bunch for your help! Yeah, I have been eating lots of coconut oil, maybe 2-3 heaping TBSP a day because I find it helps to keep my energy levels stable.
I know Jack says to leave 4hours between dinner and bed where you don't eat anything however I had previously gotten in the habit of having a TBSP before bed to maintain my blood sugar throughout the night. Before that my sleep was quite poor because I was always hungry - getting up every 2-3 hours to eat carbs that I later learned would spike my cortisol, drop my blood sugar and I'd get hungry again! Since adding the coconut oil before bed I am sleeping without those interruptions so that's a plus.
Butter on everything, starting to ramp up the seafood as well.
I do need to get some proper blue blockers. I have some red glasses that came in my jigsaw kit but my wife laughs at me because they look corny (They're huge!).
I also got an RF meter and checked out some stuff around the house but I think an assessment is the best way to go because that is what Jack recommends. There is so much stuff we can miss.
My dad got diagnosed with parkinson's/dementia about 2 years ago. the medical team was baffled as to why it came on so fast. The interesting thing is that a smart meter was installed on his house somewhere around the same time -right under his and my sister's bedrooms!! the main household power comes in in between their bedrooms as well.
They have always had terrible sleep and both of them have always had psychiatic problems.
interesting.
Thanks again guys..I gotta get of the computer now. It's 9:51PM!

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-02-27T08:16:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-156785>

I tried the coconut crack thing but I think I need a recipe or some thing. I just threw the ingredients together and put it in the microwave for 10 seconds (I cheated w/the microwave - I'm working on that habit)
It was OK but I'm hesitant because chocolate can trigger migraines for me. I haven't really experimented with dark chocolate yet because I don't want to risk getting a headache.
The whole day gets ruined.
I saw a chiropractor today that is suppose to be really good. He had quite the process down for checking out for muscle weaknesses.
He told me that my headaches are coming from my skull (not my neck) and that I must have really hit my head hard at some point in my life because the small bony plates on the left side of my skull are misaligned. I said that I don't recall ever hitting my head hard but maybe. I sure did my share of drinking in the past and accidents like that are quite common with drunks (which I was one - frequently!)
Wow! I was shocked at how quickly he could figure that out!

Too late to be on the computer...bye for now!

I'm very glad that chocolate is happy to go in me!!!! I'm not sure what the difference between chocolate and raw organic cacao powder is, apart from being raw, organic and nothing but the cacao powder.....its good with about two tablespoons of coconut oil to a level teaspoon of cacao powder with a drop of stevia to take the edge off the bitterness - I sometimes use a raw food antioxidant powder that is low carb and has a little cacao in it, or I add a bit of cacao powder - get a good dose of vitamins and minerals - but I always check the label that one serve has less than a gram of carbs and I count that in my less than 15g carbs a day.

If I use other fats I would go about 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, 1 tablespoon cacao butter, one generous teaspoon of butter and some cacao, rawfood powder, maybe some chopped up nuts, or coconut, and a few drops of stevia - put in freezer - its a bit melty in my climate - eat frozen.....

My chiro adjusts my cranial plates every time as well - I get a bit dizzy at times when they are out - its not so weird if at least one other person on the planet has the same thing!

Good luck with your journey! And I hope you can enjoy cacao fat bombs, or coconut crack, one day!

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-02-28T21:57:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-156912>

I have to play a gig tonight with the band. That means I'll be up late : (
Back in my partying, clubbing, guitar playing, beer drinking days, I would totally notice the next day I would have a complete loss of energy. I thought it mostly had to do with the alcohol.
But then when I quit drinking, due to 3 day migraines occurring, I noticed that I STILL would feel exhausted if I stayed out late, indicating that the disruption of the circadian clock
is even worse for how I felt than the alcohol was!
Lesson learned (but struggling to practice) - I need to go to bed earlier!

I'm trying to get to bed earlier. One thing I forgot to mention - I normally go to bed around 10:30 - 11 PM and get up at 5:50 but I also have a nap between about 4:30 - 6 PM.
But really, my total actual "amount of sleep" is about 6 - 8 hrs (I just figured that out)
Yeah you are absolutely right , I need WAAAY more sleep!
I have to put things off and just go to bed when its time to do so. Part of my problem is I'm just not that tired at night (probably from the computer screen light, even though I have the f.lux)
Gotta go - I'm playing a gig tonight (..another late night..sigh)

I would not eat raw feed lot, conventional beef, but I will eat raw elk or locally raised, non medicated beef. The bedtime thing is slowly getting better thanks to you guys reminding me just how important it is.
I think I've been "programmed" to think that going to bed early is for wankers and that all the "cool" people stay up late and that is where all the fun is.
But when you do it all the time it compromises your health.
I used to think that feeling crappy after a night of drinking was because of the alcohol.
But then when I quit, I would STILL feel exhausted the following day. Not quite as bad but it showed me that the change in circadian signaling is critical to feeling your best.

I really don't understand why our health care system has a lot of its employees doing shift work. My mother in law (nurse) and my cousin (paramedic) BOTH work odd hours. My cousin had to take leave for depression. He is normally a very happy, funny guy so that shows just how powerful circadian mismatches are! Anyway, it kind of leads us to believe that it is 'OK', because the system would be the first people to know how bad these shifts are. It just doesn't make any sense.

### rgp888 — 2015-03-01T23:25:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-157070>

Fish and tumeric go great together! Moisten a little powder with some coconut oil (my coconut oil is always a liquid because its never cold here, but if yours is solid, just warm it a little), spread on fish - don't need much, lightly pan fry with butter - hot pan = outside seared and yummy, inside almost raw - the nearest I can get to eating raw fish at present!

### caroline — 2015-03-01T23:45:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-157071>

Fitness posted a tumeric paste recipe in the recipe section - it is so yummy!
We just made some salmon/thai fish cakes with fresh herbs and layered this stuff on. It is awesome on shrimp and on everything it seems...I just put some on a celery stick. Delicious mixed in with a can of mackerel etc.

you need to fix the Rolex in your brain.....
rise and go outside and look at the sun
have a BAB within thirty minutes of rising
drink R/O water
C/T
get lots and lots of grounding and sunshine with as little clothes on as possible
hug a tree, work in the garden, grow some herbs
lots of oxy .... sex, hugging, laughter, pets, smile at strangers and have a conversation. People have amazing stories.
Really look at them and really listen
and no fake lites after the sun goes down!

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-03-02T00:27:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-157079>

I have been definitely been doing the sunshine in AM - Going outside first thing in the AM (in my underwear -shh!) standing on the grass with a little bit of frost in my backyard surrounded by cedar trees and looking towards the sun - maybe 5 minutes.
Then at this point I hope I remembered to thaw some shrimp, oysters or duck sausages to cook. I'll have a TBSP of coconut oil and let it dissolve in my mouth because it gets kind of yucky after a while. Then I will usually remember to take two teaspoons of apple cider vinegar (to stimulate stomach acid) a bit too late. (Should be 15 minutes before protein).
During the week, when I get up for work it is still dark so I can't really do the sun thing but I try to look at the sun (without anything in the way like windows or glasses) as soon as it is rises at work. Throughout the day as well I try to get outside the shop as much as I can to breath the air and look towards the sun (or clouds if it is not out) to try to get my SCN to "calibrate" with the earth.
At work we have to wear work boots that are insulated so if I'm doing a sit down job that day I will try to wrap a wire around the grounded light post and tuck the other end in my sock. I'm a bit hesitant about this however because I don't know the "dirty electricity" situation of the ground line. Jack said it could make things worse.
We have a grounding sheet on our bed at home. My wife swears by it. She has MS and one of her symptoms is a burning sensation in her hands and feet.
One day when she wasn't around and I was changing the sheets, I was lazy and I didn't put it back on. The next day she phoned me and asked "Did you remove the grounding sheet yesterday or something 'cause my hands are burning today."
Wow! I guess it really DOES work!
I haven't done the reverse osmosis water thing yet but eventually I will get there. I did put in a basic whole house filter and also a filter on the shower. I still drink out of the tap but at least it is filtered now. Our water here in the Lower Mainland is pretty good. Its all snow melt but they still put chlorine in it. No fluoride either!
The more energy I get the more of these things will get implemented. It sucks that when you need those things the most you have the least energy to do them and more temptation to do the bad stuff!!
Short term gratification = Long term suffering

I lied around all day and it was absolutely beautiful out! Very frustrating and hard to explain to anyone - even my wife, because I LOOK healthy. I'm not overweight or underweight and show no physical signs of illness. And when I am around people I am an expert at covering it up. You would never know my fatigue situation at a glance.

Mike have had a bit of a win with my latest blue blocker n=1.
Used Blue b at night from sunset on but never during the day. Somehow thought it wouldn't be affecting me.
So, 3 yrs later!  After one days avoidance actually slept all night, such a shock that I couldn't believe it could be repeated.... but going strong for a week now.
Blue light toxicity is so sneaky! creeps up on our health yet below our consciousness
There was lots of chat here in the earlier days(2-3yrs) about folk having their prescription glasses made into blue blockers. I have some new sort of blue blocker that allows the lens to remain clear which helps to avoid the whole "why I'm wearing strange coloured lens discussion".

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-03-11T06:06:59+0200

Unfortunately my wife doesn't get home until 10 PM. Then she eats "dinner" and watches "Vampire Diaries". I hate the LCD T.V.!
She thought it would be a good idea to mount it above the fire place. I really didn't have any objections until it was already done. When I lie in bed, the light from the TV flickers down the hall and through the cracks in the door! If I get up to go to the washroom at all I get the TV flickering in my face!
I now wear my blue blockers and I put up a curtain as well as some weather stripping around the door gaps. It's better but it's far from ideal.
I'm doing the BAB in the AM. It's kind of nice that I don't have to bring so much food to work now!
I have a feeling that getting these heavy metals out of my system is going to really help out. I'm just not sure how long it will take because it seems like I am getting results already but it's hard to say. That 'placebo' effect is pretty powerful, especially when you start a new protocol and you start getting hopeful.

### sebbie — 2015-03-11T20:04:36+0200

I was a big drinker many years ago but I quit because of the terrible migraines I developed over time. I drank because it made me feel good. The rest of the time I felt depressed. Now I take an SSRI and a stimulant for the depression. The stimulant has two sides: it can make me feel like I can accomplish anything at times but it can give me terrible migraines. It motivates me to learn all the Jack Kruse stuff and gives me that "excitement" when I start piecing things together but has so many negatives. I feel trapped because I know that if stop the stimulant my life will become more organized and flow better but I will lose interest in the Jack Kruse stuff. I also drink coffee to get myself going. I'll even smoke pot occasionally on the weekends (with a vapourizor - not as bad for the lungs) just to relieve the boredom. ### Cpt.Tired — 2015-05-14T07:31:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-164195>

I just changed my "name' to "Cpt. Tired" because I stupidly used my real name on the forum for the whole world to see (duh! needing more voltage here!).
I haven't really been exercising much at all lately because:

a) Since I dramatically reduced my carbs and replaced them with fat (mostly coconut oil) I have actually lost about 15 pounds with very little exercise! When I was eating a normal amount of carbs I had to exercise at least 5 days a week for about 45 minutes at moderate intensity just to maintain weight.
b) I'm concerned about overstressing my system since I am detoxing lead, aluminum and cadmium.
c) I've been doing CT in the ocean 5 days a week for 1/2 hr. I've also been swimming down about 8 ft deep with goggles checking out sea life like big crabs that I would rather not step on! Jack stated in one webinar that exposing oneself to more water pressure has health benefits but I don't think he went into much detail.

Anyway, I haven't really noticed much as far as symptom relief from CT (or the 20G Magnetico) as of yet but I'm not discouraged. I know that it takes time and they are all pieces to the puzzle of health.
Actually, there is one benefit I have noticed from CT: I can stand in the cold shower with the heat right off now for a significant amount of time, almost like I have a thin wet suit on or something. And dunking in the ocean is like nothing now.
My heat tolerance also feels like it has increased! We have had a few warm days lately here in Vancouver and I haven't been bothered by it much. That will be nice in the heat of the summer when I have to wear a stupid hard hat with zero ventilation in it while on stimulant medication!

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-05-22T07:25:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-165006>

I went camping for one night on my own to a nice "secret" spot just east of Manning Park here in British Columbia. The last few times I've been up there, there has been nobody in the entire area, and I mean NOBODY.... I could hear the odd logging truck echoing a few valleys over but that was it. Just the sound of trickling brooks, songbirds and a breeze.
It's usually pretty cold up there this time of year but I felt fine being cold adapted so I walked around with no shoes or shirt the whole time to take advantage of earthing and the photoelectric effect.
I found a creek for CT. Fresh snow melt. Holy crap was that cold! I could only handle 10 second dips.
Since there was nobody around and Jack advocates it, I did CT in the nude and laid in the sun on the grass nude. I felt kind of weird at first, like I'm going to 'go to hell' for this or something (LOL!),but I knew that this is how we were meant to live.
After that, it was absolutely refreshing! I would dunk myself for as long as I could stand, hobble over the bank and walk in circles on the grass going 'holy f&@k is that EVER cold!' while flexing my whole body. I almost felt like I was going to pass out a couple of times but once I warmed up a bit in the sun it was so awesome!!

I phoned in sick today because I just felt so tired when I got up, but now I feel guilty because I'm not 'sick'. I'm just feeling lazy...

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-06-07T06:03:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-166906>

I took Thursday off because I had a small headache when I woke but small will become large with activity. I got some guts and made another raw unpeeled prawn smoothie. This time I actually got an IgE reaction from it (itchy tongue, eyes, some small hives). I took some NAC, chlorella and some bentonite clay and it went away.
I guess I'll lay off the raw ones for a while. Instead I'll leave them unpeeled and cook them first. I want to take advantage of all that "collagen wire" in the prawn's exoskeleton.
Anyway my mood seems to be improving. (knock on wood!!)

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-06-07T23:36:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-166980>

> [Peertje80 said:](/goto/post?id=166924)
>
> Never discard heads and peels. You can also make a bad ass sauce from prawn heads and peels.

Your right! That is where the nutrition is.
I have a theory: In an effort to make food not spoil (we as a society) have removed all the nutrition out of food. We think of it is weird and gross to eat anything other than the meat of animals. We have probably developed this sense because these parts spoil first. We are not accustomed to eating those parts anymore so eating them is just gross to us now.
Food spoils because it has nutrition that life wants.
The faster it spoils, the more likely that it is good for us. (seafood really spoils fast!)

Yeah.. I feel much better now.

### JanSz — 2015-06-17T16:12:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-168586>

Test shows flat saliva cortisol 4x
Frequent migrane headaches.
\-------------------------------------------------------
Low Total Cortisol(serum)
likely low many other Steroid hormones
most likely
progesterone(serum) ---> migranes
and
DHEAs(serum)
\-------------------------------------------------------
Desirable goals:
cortisol,serum (7:30AM)---- 20ug/dL
progesterone, serum 1.4 ng/mL
DHEAs, serum 500 μg/dL

Do not exercise until you cortisol is close to your goal.
\-------------------------------------------------------
How to accomplish above goals:
eat:
pregnenolone Micronized Lipid Matrix from Nurticology (preg-MLM)

and
DHEA pills
\-----------
Start with
one 150mg tab of preg-MLM at wakeup time
and
one 50mg pill of DHEA

check blood monthly, titrate dose until goal is accomplished
\-----------------------------------------------------------

Micronutrient support is required.
get this test every 6 months, use results to compile and latter revise list of supplements.

buy directly------->>>
[http://spectracell.mybigcommerce.com/](http://spectracell.mybigcommerce.com/)

I too work in an office with no windows. I plan on wearing my BluTech lenses when at my computer and take them off when I leave my desk and go out to the floor or to meetings (natural light in those rooms).

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-06-20T21:15:07+0300

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-169071>

My best guess would be that if you are exposed to natural sunlight they shouldn't be worn but if you are in an office, which most of the time will have blue light, then they should be worn since the amount of blue light that you get exposed to is much more than you would get naturally during the same time period.
I haven't learned enough about why the blue light is so bad for us yet but this is just my best guess on what I currently know.

### JanSz — 2015-06-20T22:06:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-169074>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=169059)
>
> I plan on wearing my BluTech lenses when at my computer and take them off when I leave my desk and go out to the floor or to meetings (natural light in those rooms).

Same question,
why use blueblockers when sitting in windowless room during day time.
I would say that, during day time, light from computer screen or fluorescent tubes is better than total darkness.
.
.
We are working from idea that blue (and green) light tells us that there is a day time (time for cortisol),
and
absence of blue (and green) light tells us that there is night (time to make melatonin).

> First night with blue blockers..definitely slept better BUT I also cut out my evening coffee and stimulant last night too. ### Clayton — 2015-06-21T03:37:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-169106>

> [Cpt.Tired said:](/goto/post?id=169068)
>
> No, these ones are just for the evening. At work we wear safety glasses. Eventually I will try to get some for work too but they would have to be safety glasses that filter the blue light and preferably cheap since they get marked up a lot . We do have a lot of fluorescent lights in the shop. I've been making sure that we keep the bay door open and my co-workers have been on board with it too, but we still get blue light exposure.

These are all you need. You can pick them up at any Acklands-Grainger in the lower mainland [http://www.amazon.com/Uvex-S1933X-E...8&qid=1434847011&sr=8-1&keywords=uvex+skypers](http://www.amazon.com/Uvex-S1933X-Eyewear-SCT-Orange-Anti-Fog/dp/B000USRG90/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1434847011&sr=8-1&keywords=uvex+skypers)

Clay, do you know if they pass the spectrum test or not? If they do then that is a screaming deal! I'll get 'em for work.

I remember you posted these a while ago but me and my low redox decided to be impatient so I went to Marks Work Warehouse and bought some others that didn't pass.

Anyway, Thanks again

[http://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/legionnaires-disease/basics/prevention/con-20028867](http://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/legionnaires-disease/basics/prevention/con-20028867)

### b612 — 2015-06-21T17:56:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-169172>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=169165)
>
> [http://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/legionnaires-disease/basics/prevention/con-20028867](http://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/legionnaires-disease/basics/prevention/con-20028867)
>
> [Legionnaires' disease](http://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/legionnaires-disease/basics/definition/con-20028867)
>
> //

_L. pneumophila_ thrives in aquatic systems where it is established within [amoebae](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amoeboid) in a [symbiotic relationship](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Symbiosis). In the built environment, [central air conditioning](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Central_air_conditioning) systems in office buildings, hotels, and hospitals are sources of contaminated water.[\[8\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Legionnaires'_disease#cite_note-8) Other places it can dwell include [cooling towers](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cooling_towers) used in industrial cooling systems, [evaporative coolers](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Evaporative_cooler),[nebulizers](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nebulizer), [humidifiers](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Humidifiers), [whirlpool spas](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jacuzzi), [water heating](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Water_heating) systems, showers, windshield washers, fountains, room-air humidifiers, ice-making machines, and [misting](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Evaporative_cooler) systems typically found in grocery-store produce sections.[\[9\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Legionnaires'_disease#cite_note-pmid1552203-9)[\[10\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Legionnaires'_disease#cite_note-Physorg.com-10) (Wiki)

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-06-23T03:12:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-169282>

I'm trying to reduce my Vyvanse dosage (stimulant) since it causes me so many problems.
I was taking 125mg spread over the day plus drinking small amounts of coffee over the day too. I am especially going to be diligent about that 6PM dose of 25mg which my doctor didn't like me doing anyway. But if I didn't take it my night would be a write off.
But of course it affected my sleep significantly.
The evening coffee hasn't been helping either.
Anyway..I'm now taking 75mg..and no more evening dose or coffee(!!!!!) I must stick to this one. So far my sleep has been better the last two nights.
I am writing this to make me accountable to everyone because this is my biggest issue..stimulants! If I try to quit on my own it won't work. But having some support should work.(I hope..)
When I can get off of them and not feel like I need them that will be a good day.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-06-23T03:17:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-169283>

I would have rather supported the stimulant before titrating my dosage down, which I still have done with everything that I have learned here, but I think the stimulant is taking me down faster than I am recovering, so I have to cut the dosage and live with the crash for now.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-06-30T05:42:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-170015>

so last night I got a total stab in the heart...my wife suggests that we try an open relationship because I have a terrible sex drive and she is hitting the 'cougar' years.
She doesn't want to leave me but she needs sex.
I feel like I want to die. I didn't see that coming at all.

### b612 — 2015-06-30T10:47:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-170046>

Hey Mike. I have never believed in monogamy. It doesn't mean I cheat on my boyfriend but if I see that guy... I can't help it...
I believe people are naturally polygamous. Monogamy is just stereotype, another civilisation "habit" that we have and think it's completely normal like brushing our teeth before bed and watching tv.
I think your wife is doing the right thing being sincere with you.
Please don't judge her. Let her do what she wants and she will always come back to you. Give her freedom. Trust me

### Lahelada — 2015-06-30T14:17:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-170056>

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-07-01T00:52:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-170127>

> [Cpt.Tired said:](/goto/post?id=170015)
>
> so last night I got a total stab in the heart...my wife suggests that we try an open relationship because I have a terrible sex drive and she is hitting the 'cougar' years.
> She doesn't want to leave me but she needs sex.
> I feel like I want to die. I didn't see that coming at all.

It's interesting...at first I was totally crushed about it. But when I looked online and read up about it and saw how it actually has saved and made people's relationships even closer and stronger, I'm starting to open up to the idea.
I really appreciate everybody's comments on this because that means my view is going in the right direction, It just takes a little bit getting used to.
Thomas, I agree totally about 'society's viewpoint' and I can see that what I'm struggling with is actually society's rules that have been programmed into me. My wife has been reassuring that she still absolutely loves me, but feels sexually 'frustrated'. And actually I do too. We've been together for 15 years. Being a guy, I've always had a problem with the idea of only sleeping with one woman for the rest of my life. But you make that sacrifice because your love for that person is far more powerful than the urge for sex.
I'm actually starting to notice that being 'open' actually makes my wife more sexually attractive. I'm also starting to feel a lot of tension releasing. As long as there is total communication, respect for each others feelings, having some ground rules and staying well away from emotional attachment, it could work.
Total monogamy is actually quite rare in the animal kingdom so maybe this IS part of the road to optimal.
Thanks guys..you're awesome! I feel much better today. It's weird that I can actually feel my brain starting to open up new pathways that were blocked before because of society's 'rules'. Still, it's going to take some getting used to.
It's our own pride that hurts us.

### Deverie_Night Owl — 2015-07-01T05:47:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-170177>

> [Cpt.Tired said:](/goto/post?id=170127)
>
> It's interesting...at first I was totally crushed about it. But when I looked online and read up about it and saw how it actually has saved and made people's relationships even closer and stronger, I'm starting to open up to the idea.
> I really appreciate everybody's comments on this because that means my view is going in the right direction, It just takes a little bit getting used to.
> Thomas, I agree totally about 'society's viewpoint' and I can see that what I'm struggling with is actually society's rules that have been programmed into me. My wife has been reassuring that she still absolutely loves me, but feels sexually 'frustrated'. And actually I do too. We've been together for 15 years. Being a guy, I've always had a problem with the idea of only sleeping with one woman for the rest of my life. But you make that sacrifice because your love for that person is far more powerful than the urge for sex.
> I'm actually starting to notice that being 'open' actually makes my wife more sexually attractive. I'm also starting to feel a lot of tension releasing. As long as there is total communication, respect for each others feelings, having some ground rules and staying well away from emotional attachment, it could work.
> Total monogamy is actually quite rare in the animal kingdom so maybe this IS part of the road to optimal.
> Thanks guys..you're awesome! I feel much better today. It's weird that I can actually feel my brain starting to open up new pathways that were blocked before because of society's 'rules'. Still, it's going to take some getting used to.
> It's our own pride that hurts us.

I can imagine it was quite a shock to you when she told you her desires! Good for you to open up to the idea, it says a lot about you and how much you care for her and understand your own limitations and capabilities. I know several people who have managed open relationships. It really takes a lot of good communication and can definitely enhance your connection. I have a male friend whose wife has sexual trauma and is really not interested in sex at all; they have sex only a few times a year. He has brought up the subject of letting him go elsewhere for sex but his wife wants to be the one to fulfill that with him. Unfortunately, it's not working, and now he is thinking of cheating. I think the deception is where people get in trouble in the end, so being open about it I'm sure would help. And you never know where it will lead your sex life...maybe to fun places!!

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-07-01T06:42:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-170184>

> I believe people are naturally polygamous. Monogamy is just stereotype, another civilisation "habit" that we have and think it's completely normal like brushing our teeth before bed and watching tv.
> I think your wife is doing the right thing being sincere with you.
> Please don't judge her. Let her do what she wants and she will always come back to you. Give her freedom. Trust me

> [Deverie\_Night Owl said:](/goto/post?id=170177)
>
> I can imagine it was quite a shock to you when she told you her desires! Good for you to open up to the idea, it says a lot about you and how much you care for her and understand your own limitations and capabilities. I know several people who have managed open relationships. It really takes a lot of good communication and can definitely enhance your connection. I have a male friend whose wife has sexual trauma and is really not interested in sex at all; they have sex only a few times a year. He has brought up the subject of letting him go elsewhere for sex but his wife wants to be the one to fulfill that with him. Unfortunately, it's not working, and now he is thinking of cheating. I think the deception is where people get in trouble in the end, so being open about it I'm sure would help. And you never know where it will lead your sex life...maybe to fun places!!

Yes I believe the communication is key and respecting each others feelings. it might not be forever anyway. I also have a feeling that the stress that she is under right now is playing a role. She works her regular job during the day, runs a dance studio business at night (which hasn't made any money yet). She tends to bite off more than she can chew so to speak. She comes home and I'm just going to bed. She has MS and if you know anything about MS their symptoms are worse in the heat. It's been quite hot the last few weeks here and I see that it is affecting her. I think her serotonin is low right now, I can see by her mood. Serotonin has a lot of control over sexual impulses which dopamine plays a major role. Low serotonin + high dopamine = uncontrollable horniness.
(just my speculation on my personal experience with various antidepressants)

But you make that sacrifice because your love for that person is far more powerful than the urge for sex.
> I'm actually starting to notice that being 'open' actually makes my wife more sexually attractive. I'm also starting to feel a lot of tension releasing. As long as there is total communication, respect for each others feelings, having some ground rules and staying well away from emotional attachment, it could work.
> Total monogamy is actually quite rare in the animal kingdom so maybe this IS part of the road to optimal.
> Thanks guys..you're awesome! I feel much better today. It's weird that I can actually feel my brain starting to open up new pathways that were blocked before because of society's 'rules'. Still, it's going to take some getting used to.
> It's our own pride that hurts us.

Go with your gut. Deep down you will know if it will work for you or not.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-07-02T08:56:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-170326>

> [Deverie\_Night Owl said:](/goto/post?id=170177)
>
> I can imagine it was quite a shock to you when she told you her desires! Good for you to open up to the idea, it says a lot about you and how much you care for her and understand your own limitations and capabilities. I know several people who have managed open relationships. It really takes a lot of good communication and can definitely enhance your connection. I have a male friend whose wife has sexual trauma and is really not interested in sex at all; they have sex only a few times a year. He has brought up the subject of letting him go elsewhere for sex but his wife wants to be the one to fulfill that with him. Unfortunately, it's not working, and now he is thinking of cheating. I think the deception is where people get in trouble in the end, so being open about it I'm sure would help. And you never know where it will lead your sex life...maybe to fun places!!

I showed her how tanked my DHEA level is, so she understands, and I can understand her being frustrated.

### nicld — 2015-07-02T16:06:53+0300

This may sound bad but I had to force myself to engage with him (not for lack of attraction to him just no drive) and his mood improved. Now that I have made great strides in my health he sometimes "complains" again!!!!

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-07-03T04:20:38+0300

Now that I have made great strides in my health he sometimes "complains" again!!!!

Thanks for sharing your personal experience. One problem that I have is the 'novelty effect',
or what I think is technically called the 'Coolidge effect'. It seems for me whenever I would first meet someone I would be completely horny for them. But after a while the novelty wears off and they become less sexually attractive. And that is with everybody.
She could be the sexiest woman ever..I STILL will lose interest..UNTIL it looks like I might lose them. Then I'm horny again.
I hate it! It's just not practical!(LOL)
I will still love them to death but I just don't get aroused as easily.
I'm just speculating this..but I'm guessing that the healthier you are and the better your hormone panel is the less of an issue this becomes. It's still there but not near as intense (?)

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-07-06T00:59:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-171024>

Update: I've drastically reduced my stimulant dosage (10 - 20mg of Dexedrine in the AM) which is WAY less than what I have been on in the past (250mg Vyvanse and 80 mg of Adderall). I'm completely avoiding caffeine in the evening (destroys melatonin production)
My sleep has been better but last night I had a weird experience....
...I took a bunch of Chlorella before bed for Magnetico detox, more than what I normally take. I also had a couple of tokes from the vapourizer with my wife during a movie. I didn't really get much of a buzz. I woke up at around 2 AM and had a very long appointment sitting on the toilet (on and off for about 2 1/2 hrs). I had this ringing in my ears and was really dizzy like almost blacking out. But there was something different that I have only experienced a couple of times in my life..
....as I felt like I was blacking out and the ringing in my ears was getting louder, I felt this terrible sensation that is very hard to describe. It's like a feeling of being sick (like the flu or something) that comes on like a wave and it is so intense that it is painful or more like a real suffering feeling, And then it fades slowly away.
I have a feeling that it is hypoxia ..I've gotten a similar experience when I'm running at my maximum (HIIT) and my body is screaming for more oxygen than I can provide it.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-07-13T06:59:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-171807>

I had an interesting weekend. I was so tired, as usual and constantly trying to learn more and feeling overwhelmed. I started learning a lot about the hormone pathways and trying to figure out what would be my best approach to tackle this stuff when I remembered from my weight lifting days that high rep squats are a great way to boost hormone production.
I haven't been doing any exercise at all because of my completely flat lined ASI test from last fall but I thought I'd give it a try but just not too much. I did 3 x 20 reps with about 100 lbs with quite short rest intervals as well as some quick overhead presses.

What a difference! I felt better almost immediately! My energy really picked up. enough that I got a bunch of shit done around the house yesterday AND today.
I'm going to keep doing that for sure but I just have to be careful not to overdo it.
Some people say to not exercise with adrenal fatigue and others say that you need to so you can heal properly, just not too much.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-07-18T21:18:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-172301>

> [Cpt.Tired said:](/goto/post?id=171807)
>
> I had an interesting weekend. I was so tired, as usual and constantly trying to learn more and feeling overwhelmed. I started learning a lot about the hormone pathways and trying to figure out what would be my best approach to tackle this stuff when I remembered from my weight lifting days that high rep squats are a great way to boost hormone production.
> I haven't been doing any exercise at all because of my completely flat lined ASI test from last fall but I thought I'd give it a try but just not too much. I did 3 x 20 reps with about 100 lbs with quite short rest intervals as well as some quick overhead presses.
>
> What a difference! I felt better almost immediately! My energy really picked up. enough that I got a bunch of shit done around the house yesterday AND today.
> I'm going to keep doing that for sure but I just have to be careful not to overdo it.
> Some people say to not exercise with adrenal fatigue and others say that you need to so you can heal properly, just not too much.

Well... the exercise thing was short lived. On Monday I was just exhausted again. I tried to see what else I did that could have caused an increase in energy. I took a bunch of vitamin C and ashwaganda as well during this period. I boosted my Vitamin C up and I got energy again! Apparently the adrenals use up TONS of vitamin C to make all your hormones.
Dopamine also requires vitamin C to be synthesized and I definitely need that. I have also heard that your body will use a lot of vitamin C to deal with all kinds of infections, molds, and heavy metals. Dave Asprey has talked about it because he has had issues with this.

I also have noticed that my gums tend to bleed when I floss. This can be a sign of vitamin C deficiency. Sore joints and fatigue are all signs of a deficiency state which I get both of these. I also wonder if migraines could be a sign of weak arteries (vitamin c deficiency).

Anyway, its been about 3 days since I've drastically increased my vitamin C intake over the course of the day and my energy levels have improved quite a bit. The mornings are still hard but midday and afternoons appear to be better.

If this pans out I might be a modern day victim of scurvy!

Well.. I got the Magnetico so that increases detox. I'm taking lots of vitamin C as well (good for detox). My calcium and zinc are low so I'm taking those to replace the lead and aluminum. I should also get some more strontium because I am low in that.
My naturopath says rather than take the metals out directly it's better to add the minerals that you are low in because the metals have taken their place. Calcium and zinc are the big ones it looks like. I'm also taking Chlorella before bed so it is in my blood stream while I sleep on the Magnetico. The calcium and zinc bump out the toxic metals because the metals basically took their seat when calcium and zinc weren't around. Then the Chlorella binds to those metals when they are floating in the bloodstream. The chlorella is then excreted with the metals.
Its a slow process but it is working. This test is better than my last one. The lead is the main one to get rid of. It's very neurotoxic. It is coming down but slowly.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-07-30T22:35:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-173559>

Today's update:

Woke up with a mild headache. I phoned in because I know it will just get worse as the day progresses. My philosophy is - sure, I could go in and struggle to get through the day which would induce significant stress working in the heat with a hardhat on, make some money, but how many days am I losing on the back end of my life by doing that? I'm not 'broke' and the work is not really 'rush' so I might as well avoid the stress.

It can be easy for me to get down because I am missing so much work. And it's not really because of the money. It's more because of my own pride and just feeling 'unreliable'

But I am getting some success towards health. Increasing my vitamin c intake has helped and getting more light in the AM has really helped too. My hair mineral analysis trend is also improving.
I was originally getting up in the AM, going outside on the grass and looking up towards the sun but I found that I wasn't really feeling any benefit. I would also try to get the occasional quick sun exposure on my eyes when I was at work (I'd go outside for a minute and look up at the sun) without really feeling much either.
It seems to me that I feel the real benefit when the exposure to light in the AM is LONGER, like 15 min. - a half hour.
Even just going on the computer for 20 minutes in the AM with all the lights on seems to be working . I know it's not the ideal solution and far from a permanent one but I do feel better and fall asleep in the evening sooner as well.
I'm not feeling that overwhelming fatigue at work in the AM anymore. I just hope this benefit continues...... Getting the headaches under control is the real issue. I wonder how much of my headaches are due to my left eye. My left eye got badly damaged when I was a kid because I stupidly smashed a pop bottle. My vision is very poor and the connection to the brain has decreased a lot over time. I can tell because when I cover my good eye the darkness signal is stronger than the regular image in my bad eye. Maybe I need to strengthen that signal so my SCN gets better signalling.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-08-11T04:03:07+0300

Update: I've been experimenting with the Marty Hinz amino acid protocol lately to try and help me get off of these medications that are just depleting my neurotransmitters.

Apparently you can take a LOT of amino acid precursors and not have any of the problems with "serotonin syndrome". But you have to take both precursors together and not in isolation.
The ratio is 100 mg of 5-HTP to 1000 mg of L-tyrosine.
L- cysteine should also be taken while taking these aminos.

I think I'm feeling something from it but it's a little early to tell. I'm taking approx 900 mg/5-HTP to 9000 mg / L-tyrosine spread evenly over the day.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-10-11T22:20:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-179128>

Thanks guys for the support! Just in time because my wife's entire family thinks that I'm going nuts and that I need help because all I have been thinking about lately is about reducing EMF in our house, especially in the bedroom where we sleep because I know how critical it is for recovery and brain health. My wife tells her family that this medication that I am taking makes me crazy so they believe her. I agree with that to a point. It does do a lot of things that I don't like and I do want to get off of it. But the one thing that it DOES do is encourage me to LEARN about what is healthy and to implement the things that I need to do and prioritize them.
We all know how important sleep is. It's primordial. Jack has been driving that home to us from the beginning.
I've tried SO MANY TIMES to explain to her that I would rather improve my brain health FIRST before I try to titrate down off of this medication, since it really helps for depressive symptoms.
Most importantly creating a "sleep sanctuary". I want to eliminate ALL EMF coming into the bedroom completely. That way I have eliminated that variable. When/if symptoms continue, I know that at LEAST I have that part covered.
Right now there is one wifi signal, the cell phone signal (two bars) and the body voltage readings from the house wiring (approx 0.85 volts AC). I want to get rid of ALL of it and I know I can do it since I am an electrician.

(For Jude) Thanks for asking. I have been (other than this last 'shit show') doing better health wise for sure the last month or two. What has made the biggest difference is:

\-high dose vit C - (up to 10 grams a day..very safe and helps with adrenal fatigue)

\-high dose vit B5 -(2 grams a day..also for adrenal fatigue and acetylcholine production. This stuff has really seemed to help with me with my short term memory and anxiety in social situations. Reduces "deer in headlights" syndrome.\[When stress shuts down your ability to think\]

\-Zinc picolinate - I'm quite low in zinc on my hair analysis and on the zinc "tally test".
I took approx 250 mg of zinc a while back and felt real crappy for a day or two. This suggested to me that I was detoxing, according to
this video:

I then took a shitload of chlorella, cilantro, garlic and ginger. I started feeling WAAY better.

\- Tin foil hat - (LOL!) I know. Hilarious eh? I have noticed for a very long time that the computer screen really aggravates my headaches. I thought that maybe it was because of my eye. (My left eye is damaged from a childhood accident) or my neck position. I propped the screen up higher but it didn't help. So just for fun when no one was looking I put some tin foil in a baseball hat. WOW! IT ACTUALLY WORKS!
It's not crazy after all! I no longer feel 'headachy' when I am on the computer.
Regardless, my wife still thinks I'm crazy.

\-UV-A light (black light). Recommended by a community for setting the SCN. I just started this so I have no feedback yet. Even if I don't notice anything from it I know that the UV-A is important for me since I live in Canada.

I have been able to tolerate the stimulant medication much better lately. Less shitty side effects.

Jack's is so right - people will defend their beliefs before they believe the truth, even if it is completely wrong. It's all in the programming.

(Endless) Even if DW isn't involved in the work she still will freak out. She is a bit of an 'odd' person and she knows that. A little bit of OCD, a little bit of autism. Nothing diagnosed but just some characteristics that I have noticed. There is some distant inbreeding in her family too so she is not your typical 'normal' person. I can see that now in her reasoning. She is not thinking of her future with MS. Instead she is depending on God to keep her MS free.
OK, that's fine....but what about me then? I believe in the science and it's not going to hurt her if she has a little extra protection.

Bye for now

Mike - please take care of yourself first and foremost. Since your wife is not onboard - she will have to live with the consequences.

Can you sleep in another room? outside in a tent? dining room? think outside the box .... your health depends on it. What room, place, space has the least amount of wifi and nnEMF?

Why do you care what others think? it is your health and life and your journey on the line here

There is all kinds of new info coming out daily that support what Dr. K. has been telling us for years ....and we are all extremely lucky to be on the cutting edge.

When we know better - we do better .... we are the lucky ones!

Have you heard Dr. K's saying ....... " You are welcome to come into my Life at any time - you are also welcome to leave at any time ..... just don't stand in the doorway - you are blocking traffic!

surround yourself with positive people ....negative people steal your electrons.

I know it isn't easy ...but this is your one and only journey - your one kick at the can so to speak........

My wish for you .... live, really live ....and be real.

take care you.....

The full on lack of logic- y'know? It's like I am seeing her brain de-evolve right in front of my eyes because she is just 'reacting' and not really 'thinking'. When the mind is dominated by the sympathetic nervous system it usually makes bad decisions. She sits in front of a computer for probably 11 hrs a day. If I feel a headache coming on after 1/2 hour in front of a computer what is it silently doing to her brain? I'm glad I at least get a warning!
I'm hoping that by mitigating as much EMF as possible she might start thinking better.

I went to my cousin's for thanksgiving dinner last night. I was hesitant to talk about EMF but when I brought it up they were FULLY ON BOARD about it! I was shocked!
Even my 76 year old uncle knew that it cannot be good for us at all. He is very interested in the Magnetico because he understands physics and is very open minded. Man, what a relief! I brought out my meters and measured the bedrooms. My cousin looks after homeless children. Some have brain problems. I could not believe that she knew what a mitochondria was. She also knew that the brain uses light to communicate. Wow!
Dr Kruse's stuff was just a flowing from me and I could not believe how fluidly all the concepts were coming out and they were just fascinated by all this amazing stuff I have been learning. I felt like a 'mini Dr Kruse' even though I know maybe 1/100th of what Jack knows, if that.

I think all that DHA is paying off. We are all going to be f$%%^&g brilliant!!

Update- I've been hesitant about the whole exercise thing because I have adrenal fatigue,
however, I needed to get out of my rut somehow. I started doing some running and biking with a couple cycles of HIIT, nothing really intense.

WOW! WHAT A DIFFERENCE! Everything has improved right across the board! My energy, motivation and sex drive have all increased substantially.

I have also been using a UV bulb while I'm on the computer but I stopped because I think it may have been giving me headaches that would come on at 4 in the AM. I will have to try and navigate that one since I know that UV light is important.

Anyway I hope it continues..My wife is pretty happy about it since I have basically been a sloth for the past year!

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-10-31T23:30:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-180856>

It's interesting when I look back into my past and see that my happiest periods were the times I spent a LOT of it outdoors..camping, fishing and hiking with no shirt on. (Mind you, I was drinking a lot too). But I was out in nature a LOT

The periods of time that triggered depression were spent indoors playing guitar, jamming with other musicians and smoking pot (not tons of pot, but enough).\\

The last wave, (which I've never really come out of without medication) the majority of my time was spent drinking, chasing girls, jamming, staying out late and taking an intense avionics course out at the airport (major EMF) plus the responsibilities of living on my own.
My dream of becoming a "rock star guitarist" was dwindling. When I saw how much work it was going to be, my interest declined. When so many people inflate your ego telling you that "Wow! You are a wicked guitarist! Your gonna make it!" and being so young and naive you believe it to be true...
Around the same time the rock music scene had completely changed (early nineties).
Almost overnight the guitarists that spent every waking hour practicing their chops and shredding impossible leads were looked down on. Playing depressing music like Nirvana and Alice in Chains was the big thing now. The Seattle (depressive) music scene had now taken root and my depression along with it. I found out later that I wasn't the only guitarist that was suffering from depression.

Exactly! It's funny...when I was unemployed after high school and just having fun with my friends, it was entirely outdoors. Even when I started working, I was doing landscaping..all outdoor work but low pay. It was low stress but I was much happier. Only when I started thinking about "getting a career" is when I started spending much more time indoors. This is considered the "thing to do" even though it has never felt right to me. I have never expected someone else to "support me" but working 8 hour days for me just feels like way too much and a real waste of life because when I do have time off I'm way too tired to enjoy it. I'm just recovering so I can do it again tomorrow.

Oh I know what you're saying and I really don't need a lot of money, never really have. I can be quite creative in making a dollar go a looong way in the past. Maybe it's more about respect. Even if you can get by well with very little money people tend to not respect you.

So I think it comes down to whatever is sucking all the energy out of me. If I can fix whatever that is, I'll be fine.
Whatever it is..it runs in the family.. my dad, sister and my cousins have similar issues with low motivation, depression, migraines and other nervous system disorders. ### Cpt.Tired — 2015-11-07T03:11:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-181308>

I've been off of my Cipralex (escitalopram) for a week now with no ill effects. I've tried coming off of it before and felt fine for a little while then I start feeling shitty again..
I have a feeling this time will be different because I just feel 'different'- I don't know how to explain it but I have been detoxing metals for quite a while now with chlorella and the 20 gauss Magnetico (since about January). Still very deficient in zinc on the 'zinc tally test'. Now I'm taking 150 - 200mg of zinc citrate plus a couple lozenges a day. I haven't been too worried about copper deficiency because I work with copper all day (absorbing through my hands maybe?).

I bought a couple of large 48" 'black light' fluorescent bulbs. I figured that if I mix them in with the usual fluorescent bulbs in the kitchen that I would be getting UV more often but honestly, I didn't like it. It made me feel 'uptight'. I think I would rather have something that I can turn off. Maybe combined with infra red would be better.
I really don't like the green spike in the reptile bulb either.
Maybe this is a big sign of blue light toxicity (?). I'd really like to duplicate the AM or PM sunlight. It makes me just 'feel' good. It seems that the older incandescent bulbs do that best visually but then the UV and IR has to be added to that. I really don't like those blue-green wavelengths

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-11-12T02:58:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-181721>

Well I sure hope I got it this time. It has happened so many times to me where I feel a supplement or some other therapy is really starting to work for my fatigue or depression, only to have it fail as soon as I tell anybody about it.

Anyway...last Saturday I bought a UV blacklight bulb and mounted it in a clamp light about a foot behind my computer chair since I spend WAY too much time on the computer.

My energy level has picked up quite a bit. I'm not getting my usual energy dips that I get after each stimulant and coffee that I take just to make it through the day.

I'm being cautious with what I say because I don't want to jinx it.

If this continues I can titrate off the stims for good!

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-11-15T05:47:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-182001>

Today was a very productive day for a change.
Something (good) is definitely happening right now. I was dreaming a lot last night and they were relatively pleasant dreams.
(no, they weren't wet..)

Did I mention that I have been off of my Cipralex (escitalopram) for over two weeks?

Normally I would rarely ever dream and when I do they are usually very dark, creepy dreams.
I've been icing my wrist then using a UV flashlight and a red laser pointer on the radial artery. I also changed my f.lux setting to 'darkroom mode' since I feel that the computer screen light is still causing a circadian shift despite having f.lux on the reddest setting.
I've been trying the UV flashlight and red laser pointer on dad's wrist a few times too.

I KNOW that he is UV deficient!

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-11-15T07:53:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-182008>

Tonight I had a touch of a headache that, of course, would not go away.

I cut up a bunch of raw oysters, just in case my charge was low. Since I was not hungry, I knew that I was probably in 'sympathetic dominant' mode and unlikely to digest the oysters well.
I took two tablespoons of apple cider vinegar (to stimulate stomach acid) and to my surprise my headache went away!

I did a quick google to see if I could figure out why the apple cider vinegar worked and I got two leads: a pH imbalance and/or dehydration due to lack of potassium.

Sounds more than likely.

\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

My wife's co-worker had a baby boy last night. Something was not right so they put the baby under a 'black light'.

My wife told her "Mike would be proud"

Spencer's is here in Canada but there probably is one in the States. Or any 'sex, drugs and rock n roll' shop should have one. Yeah leave it on in whatever room you spend the most amount of time in. I would start with it at a distance say five feet to start and see how you feel after a few days. If you get too much too fast your cells will get enough energy to "finally throw out the garbage" (detox). But if the garbage gets dumped out faster than the liver can clear it, you get 'sick'. That's also why we are told to not get 'chilled' because we will catch a cold. The cells energy increases and starts detoxing.
What I am currently doing is I have a UV and IR next to me at my computer.
I would get an IR light from the pet store...look for a reptile light and check the frequency spectrum on the package. I can give you more info tomorrow if you want..I gotta go to bed

Mike I think of words as electrons, I find that if I try to engage the not interested, its like I "leak" word electrons. The trying to verbally justify my choices sucks electrons right out of me and into the other person ......but I open the door to make it happen ...... With me its also the written word .... I think one of the many reasons I lost the sight in my right eye (my weakest link) was because I spent hours on the computer leaking word electrons in emails to family about my Mum's dementia. ............. (a lot less words would have done, off being the second to certain family members....)

If someone is curious the energy flows both ways.... So now I do what I do (collect electrons) ...... wait to be asked, and try only to answer the specific questions without going off on a tangent because I'm so excited by the subject ..... then its a voluntary donation of electrons (words) from a good supply of electrons, offered to the curious, rather than a leak of electrons from a possibly poor supply to the uninterested ..... Well that's the plan anyway...

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-12-25T23:37:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-184955>

Merry Xmas to everyone!

Here is a bit of a recap of what I have been doing the last few months:

Nov 1st - stopped taking SSRI just to see what would happen because I really wasn't feeling that bad mood wise.

Nov 7th- started getting very good results from what seems to be the UV/IR lights but I also was getting a few more headaches.

Nov 22nd - started taking Lysine (4000 mg spread over the day) to combat headaches. I got such amazing results the last few weeks so my intuition (which seemed to be heightened incredibly) told me that I had the herpes virus and maybe lyme spirochetes in my left retina (the damaged one).
I did contract herpes from my wife many years ago (being young and dumb) and I did have mono as a teenager (also a herpes virus).

Dec 5th - Noticed a big improvement in headaches but started feeling lousy mentally. Tried cysteine but I found that also made me feel lousy

Dec 23 - started taking selenium (400ug-1000ug) since I saw it on Dr. Klinghardt's lyme protocol to suppress any possible viral activity.

Dec 25th- so far so good- no headaches yet!

I mean when I first tried it, it was like "Holy shit!! This is great!" That even went on for a while, for like a month and a half. I was feeling pretty good.

### Inger — 2015-12-27T13:35:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-184996>

Have you only been eating optimal, seasonal foods in addition to your other things like UV?
I would be very careful with cheating if you are not feeling well.... I know food is only a small part but it is very important too at least for me.
I need to do anything right that I can to feel my best.
I one day tried to eat coconut and papaya in the evening to see how it impacted me and it made me anxious and unwell, uh! Not good.
But I think it was a cool experiment to observe how my body react - even if it was pretty unpleasant..

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-12-27T21:46:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-185011>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=184996)
>
> Have you only been eating optimal, seasonal foods in addition to your other things like UV?
> I would be very careful with cheating if you are not feeling well.... I know food is only a small part but it is very important too at least for me.
> I need to do anything right that I can to feel my best.
> I one day tried to eat coconut and papaya in the evening to see how it impacted me and it made me anxious and unwell, uh! Not good.
> But I think it was a cool experiment to observe how my body react - even if it was pretty unpleasant..

I absolutely agree with you....food is still a huge part for me. The thing that I have noticed that probably has the biggest impact on how I feel is circadian cycles. What I eat and do when I first wake up is big.
Yesterday I felt depressive all day, especially at night. My dopamine was completely tanked! I can tell because I know what to do but I just don't have the drive or energy to do it.
I read somewhere about a experiment that was done with rats. They gave them something to block dopamine. The rats were so unmotivated that they wouldn't even eat the food that was placed right in front of them even though they were starving!
That's how I feel sometimes.
I find that I need a lot of protein to feel well. If my dopamine is 'ok' I will eat oysters, shrimp and scallops. But if it is low I tend to default to cottage cheese and milk. I know that its not optimal at all but I figure that it's better than nothing.
The worst days are the ones when I open the fridge in the AM and there is NO protein.
The grocery stores around here used to carry frozen oysters. I think I cleaned them out because they don't carry them anymore. They were very easy in the AM.
I also find that eating a generous amount of coconut oil spread throughout the day helps me too. The downside to that is my appetite is ruined when the time comes to eat real food.
Today so far (11:23AM) I feel much better than I did yesterday. I had my BAB and a vyvanse (50mg). The vyvanse is a big problem for me as I have stated before. Very hard to get off of but I have to keep trying. It works great for depression for me but it causes real problems.
My blood pressure lately has been terrible! 160/104! That is just stupid. But I'm not depressed. That's the tough part.
I stopped my SSRI a while ago. Maybe it was helping to keep my blood pressure lower.
Maybe I really need to start doing CT again. I've only really been doing the cold shower thing and only really for about a minute or two.

### Lahelada — 2015-12-27T22:01:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-185012>

Time to do something spontaneous and new. Dopamine is a fickle friend in this world. One element of the lights working was your belief,your buying your trying. You cannot alwy up the ante. Sometimes you have to hold still and wait it out. This is my misgiving about the bulbs to help. They are also a supplement ,can be more but on their own they are just that. CT ,ocean CT may be a good thing s this is one thin g that is harder to overdo.

Your dopamine profile looks like mine . My fish eating depends on dopamine being high. It rarely is at night .
Morning is where my creativity lies. Have you analysed your needs according to time of day? Maybe you need a meditation session somewhere, a sunbed?

### Cpt.Tired — 2016-01-17T02:33:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-186537>

Saw my DR. on Thursday past. I told him that I don't want to take the stimulant anymore and that I feel like I am addicted to it because I have tried so many times to get myself off of it only to be so fatigued and brain dead that I could hardly function at all so I would go back on just so I could function at least a little. He recommended another (newer) medication that I had never heard of that would replace the stimulant AND the SSRI (Lexapro). He said that it works on serotonin but does not suppress dopamine like an SSRI does. Well that was why I was taking the stimulant in the first place because the Lexapro was suppressing my dopamine really badly.
So I have been taking the vorioxetine only (no stimulant!) for about three days now and I can feel it (I think).
Unlike most people it seems that I can usually feel some effect from medications almost right away. So far things feel quite positive. I am feeling a little more drive to get myself organized and to start taking these protocols more seriously. The goal is to eventually get off of this med as well.
I'll keep you all posted...

### lilreddgirl — 2016-01-17T03:30:37+0200

### lilreddgirl — 2016-01-17T07:12:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-186559>

> Well, I hope we can both get our charge up good this year. Having the people here for support alone helps us to accomplish that!

 I charged up today!

I remember you writing somewhere that you were doing the Wim Hof breathing on the days you were feeling great... I just did it today and it helped my mood energy go Way better during the day... What if it was a big part of what was helping you? It is so fast to do it too!

This is one of the videos I followed along with. Deep breath in pushing out with the diaphragm, smaller breath out (just let the diaphragm drop)

I noticed after my breathing was way softer and relaxed, like the Buteyko method (another breathing method that says it will get more oxygen to your tissues) recommends...

I'm also going to try taping my mouth closed overnight tonight (Buteyko practice) [http://www.independent.co.uk/life-s...thing-technique-a-cure-for-asthma-411996.html](http://www.independent.co.uk/life-style/health-and-families/health-news/buteyko-breathing-technique-a-cure-for-asthma-411996.html)
and see how that affects my energy levels... I understand it should make a big difference... and I've noticed I'm waking up breathing through my mouth ever since I was on the sleep meds, which I don't remember doing before when I was sleeping more profoundly...

### Cpt.Tired — 2016-01-17T07:36:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-186561>

> [lilreddgirl said:](/goto/post?id=186559)
>
>  I charged up today!
>
> I remember you writing somewhere that you were doing the Wim Hof breathing on the days you were feeling great... I just did it today and it helped my mood energy go Way better during the day... What if it was a big part of what was helping you? It is so fast to do it too!
>
> This is one of the videos I followed along with. Deep breath in pushing out with the diaphragm, smaller breath out (just let the diaphragm drop)
>
>
>
> I noticed after my breathing was way softer and relaxed, like the Buteyko method (another breathing method that says it will get more oxygen to your tissues) recommends...
>
> I'm also going to try taping my mouth closed overnight tonight (Buteyko practice) and see how that affects my energy levels... I understand it should make a big difference... and I've noticed I'm waking up breathing through my mouth ever since I was on the sleep meds, which I don't remember doing before when I was sleeping more profoundly...

Awesome! I'm so glad that it helped you!

I actually used it last night when I was playing a gig at a pub with my band. I started getting really bored during our first set (normally a stimulant would help me with that).

At the end of the set I was thinking 'God..I'm so bored playing these same old classic rock songs. This just sucks but people are dancing and having a good time so I have to put on this brutally fake smile so people don't notice. It's so much work being fake like this!'

I went out to my car and meditated a bit while doing a couple of those exercises.

The second and third set went MUCH better. Now I'm more thinking "this is not too bad...I'm tired but I'm not entirely fake now. I am enjoying it a bit now. My smile is more genuine."

I think having that tool can really help us in 'on the spot' situations when you need that extra push. You don't have to do it intensely either. I will just breath out and try to hold it for a bit then breath in really deep but slowly and gradually try to take in more and more air. I imagine that the air is filling up my tissues and that is why I can breath in a bit more every ten seconds or so. I think we may be able to even get more oxygen in if we move our bodies a bit.
I'm going to check out that Buteyko practice that you mentioned. the more tools the better...

### Cpt.Tired — 2016-01-30T07:14:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-187658>

I have been completely off of stimulant medication now for a whole week and I don't even have the temptation to go back on because I really don't feel that bad at all. I've been doing a fair amount of meditation and deep breathing exercises like Wim Hof, getting a bit of exercise outside and trying to get as much sunlight as I can whenever I see the sun pop out of the clouds. I definitely notice an energy boost after sun gazing as well as the deep breathing exercises. Whenever I feel depressive symptoms coming on I start doing some deep breathing and holding my breath. It really seems to help, at least for a little while. Combined with sunlight, cold showers and deep breathing there is a significant effect. I was afraid that going off of the stimulant would make me lose interest in Jack's stuff since it really helps make things that are normally 'boring' more interesting but I am finding that I am still quite interested.
Still taking Vorioxetine but kind of on and off because I'm thinking that I might not even really need it now. I was taking 10mg a day but last Monday I felt absolutely terrible so I skipped a couple of doses and felt MUCH better.
